Latest sync (2026-03-26 public CUDA helper-surface coverage expansion):
`crates/ivm/src/{cuda.rs,bn254_vec.rs}`
now pin more of the exported CUDA runtime surface directly instead of relying
only on startup admission.

- the focused source-level `public_` CUDA lib sweep now covers the exported
  vector helpers (`vector_add_f32`, `vadd32_cuda`, `vadd64_cuda`, `vand_cuda`,
  `vxor_cuda`, `vor_cuda`), `sha256_compress_cuda`, `keccak_f1600_cuda`,
  Poseidon single/batch helpers, single-round AES helpers, single-round AES
  batch helpers, fused AES rounds, BN254 add/sub/mul, Ed25519 single/batch
  verification, and the SHA-256 leaves/pair-reduce helpers under one direct
  parity block;
- `sha256_leaves_cuda` now has a focused public CPU-parity regression that
  mirrors the existing Metal coverage and proves the exported Merkle-leaf entry
  point, not just the internal startup truth set;
- `sha256_compress_cuda`, `keccak_f1600_cuda`, the Poseidon public helpers, and
  the single-round `aesenc_cuda` / `aesdec_cuda` helpers now also have focused
  source-level parity regressions instead of depending only on startup
  admission or separate integration tests;
- `aesenc_batch_cuda` / `aesdec_batch_cuda` now have focused public parity
  regressions against the scalar AES reference path, matching the existing
  single-round Metal batch coverage instead of leaving those exported helpers
  covered only transitively through startup admission;
- the public BN254 and Ed25519 CUDA helpers now have the same direct source
  parity coverage instead of relying only on startup truth sets plus the older
  `cuda_extra` integration tests;
- the existing public `sha256_pairs_reduce_cuda` / fused AES rounds regressions
  still pass alongside the new tests under one focused `public_` lib sweep; and
- the stale `mul_neon debug:` instrumentation was removed from the BN254 NEON
  multiply helper, the macOS-only `min_metal` threshold binding now stays
  inside the macOS branch, and the dead non-macOS Metal SHA helper stubs were
  dropped, so the focused CUDA parity sweep no longer emits branch-local `ivm`
  warning noise.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo test -p ivm --features cuda --lib public_ -- --test-threads=1`

Open work for this IVM CUDA public-surface slice now remains:
- continue the deeper `ivm` accelerator review across the remaining
  CUDA/Metal/determinism boundaries; this pass closed the direct public
  helper parity gaps and cleaned the related `ivm` warning noise, but it did
  not attempt a broader workspace-wide accelerator sweep.

`crates/iroha_core/src/state.rs`,
`crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/rwa.rs`,

and

now close the missing RWA follow-up in the current tree:

  repeated issue/split/merge/force-transfer flows do not collide inside a
  single transaction;

Latest sync (2026-03-30 offline cash contract cleanup across Torii, maintained SDKs, and retail wallet adapters):
`crates/iroha_torii/src/lib.rs`,
`crates/iroha_torii/src/openapi.rs`,
`crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_cash_router_smoke.rs`,
`IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift`,
`kotlin/core-jvm/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/sdk/client/OfflineToriiClient.kt`,
`java/iroha_android/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/OfflineToriiClient.java`,
and
`javascript/iroha_js/src/toriiClient.js`
now align the maintained public offline surface to the first-release cash flow:
`GET /v1/offline/cash/readiness`,
`POST /v1/offline/cash/{setup,load,refresh,sync,redeem}`,
`GET /v1/offline/revocations`,
`POST /v1/offline/revocations`,
`GET /v1/offline/revocations/bundle`,
`GET /v1/offline/transfers`,
`GET /v1/offline/transfers/{bundle_id_hex}`,
`POST /v1/offline/transfers/query`,
and
`POST /v1/offline/policy`.

- implementation on this sync:
  - Torii no longer mounts the legacy public `/v1/offline/allowances*` routes, and the generated OpenAPI now publishes the mounted cash readiness + lifecycle paths while explicitly keeping the legacy allowance/certificate/settlement-era paths out of the checked-in app spec;
  - Swift now performs real JSON round trips for `setupOfflineCash`, `loadOfflineCash`, `refreshOfflineCash`, `syncOfflineCash`, and `redeemOfflineCash`;
  - Kotlin/JVM and Java Android now expose shared raw-JSON cash lifecycle helpers plus `GET /v1/offline/revocations/bundle`, with focused parity tests locking those canonical paths;
  - JavaScript now exposes `getOfflineCashReadiness()`, the five cash mutation helpers, and `getOfflineRevocationBundle()` with updated typings/tests/docs; and
  - the retail wallet adapters were reviewed and aligned with the actual deployment split already used in production apps: iOS now uses `ToriiService` on `coreApiBaseUrl` for cash calls, while Android routes cash writes and revocation-bundle fetches through the shared `OfflineToriiClient` on `coreApiBaseUrl` and keeps transfer/revocation reads on `irohaApiBaseUrl`.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 offline cash app-API canonical binding/proof hardening):
`crates/iroha_torii/src/lib.rs`,
`crates/iroha_torii/src/offline_reserve.rs`,
`IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/OfflineCashModels.swift`,
`IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift`,
and
`IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiOfflineCashEndpointsTests.swift`
now require canonical `device_binding` + `device_proof` on all offline cash app-API mutations and reject attempts to bind a funded/pending lineage to a different device.

- implementation on this sync:
  - app-facing offline cash request decoding now treats `android` and `ios` as explicit canonical platforms and translates both into the shared reserve-attestation flow;
  - canonical receipt translation now consumes `device_proof`, so multi-hop/offline ancestry stays on the same wire contract across Android and iOS;
  - reserve setup/load now reject `lineage_conflict` when an account already has a different active lineage with funded or pending offline state; and
  - the Swift SDK offline cash client now attaches deterministic `Idempotency-Key` headers to mutating offline cash requests, with focused regression coverage updated accordingly.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud private-model publish drafts now import pinned Hugging Face snapshots):
`crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs`,
`crates/iroha_cli/src/soracloud.rs`,
and
`docs/source/soracloud/cli_local_control_plane.md`
now let `iroha app soracloud model-publish-private --draft-file` normalize
either a local admitted directory or a pinned HF snapshot through the same
deterministic encrypted publish flow.

- implementation on this sync:
  - `PrivateModelPublishDraft` now carries `source: PrivateModelSourceV1`,
    with `LocalDir(path)` and `HuggingFaceSnapshot(repo, revision)` variants;
  - the CLI now normalizes pinned HF snapshots into a deterministic temp tree,
    then reuses the existing encrypted bundle/chunk/finalize/compile/allow
    flow instead of introducing a second publish protocol;
  - v1 validation now explicitly admits only the root-level HF safetensors
    layout, requires pinned 40-character commit SHAs for HF snapshots, and
    rejects GGUF, ONNX, other non-safetensors weights, and arbitrary nested
    custom layouts; and
  - focused regressions now cover the new source-type roundtrip, unpinned
    revision rejection, local unsupported-layout rejection, and successful
    pinned HF snapshot plan preparation.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud private-model publish drafts now prepare encrypted bundle plans locally):
`crates/iroha_cli/src/soracloud.rs`,
`crates/iroha_cli/Cargo.toml`,
and
`docs/source/soracloud/cli_local_control_plane.md`
now let `iroha app soracloud model-publish-private` prepare its own encrypted
bundle/chunk/finalize/compile/allow plan from a local admitted HF-style model
directory instead of requiring a prebuilt encrypted plan document.

- the new wrapper now accepts `--draft-file`, validates the local source
  directory, derives deterministic bundle metadata, encrypts the bundle
  against the active Torii upload recipient, shards it into fixed-size
  encrypted chunks, and then executes the existing
  upload/finalize/compile/allow flow;
- `--plan-file` still works for already prepared publish plans, and
  `--emit-plan-file` can now persist the prepared encrypted plan before
  submission; and
- focused CLI regressions now cover both the happy path and the missing-config
  rejection path for draft-driven plan preparation.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 `BlockCreated` now seeds the exact frontier slot and contiguous RBC rescue no longer re-enters generic missing-block fetch):
this cut transfers `committed + 1` ownership another step toward the intended single frontier state machine: accepted `BlockCreated` now marks the exact slot authoritative directly, and RBC-side “missing BlockCreated” rescue at the contiguous frontier now stays inside that slot instead of scheduling generic `FetchPendingBlock` recovery.

- implementation on this sync:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/proposal_handlers.rs` now seeds the exact `FrontierSlot` from accepted `BlockCreated` traffic instead of relying on later pending-block state to imply frontier identity,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` now diverts contiguous-frontier RBC recovery back into the exact slot so missing `BlockCreated` / post-delivery repair cannot take over the frontier with generic missing-block rescue, and
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` now lets the frontier helper retry `FetchBlockBody` whenever the slot is already authoritative, even if the newest caller only contributes passive topology evidence.

- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo check -p iroha_core --lib --message-format short`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core block_created_initializes_exact_frontier_slot --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core request_missing_block_for_pending_rbc_holds_initial_frontier_fetch_within_ingress_grace --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core handle_rbc_init_preserves_rotated_leader_preference_for_late_missing_block_recovery --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core frontier_body_repair_fetches_leader_before_voter_fallback --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core frontier_vote_placeholder_skips_generic_missing_block_request --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core defer_qc_if_block_missing_with_commit_quorum_hint_seeds_contiguous_frontier_owner_create_only --lib -- --nocapture`

- open work after this sync:
  - rerun the preserved-peer permissioned and NPoS stable soaks on this cut and require the frontier logs to show nonzero `FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse` plus zero `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT` on the contiguous frontier,
  - delete the remaining near-tip call sites that can still share fallback-anchor / block-sync repair with `committed + 1`, and
  - finish the protocol deletion so `BlockCreated` is the only frontier advertisement and RBC transport cannot keep ownership of old slots alive.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 full permissioned + NPoS preserved-peer stable soaks still miss on legacy frontier repair):
the first full preserved-peer rerun on the exact-body tranche shows that the live frontier path is still not using `FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse`; both one-hour 4-peer stable envelopes stayed safe, but both failed the duration-deadline p95 gate because legacy fallback-anchor recovery continued to dominate the frontier.

- preserved-peer soak outcome on this sync:
  - permissioned finished the full `3600s` envelope at strict/quorum height `1430` and failed with `quorum p95 block interval 5003ms exceeded threshold 1000ms`
  - NPoS finished the full `3600s` envelope at strict/quorum height `1387` and failed with the same `quorum p95 block interval 5003ms exceeded threshold 1000ms`
  - both runs kept all peers alive and aligned, so the regression is throughput/frontier churn rather than quorum loss, divergence, or a `no block height progress` stall

- preserved-peer log critique on this sync:
  - exact frontier repair never appeared on the wire in either run: `FetchBlockBody=0`, `BlockBodyResponse=0`
  - legacy frontier recovery remained hot:
    - permissioned: `fallback anchor=1260`, `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=90`
    - NPoS: `fallback anchor=1079`, `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=70`
  - the churn clustered on one peer per run (`just_quagga` in permissioned, `cuddly_grunt` in NPoS), which still points at shared frontier-owner / fallback-anchor behavior rather than a symmetric workload ceiling

- open work after this sync:
  - remove the remaining live call sites that let contiguous-frontier misses fall back into fallback-anchor sharing and RBC-era `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT` rescue,
  - finish the `BlockCreated`-only frontier protocol deletion so near-tip repair can be observed as nonzero `FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse` traffic in preserved-peer logs,
  - retire the remaining frontier lib tests that still encode the old shared-window / range-pull behavior, then rerun the full preserved-peer stable envelopes and require zero fallback-anchor / missing-`BlockCreated` churn on the frontier path before treating the redesign as validated.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 exact frontier body lane now routes `committed + 1` through `FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse`):
this cut installs the dedicated frontier slot and the exact body request/response pair, so the current-slot body miss can stop living inside the generic hash-based missing-block machine. It is still a tranche, not the final `BlockCreated`-only protocol deletion.

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/message.rs`, `status.rs`, and `mod.rs` add the exact frontier body wire pair and thread it through message classification / dedup bookkeeping,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` adds `FrontierSlot` / `next_slot_prefetch`, leader-first retry scheduling, and active-round tracking for the exact frontier lane,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/block_sync.rs` now answers exact body requests with `BlockBodyResponse` carrying `BlockCreated`, can hydrate the local slot from that response, and flushes any stashed exact-body requesters once the authoritative body arrives,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` and `qc.rs` now keep frontier vote observations passive and route highest-QC / unknown-body frontier repair onto the exact slot instead of re-entering `missing_block_requests`,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs` adds focused checks for exact body responses, passive frontier placeholders, and leader-first then voter-fallback retry ordering.

- focused validation passed:
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo check -p iroha_core --lib --message-format short`

- focused validation now reaches the new frontier cases:
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core frontier_body_repair_fetches_leader_before_voter_fallback --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core frontier_catchup_target_keeps_contiguous_frontier_with_pending_frontier_block --lib -- --nocapture`
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target cargo test -p iroha_core frontier --lib -- --nocapture`
    now runs to completion (`112` passed, `19` failed, `1` ignored) and the remaining failures are concentrated in legacy frontier-recovery/shared-window/range-pull tests rather than unrelated compile drift.

- open work after this sync:
  - finish removing standalone `Proposal` / `ProposalHint` and the remaining `RbcReady` / `RbcDeliver` wire paths so `BlockCreated` becomes the only hot-path frontier advertisement,
  - keep `BlockSyncUpdate` and range-pull logic on deep catch-up only; remove the remaining legacy frontier branches that can still reach those paths,
  - replace the remaining RBC-named frontier transport/state with explicit body transport naming and retire old-slot rescue/rebroadcast state,
  - retire or rewrite the remaining `19` frontier lib tests that still encode legacy frontier-recovery/shared-window/range-pull behavior removed by the exact-slot design, and
  - rerun the full frontier filter after that test cleanup, then rerun short localnet probes and the preserved-peer stable soak matrix on this exact-body tranche.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 exact frontier-fetch tranche removed RBC sidecars from `FetchPendingBlock`):
this cut finally stops `FetchPendingBlock` from reintroducing RBC transport into the frontier lane and removes the post-commit carry-over that kept delivered sessions hot, but it is still only a tranche of the full `BlockCreated`-only redesign.

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` adds `MissingBlockFetchMode::StrictSigners` and removes the delivered-session `RBC_REBROADCAST_COMMITTED_DEPTH` rebroadcast carry-over,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` now keeps pending / post-delivery missing-block recovery on exact signer targets, normalizing rotated-view proposer indices back into canonical roster slots before fetch planning,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` now uses the unresolved vote signer as the exact missing-block target instead of falling straight back to topology-wide repair, and
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/block_sync.rs` no longer emits `RbcInit` / `RbcChunk` sidecars on `FetchPendingBlock` responses and no longer serves RBC transport as the fallback when the block body is not yet authoritative.

- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib fetch_pending_block_ -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib plan_missing_block_fetch_ -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib request_missing_block_for_pending_rbc_ -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib handle_rbc_init_preserves_rotated_leader_preference_for_late_missing_block_recovery -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib delivered_rbc_session_ -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib handle_vote_defers_until_roster_available -- --nocapture`

- open work after this sync:
  - replace `FetchPendingBlock` with the requested exact frontier pair (`FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse`) so contiguous-frontier repair is explicit and no longer shares semantics with legacy missing-block fetch,
  - stop returning `BlockSyncUpdate` for contiguous frontier repair so `committed + 1` stays on the exact body lane and deep catch-up stays on the block-sync lane,
  - remove `RbcReady` / `RbcDeliver`, the stash-driven READY/DELIVER rescue paths, and the remaining worker-loop session scans that still keep RBC state alive after the slot is decided,
  - rerun short localnet probes and then the full permissioned / NPoS preserved-peer stable soaks to see whether the missing-`BlockCreated` churn and `~2.5s / 3.3s / 5s` cadence collapse after the exact-body message split is complete.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 TAIRA faucet adaptive PoW hardening):
the TAIRA faucet now requires a deterministic, chain-anchored, memory-hard
scrypt proof-of-work before Torii will enqueue the starter-funds transfer; the
effective difficulty adapts to recent committed plus queued faucet claim volume
in the configured lookback window, and finalized VRF seed material is required
in the challenge when that mode is enabled.

Open work for this slice now remains:
- decide whether the adaptive formula should remain purely recent-claim based or
  also incorporate faucet-reserve scarcity over time; and
- if broader `iroha_torii` test-package health matters for this branch, fix the
  unrelated `accounts_portfolio.rs` test harness breakage so the faucet slice is
  no longer hidden behind pre-existing workspace failures.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 multisig cancel integration stabilization):
`integration_tests/tests/multisig.rs`
now waits for committed multisig proposal state before assuming the cancel
route can see a prior cancel wrapper:

- the test polls `/v1/multisig/proposals/get` until the first cancel request
  becomes a committed `COLLECTING_SIGNATURES` proposal before sending the
  second cancel request that should return `APPROVE`; and
- the test also waits for the target proposal to become `CANCELED` before it
  asserts `terminal_at_ms` and terminal listing visibility, matching Torii's
  enqueue-first behavior.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo test -p integration_tests multisig_cancel_route_persists_canceled_terminal_state -- --nocapture`
  (build/test launch reached the target test, but runtime verification is
  currently blocked by an unrelated peer-startup failure:
  `active SNS domain-name lease is required before registering \`wonderland\``)

Open work for this slice now remains:
- fix the default integration-test network genesis/bootstrap path so the new
  SNS lease requirement does not abort 4-peer startup; and
- rerun the targeted multisig cancel integration test after that harness
  blocker is cleared.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud authoritative runtime config ingestion):
Soracloud services can now consume committed service config through the IVM
host surface instead of relying only on node-local materialized file paths.

- `crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs` now exposes the
  `SoracloudHostOperationV1::ReadConfig` request/response envelope types;
- `crates/ivm_abi/src/syscalls.rs`, `crates/ivm/spec/syscalls.toml`, and
  `crates/ivm/docs/syscalls.md` now publish `SORACLOUD_READ_CONFIG` in the ABI
  v1 syscall surface and keep the golden list in sync; and
- `crates/irohad/src/soracloud_runtime.rs` now resolves authoritative service
  config bytes from committed deployment state for ordinary Soracloud handler
  execution rather than only through the materialized `configs/` file tree.

Validation:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs crates/ivm_abi/src/syscalls.rs crates/irohad/src/soracloud_runtime.rs crates/iroha_cli/src/soracloud.rs crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/soracloud.rs crates/ivm/tests/abi_syscall_list_golden.rs`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_readcfg_dm cargo check -p iroha_data_model --lib`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_readcfg_ivm cargo test -p ivm abi_syscall_list_matches_golden -- --nocapture`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_readcfg_runtime cargo test -p irohad ivm_host_public_runtime_reads_authoritative_service_config_entry -- --nocapture`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_readcfg_cli cargo check -p iroha_cli --tests`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_readcfg_core cargo check -p iroha_core --tests`

Open work for this Soracloud slice now remains:
- decide the long-term secret-ingestion contract for one-click services:
  authoritative plaintext mount/injection, envelope-only reads, or explicit
  decrypt handoff;
- broaden runtime coverage from config ingestion into more end-to-end service
  execution paths that actually consume `ReadConfig`; and
- keep extending the one-click surface around budgets, domains, and private
  uploaded-model flows.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud ordinary-service config exports):
ordinary Soracloud services now have an explicit deploy-time config projection
contract instead of relying only on runtime-local convention.

- `crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs` now exposes
  `SoraConfigExportV1` plus `SoraConfigExportTargetV1::{Env,File}` and validates
  that every export references a declared required config, uses a safe target,
  and does not collide with another export;
- `crates/iroha_core/src/soracloud_runtime.rs` and
  `crates/irohad/src/soracloud_runtime.rs` now carry those exports through the
  runtime plan, materialize exported config files under
  `services/<service>/<version>/config_exports/...`, and persist the effective
  runtime environment in `effective_env.json`; and
- `crates/iroha_torii/src/soracloud.rs` now includes required
  config/secret names plus `config_exports` in control-plane revision status so
  operators can inspect the admitted runtime contract directly.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo check -p iroha_data_model --tests`
- `cargo check -p iroha_torii --tests`
- `cargo check -p irohad --tests`
- `cargo test -p iroha_data_model container_validate_ --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha_torii soracloud::tests::control_plane_snapshot_uses_authoritative_soracloud_state --lib -- --nocapture`

Open work for the full Soracloud v1 completion plan now remains:
- keep ordinary-service secrets envelope-only, but finish the remaining public
  edge surface: deterministic default hostnames, custom domain claim/verify/
  attach state, and TLS state reporting;
- add prepaid public-service budgets with billing-class derivation from admitted
  resources plus deterministic pre-dispatch balance checks;
- extend private-model publishing from local directories to pinned
  `HuggingFaceSnapshot { repo, revision }` normalization while rejecting
  unpinned or non-HF-style layouts;
- add assigned-host self-reporting for HF health so remote worker observations
  feed the same authoritative reconcile/slash path as local probes and proxy
  failures.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud multisig signed HTTP witnesses):
multisig-controlled Soracloud owners can now authorize mutation routes without
falling back to raw private keys or the legacy single-sig-only HTTP contract.

- `crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs` now exposes
  `CanonicalRequestWitnessV1` and `CanonicalRequestSignatureWitnessV1` for the
  public witness payload format;
- `crates/iroha_torii/src/app_auth.rs` now accepts `X-Iroha-Witness` for
  multisig accounts, verifies unique signer keys against the current on-chain
  `MultisigPolicy`, and preserves the existing nonce/timestamp replay window;
- `crates/iroha_torii/src/{lib.rs,soracloud.rs}` now propagate the verified
  signer set internally so mutation provenance can bind to any verified witness
  signer instead of only the primary request signer; and
- `crates/iroha/src/config{.rs,/user.rs}` plus
  `crates/iroha_cli/src/{main_shared.rs,soracloud.rs}` now let the CLI replay a
  witness JSON file via `soracloud.http_witness_file`, while the default
  single-sig path finally emits the current freshness headers
  `X-Iroha-Timestamp-Ms` and `X-Iroha-Nonce`.

Validation:
- `cargo test -p iroha_data_model canonical_request_witness_roundtrips_through_norito --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha_torii --features app_api --lib verify_accepts_valid_multisig_witness -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha_torii --features app_api --lib verify_rejects_replayed_multisig_witness_nonce -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib require_soracloud_mutation_signer_accepts_multisig_member_provenance -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha_cli build_soracloud_mutation_auth_headers -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p iroha parse_preserves_soracloud_http_witness_file --lib -- --nocapture`

Latest sync (2026-03-25 SoraFS trusted-proxy/client-IP hardening):
Torii now has a general trusted-proxy config surface and the SoraFS pin/gateway
paths resolve canonical client IPs against that allow-list instead of
collapsing callers onto the proxy socket address or trusting raw forwarded
headers on direct handler paths.

- `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/defaults.rs`,
  `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/user.rs`, and
  `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/actual.rs` now expose
  `torii.transport.trusted_proxy_cidrs` with a default-empty posture;
- `crates/iroha_torii/src/limits.rs` now provides
  `ingress_remote_ip(...)` for trusted-proxy-aware canonical-IP resolution,
  and `crates/iroha_torii/src/lib.rs` now rewrites
  `x-iroha-remote-addr` from trusted proxies only;
- `crates/iroha_torii/src/sorafs/pin.rs` and
  `crates/iroha_torii/src/sorafs/api.rs` now resolve canonical client IPs
  against `state.trusted_proxy_nets` for storage-pin policy and gateway
  fingerprinting; and
- storage-pin throttling now keys shared bearer tokens by
  `token + canonical client IP`, with focused pin/gateway/config regressions
  locking the trusted/untrusted proxy paths.

- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-trusted-proxy CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-trusted-proxy cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib storage_pin_requires_token_and_respects_allowlist_and_rate_limit -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-trusted-proxy CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-trusted-proxy cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib storage_pin_rate_limit_uses_forwarded_client_ip_from_trusted_proxy -- --nocapture`

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud required config/secret bindings):
Soracloud deploy/upgrade/rollback now validate declared required service
config and secret bindings against the effective authoritative material set,
and deploy/upgrade can carry inline initial material maps atomically.

- `crates/iroha_data_model/src/soracloud.rs` now exposes
  `SoraContainerManifestV1.required_config_names` and
  `required_secret_names`, plus an explicit
  `encode_bundle_with_materials_provenance_payload(...)` helper;
- `crates/iroha_data_model/src/isi/soracloud.rs` now carries
  `initial_service_configs` / `initial_service_secrets` on deploy and upgrade
  instructions;
- `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/soracloud.rs` now merges inline
  material maps into the effective authoritative deployment state during
  deploy/upgrade, validates required bindings for deploy/upgrade/rollback, and
  rejects config/secret deletions that would violate the active revision; and
- `crates/iroha_cli/src/soracloud.rs` plus
  `docs/source/soracloud/cli_local_control_plane.md` now expose the atomic
  first-deploy / upgrade flow through `--initial-configs` and
  `--initial-secrets`.

  declare env/file injection semantics instead of only required names plus the

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Connect remote-IP fail-closed hardening):
Connect session and websocket admission no longer treat missing internal
remote-IP metadata as loopback.

- `crates/iroha_torii/src/lib.rs` now requires the injected internal
  `x-iroha-remote-addr` header for Connect websocket/session admission and
  rejects the request when that metadata is unavailable, instead of silently
  assuming `127.0.0.1`;
- focused unit coverage now locks the missing/valid/invalid injected
  remote-IP parsing path; and
- a direct handler regression now proves Connect session creation fails closed
  when the handler is reached without the injected remote-IP header.

Validation:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/iroha_torii/src/lib.rs`
- `CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-connect-remote CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-peer-geo cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib connect_remote_ip_ -- --nocapture`
- `CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-connect-remote CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-peer-geo cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib connect_session_handler_rejects_missing_remote_addr_header -- --nocapture`

  reachable Connect bypass. The earlier CUDA runtime validation backlog closed
  on 2026-03-26.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud service config/secret runtime materialization):
committed Soracloud service config and secret entries now project into the
runtime materialization tree instead of living only in deployment state and the
private `ReadSecret` control path.

- `crates/iroha_core/src/soracloud_runtime.rs` now exposes config/secret
  generations, counts, and materialization directories in
  `SoracloudRuntimeServicePlan`;
- `crates/irohad/src/soracloud_runtime.rs` now writes committed service config
  payloads into `services/<service>/<version>/configs/...`, committed secret
  envelopes into `services/<service>/<version>/secret_envelopes/...`, and a
  synchronized raw-secret fallback tree into `secrets/<service>/<version>/...`;
- reconciliation now prunes stale authoritative secret materializations when
  entries or revisions disappear; and
- focused runtime regressions now cover both materialization and stale-entry
  pruning for committed service config/secret records.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 peer telemetry geo HTTPS-only endpoint):
peer telemetry geo lookup no longer silently falls back to a plaintext
third-party endpoint.

- `crates/iroha_torii/src/telemetry/peers/monitor.rs` now requires an
  explicit HTTPS endpoint when `torii.peer_geo.enabled=true`; missing or
  non-HTTPS endpoints fail closed and geo lookup is skipped with a warning
  instead of hitting a built-in `http://ip-api.com/json` default;
- `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/user.rs` no longer injects an implicit
  geo endpoint during parse, so the unset config state remains explicit; and
- the duplicated telemetry docs plus
  `docs/source/references/peer.template.toml` now describe
  `torii.peer_geo.endpoint` as an explicit HTTPS requirement when peer geo
  lookup is enabled.

Validation:

  2026-03-26.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Soracloud service config/secret control plane):
Soracloud now has an authoritative service-scoped config/secret mutation and
status surface instead of relying only on ad hoc local files.

- deployment state now persists service config and secret entries plus
  independent `config_generation` / `secret_generation` counters;
- new Soracloud ISIs now cover set/delete for both config and secret entries,
  with matching service-audit provenance tracking;
- Torii now serves
  `/v1/soracloud/service/config/{set,delete,status}` and
  `/v1/soracloud/service/secret/{set,delete,status}`, and the CLI exposes the
  matching `iroha app soracloud config-*` / `secret-*` commands; and
- private-runtime `ReadSecret` now checks committed deployment
  `service_secrets` before the older local file fallback.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_cfg cargo check -p iroha_core --lib`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_cfg cargo check -p iroha_torii --lib`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_cfg cargo check -p iroha_cli`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_torii_cfg cargo test -p iroha_torii authoritative_service_config_status_reads_world_state --lib`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_torii_cfg cargo test -p iroha_torii authoritative_service_secret_status_reads_world_state --lib`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_soracloud_runtime_cfg cargo test -p irohad ivm_host_private_runtime_ -- --nocapture`

Open work for this Soracloud slice now remains:
- materialize committed service config/secret records into general service
  runtime env/file mounts and deploy-time binding validation, not only the
  current private-runtime `ReadSecret` lookup path;
- add broader end-to-end mutation-route coverage for the new
  `service/config/*` and `service/secret/*` control-plane APIs, especially
  across deploy/upgrade/rollback persistence transitions; and
- decide whether the long-term runtime contract for service secrets should
  expose raw payload bytes, encoded secret envelopes, or explicit decrypt/
  mount semantics so one-click services have a single authoritative secret
  ingestion path.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 P2P TLS-only default):
outbound P2P TLS-over-TCP now fails closed by default instead of silently
downgrading to plaintext on handshake failure.

- `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/user.rs` now defaults
  `tls_fallback_to_plain` to `false`, so `network.tls_enabled=true` enforces
  TLS-only outbound dials unless operators explicitly opt into plaintext
  fallback;
- the default-config fixture snapshot, Kagami sample config, and default-like
  P2P/Torii test helpers were updated to mirror the runtime default; and
- the duplicated `docs/source/p2p*.md` transport docs now advertise plaintext
  fallback as an opt-in deployment choice instead of the default.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-sec13 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-config-tls2 cargo test -p iroha_config tls_fallback_defaults_to_tls_only -- --nocapture`
- `CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-sec13 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-p2p-sec13 cargo test -p iroha_p2p --lib start_rejects_tls_without_feature_when_tls_only_outbound -- --nocapture`
- `CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-sec13 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-p2p-sec13 cargo test -p iroha_p2p --lib tls_only_dial_requires_p2p_tls_feature_when_no_fallback -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_HOME=/tmp/iroha-cargo-home-sec13 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-torii-connect-gating cargo test -p iroha_torii --test connect_gating --no-run`

Open work from this slice:
- no new P2P transport-default finding remains once `tls_enabled=true`; the
  earlier CUDA runtime validation backlog closed on 2026-03-26.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 local QUIC proxy bind hardening):
the SoraFS workstation proxy now enforces its "local" trust boundary in code
instead of relying on operator discipline.

- `crates/sorafs_orchestrator/src/proxy.rs` now rejects non-loopback
  `LocalQuicProxyConfig.bind_addr` values with
  `ProxyError::BindAddressNotLoopback`, so the local QUIC proxy cannot be
  exposed as a remotely reachable unauthenticated bridge by configuration
  drift;
- the canonical orchestrator docs now describe `bind_addr` as loopback-only
  in `docs/source/sorafs/developer/orchestrator.md` and
  `docs/portal/docs/sorafs/orchestrator-config.md`; and
- focused regression coverage now proves a non-loopback bind fails closed
  while the intended loopback TCP bridge path still succeeds.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-proxy cargo test -p sorafs_orchestrator spawn_local_quic_proxy_rejects_non_loopback_bind_addr -- --nocapture`
- `/tmp/iroha-codex-target-proxy/debug/deps/sorafs_orchestrator-b3be10a343598c7b --exact proxy::tests::tcp_stream_bridge_transfers_payload --nocapture`

Open work for this slice now remains:
- no additional live SoraFS local-proxy finding from this pass. If non-loopback
  exposure is ever required later, design an authenticated remote mode instead
  of weakening the loopback-only bind rule.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 active `tar` advisories removed):
the previously live `tar` advisories are no longer present in the active
dependency graph.

- `xtask/src/mochi.rs` now shells out to the system `tar` binary with a fixed
  argument vector rooted at the approved bundle directory instead of using the
  Rust `tar` API, and `xtask/Cargo.toml` dropped the direct `tar` and
  `flate2` dependencies;
- `iroha_crypto` no longer pulls `libsodium-sys-stable` for Ed25519 interop
  tests; those checks now use OpenSSL in
  `crates/iroha_crypto/src/signature/ed25519.rs`, which removes the last
  active `tar` path; and
- `cargo-deny` had reduced the active advisory backlog from seven to three at
  that point: `rustls-webpki`, `derivative`, and `paste`. Later same-day
  follow-up work moved `rustls-webpki` onto the patched `0.103.10` line and
  explicitly accepted the remaining two transitive macro advisories in
  `deny.toml`.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 cargo test -p xtask create_archive_packages_bundle_directory -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 cargo check -p xtask`
- `cargo tree -p xtask -e normal -i tar`
- `cargo tree -p iroha_crypto -e all -i libsodium-sys-stable`
- `cargo tree -p iroha_crypto -e all -i tar`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 cargo test -p iroha_crypto ed25519_verify -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 cargo test -p iroha_crypto ed25519_sign -- --nocapture`
- `cargo deny check advisories bans sources --hide-inclusion-graph` (at that point still failed on `rustls-webpki`, `derivative`, and `paste`; later same-day follow-up closed those active failures)

Open work for this dependency/security slice now remains:
- no additional active advisory failure remains in the current tree; future
  maintenance is limited to upstream cleanup of the accepted transitive
  `derivative` / `paste` debt;
- clean the stale historical `tar` / `libsodium-sys-stable` lockfile entries

  of the active dependency graph.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 SDK transport guard closure):
the sampled Swift, Java/Android, Kotlin, and JS clients now treat auth
headers, app `canonicalAuth`, and raw `private_key*` JSON bodies as sensitive
transport material instead of only checking explicit bearer/API-token headers.

- Swift now centralizes the shared policy in
  `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/TransportSecurity.swift` and applies it from
  `NoritoRpcClient`, `ToriiClient`, and `ConnectClient`;
- Java/Android and Kotlin now apply the same guard from shared
  `TransportSecurity` helpers across Norito-RPC, request-builder,
  offline/subscription, SSE, and websocket surfaces;
- JS `ToriiClient._request(...)` now blocks insecure or cross-host delivery
  for `canonicalAuth` requests and JSON bodies containing `private_key*`
  fields, and its downgraded-transport telemetry now records whether the
  request carried sensitive body data or canonical auth; and
- focused regressions passed in Swift, Kotlin, Java/Android, and JS.

Validation:
- `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter 'NoritoRpcClientTests/testCallRejectsInsecureAuthorizationHeader'`
- `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter 'ConnectClientTests/testBuildsConnectWebSocketRequestRejectsInsecureTransport'`
- `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter 'ToriiClientTests/testCreateSubscriptionPlanRejectsInsecureTransportForPrivateKeyBody'`
- `cd kotlin && ./gradlew :core-jvm:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.sdk.client.TransportSecurityClientTest --console=plain`
- `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 21) ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Android/sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=~/Library/Android/sdk ./gradlew android:compileDebugUnitTestJavaWithJavac --console=plain`
- `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 21) ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Android/sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=~/Library/Android/sdk ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.NoritoRpcClientTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.SubscriptionToriiClientTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.stream.ToriiEventStreamClientTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.websocket.ToriiWebSocketClientTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --console=plain`
- `node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/transportSecurity.test.js javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiCanonicalAuth.test.js`
- `node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiSubscriptions.test.js`

Open work for this SDK/security slice now remains:
- rerun the broader Swift/Android/Kotlin/JS suites once the unrelated branch
  blockers are cleared, so the new transport guards are covered beyond the
  focused auth/private-key regressions.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 Torii webhook + MCP trust-boundary closure):
`crates/iroha_torii/src/webhook.rs` and
`crates/iroha_torii/src/mcp.rs` now preserve the same destination/caller trust
boundary across the internal bridge layers that policy checked at the edge.

- secure webhook delivery no longer re-resolves HTTPS / WSS hostnames after
  the egress guard approves a vetted address set; HTTPS now pins approved
  DNS answers into the actual connect path and WSS now handshakes over a raw
  TCP stream connected to a vetted address;
- MCP internal dispatch no longer stamps loopback for every tool call; it now
  preserves the outer request's injected remote IP and blocks
  `x-iroha-remote-addr` plus `x-forwarded-client-cert` supplied through MCP
  `headers`, so read-only MCP tools cannot inherit CIDR-based route privilege
  or ingress-proxy trust through the bridge; and
- focused regression coverage now proves both the webhook pinning helpers and
  the MCP remote-address preservation/spoof-blocking behavior.

Validation:
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-webhook-https cargo check -p iroha_torii --lib --features app_api_https`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-webhook-https cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib https_delivery_dns_override_ --features app_api_https -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-webhook2 cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib websocket_pinned_connect_addr_pins_secure_delivery_when_guarded -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-mcp cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib dispatch_route_preserves_inbound_remote_addr_for_internal_allowlist_checks -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-mcp cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib dispatch_route_blocks_remote_addr_spoofing_from_extra_headers -- --nocapture`
- `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-mcp cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib apply_extra_headers_blocks_reserved_internal_headers -- --nocapture`

Open work for this slice now remains:
- no additional live webhook/MCP trust-boundary finding from this pass, but
  the narrower `--no-default-features --features app_api,app_api_https` Torii
  lib-test matrix still has unrelated existing compile breakage in DA /
  Soracloud-gated code and should be repaired separately if minimal-feature
  coverage is expected.

Latest sync (2026-03-25 outbound P2P peer-id binding closed):
`crates/iroha_p2p/src/peer.rs` now fails outbound signed handshakes closed if
the authenticated remote key does not match the `peer_id` the network actor
intended to dial.

- The fix carries the expected outbound `PeerId` through the `ConnectedTo`,
  `SendKey`, and `GetKey` states and returns `HandshakePeerMismatch` when a
  signed handshake authenticates the wrong key, instead of silently
  constructing a different `Peer`;
- focused regression coverage now proves a mismatched-key outbound handshake is
  rejected while the normal `handshake_v1_defaults_to_trust_gossip` path still
  succeeds; and
- validation passed with
  `NORITO_KOTLIN_SKIP_TESTS=1 NORITO_JAVA_SKIP_TESTS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-target-p2p cargo test -p iroha_p2p --lib --no-run`,
  `... outgoing_handshake_rejects_unexpected_peer_identity -- --nocapture`,
  and `... handshake_v1_defaults_to_trust_gossip -- --nocapture`.

Open work for this networking/security slice now remains:
- continue the dependency/fuzz remediation backlog below. The sampled QUIC
  streaming helper path was traced in this pass and did not become a live
  runtime finding because its current `connect` / `bind` call sites are
  test-only.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 dependency + fuzz audit unblocked):
`deny.toml`, `scripts/fuzz_smoke.sh`,
`crates/{norito,ivm}/fuzz/Cargo.toml`, and

Latest sync (2026-03-24 alias-bootstrap fix restored soak readiness, but both full stable reruns are still red):
the Izanami/test-network genesis contract now satisfies the alias-leasing bootstrap rules again, so both permissioned and NPoS re-entered block production; however, the actual frontier redesign still has not removed the old RBC-era recovery behavior or reached the `<1000ms` stable-soak target.

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sns.rs` adds `seed_genesis_alias_bootstrap(...)`, which pre-seeds genesis-time SNS leases plus `CanManageAccountAlias` permissions for any domains, labeled accounts, `SetAccountLabel`, and `BindAccountAlias` instructions present in the genesis block,
  - `crates/iroha_test_network/src/config.rs` now validates generated genesis against a pre-exec world that already contains the `genesis` domain/account bootstrap state and the alias bootstrap records/permissions consumed by that genesis,
  - `crates/irohad/src/main.rs` now seeds the same alias bootstrap state on empty-state and snapshot-rebuild startup paths, and fixes `genesis_domain(...)` ownership so the bootstrap domain is owned by the raw genesis authority.

- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib seed_genesis_alias_bootstrap_covers_domains_and_account_labels -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_test_network --lib populate_genesis_results_leases_genesis_account_labels -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p irohad genesis_domain_owner_matches_genesis_authority -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo build --release -p irohad --bin iroha3d -p izanami --bin izanami`

- full-soak verdict on this cut:
  - permissioned log:
    `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_aliasbootstrap_20260324T205028Z.log`,
    peer dirs:
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-permissioned-aliasbootstrap_20260324T205028Z/irohad_test_network_9tW8sv/*`,
    failed at the one-hour duration checkpoint after `1391` strict/quorum blocks in
    `3599.99381175s` (`~2.588s/block`) with
    `quorum p95 block interval 5003ms > 1000ms`;
    final distribution:
    `median=2503ms`, `p95=5017ms`, `max=10390ms`;
    aggregate markers:
    `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=214`,
    `FetchBlockBody=0`,
    dominant lagging peer:
    `untouched_roller=114` missing-`BlockCreated` requests.
  - NPoS log:
    `/tmp/izanami_npos_aliasbootstrap_20260324T215301Z.log`,
    peer dirs:
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-npos-aliasbootstrap_20260324T215301Z/irohad_test_network_lZOryH/*`,
    failed earlier on divergence after about `2664.424966s`
    (`quorum_min=989`, `strict_reference=1017`, divergence `28` > threshold `16`,
    `~2.694s/block` at the last quorum height);
    final distribution:
    `median=2503ms`, `p95=5157ms`, `max=30125ms`;
    aggregate markers:
    `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=145`,
    `FetchBlockBody=0`,
    dominant lagging peer:
    `renewing_nightjar=95` missing-`BlockCreated` requests.

- open work after this sync:
  - keep the alias-bootstrap fix, but stop treating it as a consensus fix; it only restored soak readiness,
  - implement exact contiguous-frontier repair (`FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse`) and thread it into the happy path, because both full reruns still show zero exact-body pulls and sustained RBC-side missing-`BlockCreated` repair,
  - remove the remaining RBC hot-path recovery / stash-driven rescue behavior that is still visible as `rbc_chunk_stash`, `rbc_ready_stash`, `rbc_deliver_stash`, and `rbc_*_single_chunk_frontier_body` contexts with multi-second dwell,
  - trace the late laggards `untouched_roller` and `renewing_nightjar` around the failing windows (`~1376-1391` permissioned, `~989-1017` NPoS) to confirm why frontier repair still peels one peer off instead of collapsing onto exact body pull.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 full preserved-peer soak attempt on current tree is blocked at genesis):
the intended permissioned and NPoS one-hour stable reruns did not reach block
production. Both modes failed during peer startup because the Izanami-generated
genesis now violates runtime invariants enforced by the current executor.

- execution/artifacts:
  - rebuilt current binaries with
    `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo build --release -p irohad --bin iroha3d -p izanami --bin izanami`
    because the dirty tree currently fails unrelated Norito Kotlin parity checks
    during release build,
  - permissioned run:
    `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_inline_frontier_20260324T192726Z.log`,
    peers under
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-permissioned-inline_frontier_20260324T192726Z/irohad_test_network_1AKX9T/*`,
  - NPoS run:
    `/tmp/izanami_npos_inline_frontier_20260324T192911Z.log`,
    peers under
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-npos-inline_frontier_20260324T192911Z/irohad_test_network_OQ2Rdr/*`.

- observed blocker:
  - both modes fail genesis validation before block 1 with the same SNS/account
    bootstrap errors:
    `active SNS domain-name lease is required before registering wonderland`,
    `active SNS domain-name lease is required before registering chaosnet`,
    and a missing-account `GrantBox`,
  - NPoS also fails `RegisterPublicLaneValidator` because the referenced asset
    definition is absent in the generated genesis.

- open work after this sync:
  - fix Izanami / test-network genesis generation so it satisfies the current
    SNS and staking/runtime bootstrap invariants before attempting any more
    consensus soak comparisons,
  - only after the cluster reaches block 1 again should the permissioned/NPoS
    `3600s / 2000 blocks / progress-timeout 600s` envelope be rerun to critique
    frontier cadence or late-peer peel-off on this branch.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 `BlockCreated` inline-frontier metadata tranche):
this cut starts the protocol simplification by making `BlockCreated` capable of
carrying the frontier metadata the leader previously split across proposal-side
messages, while deliberately stopping short of the full exact-pull and RBC
message removal.

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/message.rs` adds optional
    `BlockCreatedFrontierInfo` so a single `BlockCreated` can advertise the
    highest QC, payload hash, proposer/epoch, roster hash, chunk manifest,
    chunk root, and leader signature for the slot,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/proposal_handlers.rs` now treats
    that inline metadata as sufficient to seed proposal/hint cache state and to
    mark `(height, view)` as observed once the body is accepted,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs` now prefers enriched
    `BlockCreated` emission on both fresh proposal assembly and cached-slot
    rebroadcast, skipping standalone `Proposal` posts when the frontier
    metadata is embedded, and
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/commit.rs` upgrades payload gossip
    to the enriched `BlockCreated` form instead of separately reposting
    `ProposalHint`.

- focused validation passed:
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib assemble_proposal_schedules_block_created_before_rbc_without_frontier_proposal_posts -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib pacemaker_rebroadcasts_cached_frontier_block_when_leader -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_with_frontier_metadata_marks_view_seen_without_proposal_message -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_after_block_created_is_safe_and_marks_metadata -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_does_not_wake_commit_pipeline_without_block_created -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_without_cached_proposal_advances_active_view -- --nocapture`
  - `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_with_matching_proposal_advances_active_view -- --nocapture`

- open work after this sync:
  - remove standalone `Proposal` / `ProposalHint` receive-side dependencies so
    `BlockCreated` is not just richer, but truly the only frontier
    advertisement on the hot path,
  - replace contiguous-frontier repair with exact
    `FetchBlockBody` / `BlockBodyResponse` instead of `FetchPendingBlock` /
    block-sync fallback,
  - delete `RbcReady` / `RbcDeliver` and the worker-loop near-tip repair /
    rebroadcast scans that keep old sessions hot, and
  - rerun short stable probes, then the preserved-peer one-hour envelope, only
    after the exact-pull path and READY/DELIVER removal are both in place.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 full preserved-peer soak rerun on signer-first frontier repair):
this cut is still not acceptable. It reduced some recovery chatter, but
permissioned regressed and NPoS still ended in late one-peer divergence.

- full-soak verdict on this cut:
  - permissioned log:
    `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_signerfirst_20260324T150450Z.log`,
    peer dirs:
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-permissioned-signerfirst_20260324T150450Z/irohad_test_network_S4WDKa/*`,
    failed at the duration checkpoint after `1402` strict/quorum blocks in
    `3599.9948775s` (`~2.57s/block`) with
    `quorum p95 block interval 5003ms > 1000ms`;
    aggregate markers:
    `requested missing block payload from fetch targets=126`,
    `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=127`,
    `deferring local RBC READY: authoritative payload unavailable=157`.
  - NPoS log:
    `/tmp/izanami_npos_signerfirst_20260324T160631Z.log`,
    peer dirs:
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-npos-signerfirst_20260324T160631Z/irohad_test_network_4GU655/*`,
    failed on divergence after `1182` strict/quorum blocks in `3218.652414s`
    (`~2.72s/block`) with
    `height divergence exceeded safety window (divergence 29, threshold 16)`;
    aggregate markers:
    `requested missing block payload from fetch targets=105`,
    `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT=109`,
    `deferring local RBC READY: authoritative payload unavailable=75`.
  - the late NPoS laggard was `educated_sparrow`
    (`ea0130A685FD8C5A...`), which carried the highest missing-`BlockCreated`
    and READY-deferral counts before divergence.

- next work after this rerun:
  - stop treating signer-first frontier fetch plus self-target filtering as
    sufficient; they reduce some noise, but they do not restore a 1-second
    hot path or prevent late peer peel-off,
  - trace the late `educated_sparrow` failure window around heights
    `1182-1183`: the peer was still receiving targeted READY/DELIVER traffic,
    but it did not turn that into committed progress before divergence fired,
  - explain why exact frontier repair still leaves the cluster in repeated
    `2500ms` / `3335ms` / `5000ms` cadence even when no peer is yet lagging,
  - separately explain the permissioned regression from `1459` to `1402`
    blocks/hour on the same stable envelope before making another repair-path
    semantics change.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 signer-first frontier repair + full latest unknown-prev refresh):
this cut narrows the remaining recovery trigger without changing wire/config
semantics:

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` keeps metadata-only
    frontier `BlockCreated` recovery signer-first whenever INIT already names a
    proposer, instead of immediately blasting the whole topology,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` drops the local peer from
    missing-block request target sets before posting `FetchPendingBlock`,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs` keeps unknown-prev fallback in
    full-share mode when the requester is asking for the latest frontier so
    exact repair is not downgraded to tiny incremental slices.

- next verification / follow-up:
  - rerun the full preserved-peer permissioned and NPoS stable soaks on this
    cut and compare `s/block`, divergence, `requested missing block payload`,
    `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT`, READY deferrals,
    and fallback-anchor shares against the previous frontier-repair soak,
  - if one-peer divergence still persists in NPoS, inspect whether exact
    missing-block responses are still falling back to anchor sharing on the
    lagging peer before changing pacemaker behavior again.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 frontier repair + single-chunk authoritative-body fast path):
this cut keeps `BlockCreated` authoritative but closes two remaining slow-path
regressions and one catch-up bottleneck:

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs` now treats unknown-prev full fallback
    as contiguous catch-up instead of tiny-fanout gossip, serving a materially
    larger suffix per response before frame-cap trimming,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` now suppresses
    `QuorumTimeout` / `StakeQuorumTimeout` view rotation while same-slot
    contiguous-frontier repair is still active,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` now tracks missing
    `BlockCreated` immediately for near-frontier single-chunk RBC sessions even
    when INIT metadata is complete.

- next verification / follow-up:
  - rerun the full preserved-peer permissioned and NPoS stable soaks on this
    cut and compare divergence, missing-payload fetch volume, READY deferrals,
    and effective `s/block` against the ingress-grace soak,
  - if `trigger_view_change_` becomes a gating slice again, repair the fixture
    setup in
    `trigger_view_change_realigns_conflicting_committed_edge_qcs_before_new_view_vote`
    so it deterministically seeds a committed QC before the trigger path runs.

Latest sync (2026-03-24 contiguous-frontier authoritative-body ingress grace):
the next cut after the slot-owner fix now directly addresses the remaining hot
path regression: contiguous-frontier votes / READY that briefly arrive before
`BlockCreated` are no longer allowed to open missing-payload recovery at
`dwell_ms=0`.

- implementation on this cut:
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` now tracks contiguous-frontier
    missing bodies immediately but holds the first fetch for a short bounded
    `AUTHORITATIVE_BODY_INGRESS_FETCH_GRACE`, then forces the first fetch once
    that grace expires,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/qc.rs` applies that gate before
    QC-first, locked-QC, and retry-loop missing-block fetch planning,
  - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` applies the same gate
    before pending-RBC missing-`BlockCreated` recovery,
  - RBC READY / DELIVER now recompute authoritative payload availability after
    local hydration in the same pass.
- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib missing_block_ingress_fetch_gate -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib defer_qc_for_missing_block_force_retry_now_bypasses_retry_window -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib request_missing_block_for_pending_rbc_ -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib maybe_emit_rbc_ready_missing_chunks_requests_missing_block_for_metadata_only_session -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_ -- --nocapture`

Open work after this sync:
- rerun short permissioned and NPoS stable probes on this exact ingress-grace
  cut and compare `requested missing block payload from fetch targets`,
  `requested missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT`, and
  `deferring local RBC READY: authoritative payload unavailable` directly
  against the slot-owner-only soak,
- only rerun the full preserved-peer one-hour envelope after those shorter
  probes show the eager `dwell_ms=0` recovery chatter materially collapsing,
- if READY / DELIVER deferrals remain high after the grace cut, isolate the
  residual path to true RBC session convergence instead of reopening proposal
  ownership or same-slot owner logic.

Latest sync (2026-03-23 authoritative slot-owner fix for `BlockCreated` happy-path reentry):
the primary regression on the `BlockCreated`-authoritative cut is no longer
"find the cause". The cause was same-slot ownership reopening after cleanup:
once a `BlockCreated` body had claimed `(height, view)`, later cleanup and
pacemaker paths could admit a second body or locally re-propose the same view.

- implementation on this cut:
  - Sumeragi now keeps an internal authoritative `(height, view) -> block_hash`
    map populated only by accepted `BlockCreated` bodies,
  - normal `handle_block_created(...)` drops conflicting same-slot bodies when
    an authoritative owner already exists, while block-sync
    commit-evidence-import paths still retain the explicit override path, and
  - pacemaker proposal assembly now refuses same-view reassembly once an
    authoritative `BlockCreated` already owns the slot; frontier cleanup keeps
    the active frontier slot closed while pruning future authoritative slots.
- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_without_proposal_does_not_replace_authoritative_same_slot_owner -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib frontier_cleanup_preserves_frontier_authoritative_slot_and_prunes_future_slots -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib pacemaker_defers_reproposal_when_authoritative_block_already_owns_slot -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_sync_payload_mismatch_with_commit_evidence_replaces_stale_frontier_owner -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_payload_mismatch_preserves_active_frontier_owner -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_ -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_does_not_wake_commit_pipeline_without_block_created -- --nocapture`

Open work after this sync:
- rerun the preserved-peer permissioned and NPoS stable soaks on this exact
  authoritative-slot-owner cut and compare them directly against the earlier
  failing `blockcreated_authoritative` artifacts,
- confirm that the height-`400/401` same-view reentry / double-vote pattern is
  gone before spending more time on secondary READY or missing-payload churn,
  and
- only if the rerun still misses the envelope, critique the remaining
  permissioned/NPoS tail strictly after removing this same-slot ownership bug
  from the picture.

Latest sync (2026-03-23 `BlockCreated` authoritative happy path cut):
the internal Sumeragi happy path now treats `BlockCreated` as the only
authoritative payload ingress while keeping `Proposal` and `ProposalHint` on
the wire for compatibility and liveness metadata only.

- implementation on this cut:
  - proposal assembly now caches metadata but handles/posts `BlockCreated`
    before `Proposal` and before RBC follow-on traffic,
  - `handle_proposal(...)` still performs stateless and highest-QC/lock checks
    plus cache/liveness bookkeeping, but it no longer wakes the payload
    pipeline or starts missing-payload recovery on its own,
  - payload-dependent gating now keys off authoritative local body presence
    instead of proposal evidence, and duplicate / block-sync `BlockCreated`
    hydration now re-stamps RBC metadata and retries READY/DELIVER immediately.
- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_created_ -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_does_not_wake_commit_pipeline_without_block_created -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_hint_does_not_wake_commit_pipeline_without_block_created -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_after_block_created_is_safe_and_marks_metadata -- --nocapture`
  - `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_gated_by_missing_dependencies_clears_authoritative_local_payload -- --nocapture`

Open work after this sync:
- use the newer full-soak verdict above as the runtime acceptance source for
  this cut instead of rerunning the same commands again without another
  consensus change,
- if payload fetch churn remains visible, keep narrowing the remaining
  authoritative-payload / recovery interaction rather than reintroducing
  proposal-led progression, and
- finish broader repository validation (`cargo test --workspace`,
  `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`) once the consensus
  runtime verdict on the next cut is worth keeping.

Latest sync (2026-03-23 unified RBC session ingress lane plus sampled preserved-peer reruns):
the `RbcChunks` lane now carries the full healthy-path RBC session ingress
chain (`BlockCreated`, `RbcInit`, `RbcChunk*`, `RbcReady`, `RbcDeliver`) while
`Blocks` is left for fallback block/control traffic, and sampled preserved-peer
stable reruns show the INIT-side metadata split collapsing without curing the
remaining READY convergence lag:

Latest sync (2026-03-21 payload-first RBC local progression):
implemented the requested payload-first RBC hot-path simplification without any
wire-format or public API change:

  `payload_available_for_da(...)` accept authoritative local RBC payload before

Latest sync (2026-03-21 INIT-side missing-`BlockCreated` recovery fix):
removed the bogus same-slot missing-`BlockCreated` fetch that was still being
seeded after RBC INIT even when INIT metadata was already sufficient to
rebuild the signed block after payload delivery:

  `BlockCreated` on INIT/READY when the RBC session is genuinely missing
  payload hash, block header, leader signature, or is already invalid,
- `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` now checks that invariant
  against the in-flight session inside `maybe_emit_rbc_ready()` instead of
  consulting the map after the session has been removed for local processing,
- focused RBC regressions passed:

- `cargo fmt --all`, `git diff --check`, and
  `cargo build --release -p irohad --bin iroha3d -p izanami --bin izanami`
  passed,

    `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_rbc_init_recovery_fix_20260321T230747.log`,
    peer dirs:
    `/tmp/iroha-soak-permissioned-rbc_init_recovery_fix_20260321T230747/irohad_test_network_2ytcak/*`,

Latest sync (2026-03-21 diagnose-first Sumeragi simplification):
landed the first actor-path simplification tranche on the current tree:

- added an internal round trace keyed by `(height, view)` so the actor now
  records explicit phase/cause transitions for proposal, block/RBC, validation,
  votes, QC, commit request/completion, and no-progress wakes,
- removed the runtime commit worker attachment; commit work now runs inline from
  `finalize_pending_block()` while retaining only the synchronous `commit.inflight`
  marker for guard/status compatibility,
- switched the happy-path wakeups to cause-aware actor nudges across proposal,
  validation, votes, QCs, RBC, block sync, and commit completion,
- focused `iroha_core` validation passed, but preserved-peer soaks have not been
  rerun on this refactor yet.

Open work after this sync:
- rerun both actual preserved-peer shared-host stable soaks on this refactor
  before claiming any pacing win; current soak numbers still refer to the
  pre-trace / pre-inline-commit actor path,
- use the new round trace to identify which phase dominates the remaining
  `5-10s` windows before changing any timing formulas again,
- continue collapsing Sumeragi progression into one explicit actor-owned FSM;
  the trace/cause plumbing is in place, but some handler-local progression and
  compatibility shims still remain,
- once the dominant phase is proven, remove the remaining compatibility-only
  commit-result drain path and any stale split-era tests that are no longer
  exercising real runtime behavior.

Latest sync (2026-03-21 fixed high-priority plaintext batch class loss in `iroha_p2p`):
the permissioned preserved-peer crash was a transport batching bug, not a
consensus commit bug:

- `crates/iroha_p2p/src/peer.rs` now restores `plain_high_class` after a
  cap-triggered high-priority plaintext flush before appending the next
  message,
- root cause was the overflow path clearing `plain_high_class` inside
  `flush_plain_high()` and then reusing `plain_high` for the next message
  without restoring its scheduling class, which later panicked on
  `high plaintext batch must track its scheduling class`,
- new regression coverage:
  `message_sender_restores_high_batch_class_after_msg_cap_flush`,
- focused validation passed:
  - `cargo fmt --all`,
  - `cargo test -p iroha_p2p message_sender_ -- --nocapture`,
- post-fix permissioned smoke log:
  `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_p2p_fix_smoke_20260321T163931.log`,
  peer dirs:
  `/tmp/iroha-smoke-permissioned-p2p_fix_20260321T163931/irohad_test_network_Z5LEV5/*`,
  and the smoke reached target cleanly at quorum/strict height `133` without
  the prior panic, `Connection refused`, or strict-stall signature.

Open work after this sync:
- rerun the full preserved-peer permissioned soak on the current tree now that
  the transport panic is fixed, so permissioned can be compared fairly against
  the prior `~3.3-3.5s` plateau,
- keep treating the remaining latency issue separately from the transport fix:
  the post-fix permissioned smoke still reported `interval_p95_ms=3335`,
- keep the new `iroha_p2p` regression test in the regular focused suite so the
  high-priority batching invariant stays covered while consensus pacing work
  continues.
