Open work remains late, global commit-quorum convergence (all peers stalled at
the same frontier under load), not early single-peer preserved-mismatch
deadlock.

Latest sync (2026-03-17 zero-vote active-owner no-op drop suppression):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` now suppresses
contiguous-frontier zero-vote quorum-timeout drop/requeue when the existing
`quorum_timeout` frontier owner for that height is still active in the current
window but has no recorded `last_action_at` yet. This removes the observed
no-op shape where commit-quorum warnings could still emit
`drop_pending=true` together with `frontier_recovery_advance=Some(None)`.
Added regression
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_does_not_drop_while_quorum_timeout_owner_active_without_action`
in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`, fixed the unrelated
`iroha_core` lib-test import blocker in
`crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/domain.rs` (`BOB_ID`,
`CanRegisterAccount`), and revalidated the ownership subset green
(`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`, `zero_vote_quorum_timeout_*`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`).

Warmed keep-dirs probe rerun
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_zero_vote_owner_active_noop_guard_20260317T001915.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_VEMRRO`)
still timed out at `quorum 67 / strict 72`, but compared to the previous warmed
rerun (`1qE1Ki`, `61 / 67`) peer-log churn improved:
`commit quorum missing ...` `16 -> 8`, `drop_pending=true` `6 -> 0`,
and `frontier_recovery_advance=Some(None)` warnings `1 -> 0`. Open work remains
late commit-quorum convergence beyond ~`70` blocks (vote-backed/split-vote
stalls), not repeated zero-vote no-op drop ownership churn.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 zero-vote recent-progress drop guard):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` now defers zero-vote
quorum-timeout drop/requeue when pending progress is still inside the current
reschedule backoff window (`progress_age < reschedule_backoff`), instead of
dropping immediately on every zero-vote timeout edge. Added regression
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_defers_drop_when_progress_is_recent` in
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs` (defer first, then drop and
handoff after backoff expiry). Build validation on this tree is green
(`cargo check -p iroha_core --lib`, `cargo build --release -p irohad --bin iroha3d`);
`cargo test -p iroha_core --lib ...` was temporarily blocked at that point by unrelated existing
lib-test compile errors in `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/domain.rs`
(missing `BOB_ID` / `CanRegisterAccount` imports in that test module). Warmed
keep-dirs probe
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_zero_vote_recent_progress_guard_20260316T234527.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_oL218c`)
still timed out at `quorum 48 / strict 54`, but removed the pathological
near-zero-progress zero-vote drop signature (`drop_pending=true` with
`progress_age_ms <= 100`: `1 -> 0`; total `drop_pending=true`: `5 -> 3` vs prior
run). Immediate open work remains commit-quorum convergence under queue pressure:
this run collapsed around heights `10` and `33` with competing same-height block
hashes and split votes (`1/2`), not missing-block dwell ownership duplication.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 strict elapsed-window dwell ownership revalidation):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` now enforces contiguous-frontier
owner windows via elapsed cooldown (`now - last_action_at < frontier_recovery_window`)
for both `frontier_recovery_owns_height_window(...)` and
`frontier_recovery_action_taken_in_current_window(...)`, and the same-height
reset path now preserves the active frontier owner when an action already
occurred in-window (so dependency-progress resets do not re-arm a second
same-window catch-up action). Added regression
`frontier_stall_does_not_emit_second_recovery_action_in_same_window`, and
re-ran the ownership subset green
(`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`). Fresh warmed
keep-dirs probe
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_missing_payload_owner_20260316T230137.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_jLEG3t`)
timed out at `quorum 55 / strict 56`, but peer logs showed one contiguous-frontier
missing-block dwell routed handoff (`height=36`) and no direct `MissingPayload`
fallback warnings. Immediate open work remains vote-backed commit-quorum
convergence / queue-pressure collapse, not mid-window ownership duplication in
contiguous-frontier missing-block dwell.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 zero-vote same-window quorum-timeout suppression):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` now suppresses
contiguous-frontier zero-vote quorum-timeout repeats while the frontier recovery
owner already holds the current window (`frontier_recovery_owns_height_window`),
instead of re-entering the same handoff path again in-window. Added focused
regression
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_suppresses_same_window_repeat_while_frontier_owner_active`
and re-ran the ownership subset (`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_*`,
`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`) green. Warmed
keep-dirs runtime probes still time out before `target_blocks=120`, but the
no-fault/latency-gated run
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_zero_vote_window_owner_f0_lg1000_20260316T221556.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_nxBw0A`)
reduced commit-quorum reschedule churn relative to the previous comparable
keep-dirs baseline (`WN2zje`): total warnings `34 -> 27`,
`drop_pending=true` `12 -> 7`, and
`drop_pending=true + frontier_recovery_advance=Some(None)` `6 -> 0`. Open work
remains commit-quorum convergence (the run still stopped at `quorum/strict 64/64`),
not contiguous-frontier ownership duplication.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 dwell warning attribution cut):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/qc.rs` now logs contiguous-frontier
missing-block dwell escalation with per-request `block` context and post-handoff
action attribution. Warn-level dwell lines now map to actual routed actions or
explicit direct fallback (`frontier controller not responsible`), while no-op
handoff (`FrontierRecoveryAdvance::None`) is emitted at debug-level to avoid
overstating repeated work. Focused ownership subset remains green
(`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_and_hands_frontier_to_quorum_timeout_recovery`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`). Keep-dirs probe
rerun to completion
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_frontier_dwell_window_owner_logcut_full_20260316T212527.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_UBttAh`)
timed out at `quorum 62 / strict 69`. In that run, peer logs emitted none of the
old generic `routing through frontier recovery` dwell strings and instead
produced block-scoped direct-fallback warnings
(`frontier controller not responsible ...`) only twice for the same
`height=26`/hash pair. Immediate open work remains runtime convergence, not log
attribution: vote-backed commit-quorum stalls still cap the run in the high-60s.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 revalidation of contiguous-frontier dwell ownership):
re-ran the focused ownership subset (`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_and_hands_frontier_to_quorum_timeout_recovery`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`) and all remain
green. Warmed 120-block healthy NPoS probe with keep-dirs
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_frontier_dwell_window_owner_keepdirs_20260316T204058.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_6IxdNP`)
still timed out at `quorum 66 / strict 70`. Peer logs still include repeated
`missing block dwell exceeded retry window; routing through frontier recovery`
warnings (`42` lines), but `requested unified frontier recovery range pull` with
`reason="missing_payload"` appeared once (height `69`), so the contiguous-frontier
missing-payload handoff itself remains one-action in that window. Immediate open
work is still vote-backed commit-quorum convergence beyond the low-70s and
warning-path noise reduction (for example, include hash/context in the dwell warn
line so same-height multi-request fan-out is distinguishable from repeated action).

Latest sync (2026-03-16 vote-backed contiguous-frontier window ownership):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` now suppresses
vote-backed contiguous-frontier quorum-timeout retransmit directly via
`frontier_recovery_owns_height_window(height, now)` instead of using
`last_quorum_reschedule`/cause heuristics, so same-window retries no longer use
reschedule markers as a rearm signal. The focused regression
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`
was expanded to assert same-window suppression without a prior marker, no direct
`MissingQc`/`MissingPayload` fallback while frontier ownership is active, and
next-window rearm behavior. Focused ownership subset remains green
(`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_*`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_and_hands_frontier_to_quorum_timeout_recovery`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`). Warmed
120-block NPoS probe
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_vote_backed_window_owner_20260316T202521.log`)
still timed out, but moved to `quorum 67 / strict 71` from the prior warmed
`52 / 57` rerun. Immediate open work remains vote-backed commit-quorum
convergence past the low-70s; this cut reduced duplicate same-window ownership
but did not close the target-block gate.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 contiguous-frontier missing-block dwell handoff-once):
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{main_loop.rs,qc.rs}` now treats
contiguous-frontier missing-block dwell as a pure handoff into the existing
frontier controller at most once per recovery window. A new
`frontier_recovery_owns_height_window(...)` helper gates retry-window and
view-change-window dwell branches so they suppress both re-handoff and direct
`MissingPayload` fallback when the current frontier owner already acted in the
same window, and a debug-only suppression line was added. Focused regressions
are green
(`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_hands_off_once_per_window`,
`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_does_not_fallback_to_direct_view_change_while_window_owned`,
`contiguous_frontier_missing_payload_dwell_rearms_only_after_next_window`) and
the earlier ownership subset remains green
(`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_and_hands_frontier_to_quorum_timeout_recovery`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`frontier_recovery_rotates_once_after_cleanup_window_expires`). Warmed 120-block
NPoS probes still timed out (`46/50`, rerun `52/57`, `46/51`, then `14/14` in a
run with concurrent workload overflow failures), so the next bottleneck remains
vote-backed commit-quorum convergence/reschedule churn rather than ownership
duplication in contiguous-frontier missing-block dwell.

Latest sync (2026-03-16 zero-vote frontier-owner handoff):
simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` so a
zero-vote quorum-timeout drop on the contiguous frontier now immediately seeds
and advances the existing `quorum_timeout` frontier controller instead of
deleting the pending block and leaving an ownerless empty slot behind. Focused
regressions are green
(`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_and_hands_frontier_to_quorum_timeout_recovery`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_empty_frontier_missing_qc_while_quorum_timeout_recovery_owns_window`,
`reschedule_defers_zero_vote_quorum_timeout_while_block_queue_backlogged`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`stake_quorum_timeout_skips_noop_reschedule_with_full_signer_set`). A fresh
direct-binary healthy NPoS probe
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_zero_vote_owner_handoff_20260316T101333Z.log`,
network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_WIrcsR`)
shows the handoff firing live at height `50`
(`drop_pending=true handoff_frontier_quorum_timeout_owner=true frontier_recovery_advance=Some(CatchUp)`),
and the overall ceiling moved slightly from `quorum 65 / strict 69` to
`quorum 67 / strict 73`. That is not enough. The dominant runtime owner is now
clearer: repeated `missing block dwell exceeded retry window; routing through
frontier recovery` is still driving the collapse, with late vote-backed
quorum-reschedule warnings only as a secondary symptom. Immediate open work is
to reduce the repeated missing-block dwell churn itself, not to add another
timeout stage. The next useful cut is in the QC/missing-block path: make
contiguous-frontier missing-block retry/reanchor idempotent within a window so
the same height does not keep re-triggering frontier recovery after the first
catch-up action.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 vote-backed reschedule waits for stale progress):
routed active-height lock rejection in
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/proposal_handlers.rs` through the
same frontier recovery owner used by idle/quorum-timeout recovery, so that
lock-rejected `BlockCreated` no longer performs a direct `MissingQc` view
change on its own. Tightened
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/pending_block.rs` so
`vote_backed_reschedule_due(...)` now requires one full backoff interval with
no new vote/QC progress before retransmit is allowed. Focused regressions are
green
(`block_created_without_hint_reject_at_active_height_routes_through_unified_frontier_recovery`,
`vote_backed_reschedule_requires_progress_after_last_attempt`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_quorum_timeout_while_progress_is_recent`,
`reschedule_skips_repeated_vote_backed_quorum_timeout_without_new_progress`),
and a fresh direct-binary healthy NPoS probe moved the ceiling from
`quorum 56 / strict 63` to `quorum 65 / strict 71`. Immediate open work is no
longer the early `~200ms` vote-backed retransmit bug; it is the later low-70s
stall where repeated executor trigger lookup failures
(`Find(Trigger("repeat_trigger_0"))`) line up with renewed single-vote quorum
misses. Next useful cuts are: isolate whether that executor noise is creating
the consensus backlog, and inspect why the terminal frontier still falls back
to `votes=1/3` after the pacing fix.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 no-op quorum-reschedule suppression):
simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs` so
vote-backed quorum timeout handling no longer records a reschedule marker when
it has no actionable work. If a stalled pending block already has a numerically
full observed signer set and no missing retransmit targets, or if pacing/cooldown
suppresses every retransmit action, the helper now leaves the pending block
untouched and returns `false` instead of pretending progress happened. The
pacemaker attempt logs in `propose.rs` now also report `tracked_height` instead
of the committed tip height, so late proposal-gap analysis matches the actual
frontier under evaluation. Focused regressions are green
(`stake_quorum_timeout_skips_noop_reschedule_with_full_signer_set`,
`reschedule_skips_vote_backed_retransmit_while_frontier_quorum_timeout_window_owned`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_without_immediate_view_change`,
`reschedule_skips_repeated_vote_backed_quorum_timeout_without_new_progress`).
This cut also suppresses another duplicate edge: once the contiguous frontier is
already inside a `quorum_timeout` recovery window, vote-backed pending no longer
emits a second retransmit cycle inside that same window. Immediate open work is
runtime validation, not another state branch: the currently running NPoS probe
in session `95142` is stale for the latest tree because this suppression landed
after it started, so the next useful action is a fresh short healthy NPoS probe
on the current code and then comparison against the prior late churn around
heights `77/92/100/102`.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 matching frontier pending suppresses direct MissingQc):
simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` so
`force_view_change_if_idle(...)` no longer emits a direct `MissingQc` rotation
when the contiguous frontier already has a matching pending block for the
current `(height, view)`. That path now yields to the existing pending/cached
slot owner instead of acting as a second rotation authority. Focused
regressions are green
(`force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_missing_qc_when_matching_frontier_pending_exists`,
`force_view_change_if_idle_`). The previous warmed NPoS probe
(`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_propose_owner_unified_20260315T1234Z.log`)
still failed at `quorum 63 / strict 70`, but it materially narrowed the live
shape: cached-slot timeout no longer forced view change directly, and the
remaining direct owner in the logs was `proposal_handlers` `missing_qc` with
`pending_match=true` at heights `9`, `28`, and `48`. Immediate open work is to
finish the rerun in session `26144` and verify that those `pending_match=true`
`MissingQc` lines disappear or move later. If they do not, the next candidate
owner to collapse is the active-height lock-reject path in
`proposal_handlers.rs`.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 cached proposal-slot timeout owner reduction):
simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs` so a stalled
cached proposal on the contiguous frontier no longer triggers its own direct
`QuorumTimeout` view change. That path now seeds/advances the existing
`frontier_recovery` controller and leaves non-contiguous heights on the old
direct path. Focused regressions are green
(`pacemaker_routes_contiguous_cached_slot_stall_through_frontier_recovery`,
`maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending_`). Immediate open work remains
runtime validation, not more branching: let the fresh release NPoS probe in
session `35751` clear the child `iroha3d` warmup build, then compare the live
peer logs against the old failure signature. The specific questions are whether
`cached proposal slot stalled past quorum timeout; forcing view change`
disappears and whether the plateau moves beyond the previous `66/72` envelope.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 frontier recovery owner unification): simplified
`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` so stalled contiguous frontier
pending and empty-frontier `missing_qc` no longer act as competing timeout
owners. `maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending(...)` now hands the
contiguous frontier into the existing `frontier_recovery` controller instead of
forcing a second direct rotation, and empty-frontier `missing_qc` now suppresses
rotation/cleanup while that quorum-timeout-owned controller still holds the
active window. Focused regressions are green across the affected slices
(`maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending_`, `force_view_change_if_idle_`,
`frontier_recovery_`, `stake_quorum_timeout_reschedules_without_immediate_view_change`,
`zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_without_immediate_view_change`,
`missing_qc_view_change_suppressed_when_frontier_reanchor_already_emitted`,
`committed_edge_conflict_`). Immediate open work is runtime validation, not more
state machinery: finish the fresh release NPoS 120-block probe currently still
compiling in session `27010`, inspect whether the previous fixed-height
`missing_qc` orbit is reduced, then rerun the healthy canary/full soak matrix
only if that probe looks materially better.

This roadmap enumerates the outstanding efforts required to ship the optional
NPoS Sumeragi mode and keep the broader Nexus transition on track. For every task listed here we are preparing the first public release, so teams can design and implement with a clean slate. Completed
items continue to live in `status.md`; only tasks that still need engineering
work appear here.
SDK parity note: Swift offline receipt vectors are now synced to the canonical artifacts; no new SDK open items were added.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 vote-backed quorum-reschedule damping): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{pending_block.rs,reschedule.rs}` so vote-backed quorum reschedule no longer refreshes frontier progress and can only rearm after real new progress (`last_progress > last_quorum_reschedule`). Focused regressions are green (`vote_backed_reschedule_requires_progress_after_last_attempt`, `stake_quorum_timeout_reschedules_without_immediate_view_change`, `reschedule_defers_vote_backed_quorum_timeout_while_consensus_backlogged`, `reschedule_skips_repeated_vote_backed_quorum_timeout_without_new_progress`). This cut is intentionally narrow: zero-vote stale payload drop/requeue semantics are unchanged, but repeated vote-backed retransmit churn is now bounded and no longer acts like a second frontier timeout owner. Runtime verdict is still pending because the fresh keep-dirs healthy NPoS probe (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_vote_backed_reschedule_once_20260315T075049Z.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_oOdetz`) is still in the child `iroha3d` warmup build. Immediate open work for this tranche: finish that probe and compare the preserved peer logs against the earlier `57/64` keep-dirs run. The specific acceptance question is whether ordinary frontier heights still produce repeated `commit quorum missing past timeout; rescheduling block for reassembly` warnings after the first vote-backed retransmit, or whether the dominant stall shape shifts elsewhere. If the churn is materially reduced, rerun the 15-minute healthy canary before resuming any 60-minute matrix work.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 proposal-owned commit activation): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{proposal_handlers.rs,validation.rs,commit.rs}` so a slot cannot become active commit work without a real `Proposal`. `BlockCreated` may still cache payload in `pending_blocks`, but pending blocks with no observed/cached proposal and no commit QC now defer validation and cannot become `commit.inflight`. Focused regressions are green (`validation_defers_without_proposal_evidence`, `commit_pipeline_defers_valid_pending_without_proposal_evidence`, plus the updated direct commit-pipeline fixtures that now explicitly call `note_proposal_seen(...)` when they intend to model proposal-owned state). Runtime outcome is mixed but useful:
- `/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_proposal_owned_commit_20260315T113400Z.log` still timed out before `target_blocks=120`, at quorum/strict `68/76`;
- `/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_proposal_owned_commit_keepdirs_20260315T114000Z.log` reran the same scenario with preserved peer logs and stalled earlier at quorum/strict `57/64`, preserving `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_QgNYCr`;
- the important structural change held in preserved peer stdout: `no proposal observed for view before changing view` still appears at the failing height (`57`), but those records now carry `commit_inflight=None`, so the proposal-less active-commit path is no longer the source of the rotation.
What remains open after this tranche is narrower and more concrete: healthy NPoS still stalls under repeated vote-backed quorum reschedules (`votes=1` or `2` out of `3`) plus later missing-QC churn. The next simplification should target reschedule ownership for late vote-backed frontier candidates and the remaining zero-vote `drop_pending=true` cases in the reschedule sweep, not add more timeout stages or reintroduce payload-owned slot state.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 BlockCreated proposal-owned round state): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/proposal_handlers.rs` so payload-only `BlockCreated` no longer owns proposal observation or round/view advancement. `CollectDa` phase is now recorded only after the slot was already observed through `Proposal` handling, which removes payload delivery as an independent round-state machine while keeping pending payload availability intact. Focused regressions are green (`block_created_without_cached_proposal_does_not_advance_active_view`, `block_created_with_matching_proposal_advances_active_view`, `block_created_records_collect_da_phase_after_proposal_seen`, `block_created_without_hint_accepts_extending_lock`, `block_created_uses_cached_proposal_when_lock_missing`). A fresh healthy NPoS probe is already running on this cut (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_block_created_state_reduction_20260315T000000Z.log`) but is still in the release-build warmup stage. Remaining open for this tranche: wait for that probe verdict, then decide whether the next state-reduction cut should stay in `BlockCreated`/proposal ownership or move further upstream into proposal assembly if the same `65-71` plateau persists.

Latest sync (2026-03-15 zero-vote quorum-reschedule rotation removal + aborted-pending revive restore): reverted the regressed aborted-pending non-revive experiment in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{main_loop.rs,commit.rs,proposal_handlers.rs,tests.rs}` and restored the earlier commit-QC / duplicate-`BlockCreated` revive semantics. Then simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{reschedule.rs,commit.rs}` so `reschedule_pending_quorum_block(...)` no longer returns or triggers an immediate view change; zero-vote quorum reschedules may still drop/requeue stale frontier state, but post-reschedule rotation is now owned only by the frontier timeout controller. Targeted regressions are green (`commit_qc_revives_aborted_pending_block`, `block_created_revives_aborted_pending`, `stake_quorum_timeout_reschedules_without_immediate_view_change`, `zero_vote_quorum_timeout_drops_pending_without_immediate_view_change`, `reschedule_defers_zero_vote_quorum_timeout_while_block_queue_backlogged`). Runtime signal is informative but still insufficient:
- the reverted commit-QC experiment recovered from the sharper regression (`66/73`, `65/69`) back to the prior early-stall envelope (`69/73`);
- a live-inspection probe (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_snapshot_20260315T063717Z.log`) showed the recurring bad path is repeated `commit quorum missing past timeout` reschedules at ordinary frontier heights (`3`, `7`, `15`, `21`, `24`, `33`, `40`), including zero-vote cases with `drop_pending=true` and the old `rotate_immediately=true` behavior;
- removing that direct rotation only improved the healthy NPoS probe marginally (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_no_resched_rotate_20260315T064146Z.log` stalled at `66/71`), so the remaining dominant stall is upstream of the reschedule-owned rotation.
Remaining open for this tranche: keep the reschedule simplification, but shift the next investigation to why ordinary frontier proposals reach zero-vote quorum reschedule so frequently in healthy NPoS runs. Focus on proposal assembly / block issuance / early frontier drop paths before resuming the 60m full soak matrix. The acceptance gate is still unchanged: quorum interval `p95 < 1000ms`, no `600s` no-progress abort, no persistent fixed-height loop, and full-duration completion.

Latest sync (2026-03-14 pending-frontier timeout simplification): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{reschedule.rs,commit.rs,main_loop.rs}` so vote-backed quorum reschedules no longer own an immediate rotation and the remaining `maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending(...)` path now uses one bounded long grace for non-fast-path frontier pending blocks (`max(backlog_timeout, 2 * recovery_deferred_qc_ttl)`) instead of the previous short backlog timeout. Targeted regressions are green (`maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending_*`, `stake_quorum_timeout_reschedules_without_immediate_view_change`, `zero_vote_quorum_timeout_reschedules_and_records_missing_qc_view_change`). Runtime signal is mixed but informative:
- baseline short healthy NPoS probe before the long frontier-pending timeout (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_vote_backed_resched_20260314T212202Z.log`) reached `target_blocks=260` but still showed the old `reschedule -> stalled_pending view change -> revived aborted pending block after commit QC` pattern and failed `p95` at `2501ms`;
- an intermediate narrow deferred-QC gate regressed to a fixed-height stall at `height 94` (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_deferred_qc_ttl_20260314T213707Z.log`);
- the current simplified non-fast-path timeout probe (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_probe_frontier_pending_timeout_20260314T214737Z.log`) reached `target_blocks=120` at `height 123` without replaying the old `68-94` wedge, but still failed the latency gate at `p95=2501ms` and still showed at least one later late-QC revive (height `102`).
Remaining open for this tranche: treat the early wedge shape as improved but not fixed. The next step is to quantify the residual late revive path after height `100` and determine whether it now comes from zero-vote `drop_pending` rotation, a committed-edge cleanup, or another non-frontier path. Do not resume the 60m full matrix yet; first extend the healthy NPoS probe depth on the current binary past the `120`-block target and profile which path is still creating the `2501ms` latency plateau.

Latest sync (2026-03-14 frontier cleanup NEW_VIEW reset): tightened `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` so `apply_frontier_recovery_cleanup(...)` now clears `new_view_tracker` entries, `forced_view_after_timeout` markers, and `Phase::NewView` vote history at/above the contiguous frontier; committed-edge cleanup in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` now reuses the same vote-history clearing helper. Follow-up cleanup in the same tranche now makes RBC purge paths state-owning even without a live RBC session object: `purge_rbc_sessions_at_or_above_height(...)` and `clean_rbc_sessions_for_block(...)` now purge orphan `da_rbc.rbc.pending`, rebroadcast cooldown, persisted, and seed-inflight state keyed by the same RBC session key. Targeted regressions are green (`frontier_recovery_cleanup_*`, `observe_new_view_highest_qc_suppression_*`, `clean_rbc_sessions_for_block_clears_seed_inflight`, `frontier_recovery_cleanup_purges_orphan_pending_rbc_without_sessions`). The new 15-minute NPoS healthy canary (`/tmp/izanami_npos_healthy_canary_frontier_cleanup_new_view_reset_20260314T204030Z.log`) reached `target_blocks=400` and stopped at height `406` with no fixed-height wedge, no strict/quorum divergence, and no late `293/294` stall, but it still failed the latency hard gate (`interval_p50=1429ms`, `interval_p95=2501ms`). This turns the open problem from consensus-stall recovery into latency reduction and cleanup of residual backlog state.

Latest sync (2026-03-14 frontier follow-up): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/rbc.rs` so far-future RBC recovery no longer creates per-height missing-block state and instead reanchors the contiguous frontier (`reason = "rbc_far_future_missing_block"`); removed the old non-roster-only forced RBC delivery special case from `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/block_sync.rs`; and tightened committed-edge conflict cleanup in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` so it now clears frontier/future `Phase::NewView` votes from `vote_log` in addition to clamping the frontier round and clearing NEW_VIEW tracker state. Targeted regressions are green (`future_consensus_window_rbc_messages_reanchor_frontier_without_far_height_requests`, `request_missing_block_for_pending_rbc_far_future_*`, `recover_block_from_rbc_session_far_future_reanchors_contiguous_frontier`, `observe_new_view_highest_qc_suppression_*`, `block_sync_update_known_block_*`, `clean_rbc_sessions_for_block_clears_seed_inflight`). The far-future RBC clamp canary failed at `height 265`, and the committed-edge NEW_VIEW vote cleanup follow-up then failed at `height 292`; both of those wedge points are now superseded by the frontier-cleanup NEW_VIEW reset build, which no longer stalls before target height.

Latest sync (2026-03-14 Sumeragi frontier-recovery simplification): replaced the timeout-driven same-height/frontier recovery stack in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` with one frontier-scoped controller (`CatchUp` -> `RotateArmed`) and routed committed-edge conflict recovery in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` through the same controller. The contiguous frontier (`committed + 1`) is now the only timeout-recovery owner; repeated no-progress windows produce one bounded catch-up reanchor, then one cleanup + all-peer reanchor, then one bounded rotation on the next window if progress still does not resume. The public status surface stays intact (`consensus_no_proposal_storm_*`, `blocksync_range_pull_expiry_streak_*`), but the old same-height timeout choreography is no longer authoritative. Targeted `iroha_core` regressions are green (`frontier_recovery_*`, `same_height_no_proposal_storm_breaker_*`, `same_height_no_proposal_storm_counter_persists_until_true_progress`, `repeated_range_pull_expiry_forces_tier_jump_cooldown_clear_and_reanchor`, `committed_edge_conflict_*`).

Latest sync (2026-03-14 unknown-prev fallback cap validation): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs` so unknown-prev fallback rewind keeps short-chain half-window behavior but caps mid/deep rewind to the existing 64-block recent-hash hint window. Targeted `iroha_core --lib unknown_prev_` regressions are green. A 15-minute NPoS healthy canary with this kept variant improved the previous plateau from `height 345` to `height 384` and delayed fallback-anchor churn until the final minutes, but it still missed `target_blocks=400` and still violated the latency gate envelope (`median 1449ms`, `p95 2501ms`). A follow-up variant that clamped repeated incremental fallback sharing directly to the committed edge regressed badly to an earlier `height 280` stall and was reverted.
Remaining open for this tranche: turn the canary stability win into acceptance-gate compliance. The orphan-`rbc.pending` cleanup regression and patch are now in place; rerun the 15m NPoS healthy canary on this newest tree to determine whether the latency envelope changes or whether the remaining `p95=2501ms` issue is independent of residual backlog state. If latency remains flat, shift the investigation to proposal cadence / block production pacing rather than recovery churn, then resume the 60m full soak matrix. Acceptance gates remain unchanged: quorum interval `p95 < 1000ms`, no `600s` no-progress abort, no persistent fixed-height frontier loop, and full-duration completion. Frozen-peer scenarios still need the separate `trusted_peers_pop` harness/config generation issue corrected before those soak results can be treated as signal.

Latest sync (2026-03-13 timeout-driven same-height recovery stabilization execution): completed the bounded suppression/escalation tranche in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/status.rs`, and `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs`. This includes dedicated per-height same-height no-proposal storm tracking (independent from timeout-marker cleanup), storm-count-driven breaker behavior, forced-cleanup post-window single-rotation allowance, persisted per-height range-pull expiry streak continuity, repeated no-progress expiry tier jump + cooldown clear + canonical reanchor emission, committed-edge storm-break gating at forced-escalation threshold, and new storm diagnostic status fields (`last_height`, `last_count`, `max_count`). Also fixed a regression where deterministic frontier reset could clear same-height state before suppression checks by preserving in-window canonical frontier reanchor suppression for that timeout cycle unless explicit post-cleanup rotation allowance is active. Targeted regressions are green (`missing_qc_view_change_suppressed_when_frontier_reanchor_already_emitted`, `highest_qc_fetch_suppresses_committed_height_hash_conflict`, `same_height_no_proposal_storm*`, `repeated_range_pull_expiry_forces_tier_jump_cooldown_clear_and_reanchor`, and `strict_progress_timeout_enforcement_respects_bft_tolerance`).
Remaining open for this tranche: execute the full soak validation matrix (15m canary + 60m full for permissioned/NPoS healthy and +1 frozen) and confirm acceptance gates: quorum interval `p95 < 1000ms`, no `600s` no-progress abort, no persistent fixed-height same-height rotation loop, and full-duration completion (`target_blocks` KPI-only).

Latest sync (2026-03-11 fresh full 60-minute replay on current tree): reran both stable envelopes with unchanged flags and `--progress-timeout 600s`. Permissioned command: `cargo run -p izanami --release --locked -- --allow-net --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 600s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable` produced `/tmp/izanami_permissioned_full_soak_20260311T20260311T180117_escalated.log` and network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_1VF8vL`; NPoS command (same + `--nexus`) produced `/tmp/izanami_npos_full_soak_20260311T20260311T192506_escalated.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_IZ1iy1`. Both completed duration with no `600s` no-progress abort and zero ingress failover/unhealthy totals (`permissioned successes=17998`, `NPoS successes=18000`), but persisted final heights still show one lagging peer per mode (permissioned `1414/1414/1414/569`; NPoS core lane `959/959/959/184`). Churn remains fixed-height lag amplification (`no_proposal_cutoff`, fallback-anchor replay, sidecar mismatch ignores), while committed-edge suppression remains bounded and non-dominant (`permissioned=5`, `NPoS=10`).
Remaining open for this tranche: retain duration-first liveness success and actionable-dependency behavior, but close strict-vs-quorum convergence under one-peer lag (reduce prolonged fixed-height fallback-anchor/no-proposal churn, especially NPoS sidecar-mismatch-heavy loops) so a single freezing peer does not depress strict pace this severely.

Latest sync (2026-03-08 roster-unavailability FSM state collapse pass): simplified `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` again by removing `AcquireDependencies` from the roster-recovery FSM and treating dependency reacquire as stateless side-effects only. The roster-recovery reducer now uses a smaller finite transition set: `Steady -> ReelectRoster -> (RotateView | WaitCandidates)`, plus `WaitCandidates -> ReelectRoster` on candidate availability and `WaitCandidates -> Steady` on round advance. Event alphabet reduced to `RosterUnavailable`, `CandidatesAvailable`, `CandidatesEmpty`, `RoundAdvanced`; recovery entry dependency-tracking fields were removed; transition/recovery tests were updated in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`. Follow-up simplification: `handle_roster_unavailable_recovery(...)` now always reports handled progress (including empty `WaitCandidates`) to avoid external callers spinning on false no-progress while recovery is intentionally waiting. Validation in this tree: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib roster_recovery_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib roster_unavailable_recovery_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib deterministic_roster_election_is_order_invariant_for_permissioned_and_npos -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib roster_unavailability_candidate_source_matches_consensus_mode -- --nocapture` (ok).

Latest sync (2026-03-08 roster-unavailability FSM simplification + hang fix): simplified the roster-unavailability reducer/runtime path in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` by removing escalation/noop branches (`EscalateEpoch`, `CandidatesNoop`, `EscalationReady`) and using direct finite transitions (`DependencyTimeout -> RotateView`, `ReelectRoster + CandidatesAvailable -> RotateView`). Latest sync (2026-03-04 tranche-7C h=178/179 stall-loop closure): implemented coordinated core+ingress damping in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`, and `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` without CLI/config-schema/metric-name changes. Core now keeps frontier stall mode across transient unresolved/gap blips (including transient no-target windows), applies deterministic adaptive frontier reanchor stride (`1/2/4/8` windows by no-progress duration), unifies frontier-equivalent reanchor emission via shared canonical gate keyed by `(frontier_height, canonical_height, window_index)`, suppresses same-window missing-QC rotation when in-window frontier reanchor already emitted without dependency progress, couples committed-edge sidecar mismatch to one bounded per-window conflict bundle, and tightens deferred-QC far-ahead replay boundary to `local_height_at_entry + 1` under active frontier stall. Ingress now consumes an internal lag snapshot (`quorum_min_height` + per-endpoint sampled heights), excludes lagging endpoints from normal ordering while healthy non-lagging options exist, hardens queue-pressure sticky cooldown with bounded exponential backoff, and keeps forced-probe dead-end escape by probing excluded lagging endpoints when normal candidates are exhausted. Targeted tranche tests are green in `iroha_core` and `izanami` (including new regressions for transient frontier hysteresis, stride cadence, shared window gate, missing-QC suppression, committed-edge conflict bundle, far-ahead replay boundary, lagging-endpoint exclusion, forced-probe fallback, and sticky queue-pressure cooldown). Latest sync (2026-03-02 tranche-5 single-peer frontier stall escape): landed internal lock-lag stall damping in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` with no CLI/config/telemetry-schema changes: per-frontier stall-state tracking (3 no-progress windows to activate), stalled prune/reanchor throttling to one cycle per stall window, deterministic stalled reanchor peer selection (2-peer rotating cohort with every-3rd-window all-peer sweep), and widened stalled lock-lag reanchor dedup floor (`max(existing, 2 * missing_block_height_ttl)`). Also landed contiguous far-future missing-parent suppression (`frontier_gap_realign` reanchor) and DA quorum-window adjustment to `block_time + 3 * commit_time` (multiplier semantics unchanged), plus shared-host stable soak profile tightening in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs`. Focused regressions and targeted suites are green (`lock_lag_frontier_stall_mode_*`, `lock_lag_prune_`, `range_pull_lock_lag_`, `missing_block_height_hard_cap_`, `request_missing_parent_*`, `commit_quorum_timeout_*`, `izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_`). Immediate open work: run the unchanged 3600s soak envelope against tranche-4 baseline (`/tmp/izanami_tranche4_soak_20260302T122520.log`) and report acceptance-gate deltas (`strict_min_height`, strict/quorum gap, frontier-536 `lock_lag_prune_warn_total`, `lock_lag_future_prune` range-pull volume, and `missing_qc` view-change drift).

Latest sync (2026-03-02 tranche-4 backoff-true hash-miss accounting): landed recovery bookkeeping reclassification in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop{.rs,/qc.rs,/votes.rs}` so `MissingBlockFetchDecision::Backoff` no longer increments hash-miss streaks (new helper `note_missing_block_height_backoff(...)` tracks stage/last-seen only). Added focused regressions in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs` (`missing_block_backoff_does_not_increment_hash_miss_or_attempt_streak`, `missing_block_backoff_waits_for_no_progress_window_before_escalation`, `missing_block_attempt_streak_escalates_with_no_target_kind`) and validated with targeted `cargo test -p iroha_core ...` runs (all green). Immediate open work: run the unchanged 3600s soak envelope and quantify whether `missing_block_hash_miss_streak`-driven range pulls and corresponding churn/throughput penalties decrease versus the 2026-03-02 baseline artifacts.

Latest sync (2026-03-02 tranche-3 full-soak rerun): executed a fresh unchanged-envelope soak (`/tmp/izanami_tranche3_soak_rerun_20260302T003248.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_elnrAh`) and reached strict/quorum `1459/1459` before duration expiry (max gap `2`, `successes=7082`, `failures=8`, ingress unhealthy `2`). This confirms the prior hard divergence mode is substantially reduced, but target-block throughput is still below gate (`1459/3600`) with dominant RBC readiness churn (`deferring RBC DELIVER`/`deferring local RBC READY`/`missing BlockCreated while awaiting RBC INIT` all high across peers, including terminal heights `1458-1462`). Immediate open work: reduce RBC READY/DELIVER completion latency under shared-host stable profile without changing safety rules (prioritize deterministic READY quorum convergence and duplicate-targeted payload suppression at terminal heights), then rerun the unchanged 3600s envelope to verify higher strict-height slope.

Latest sync (2026-03-01 tranche-3 full soak bottleneck readout): executed unchanged-envelope acceptance soak (`/tmp/izanami_tranche3_soak_20260301T231415.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_fb0Z20`) and still stopped before target (`successes=3238`, `failures=127`, strict `496` vs quorum `803`, max gap `307`). Tranche-3 objective was partially met: split-frontier lock-lag thrash is collapsed (lock-lag future-prune concentrated at frontier height `497`), but dominant remaining bottlenecks are now (1) single-peer frontier stall persistence, (2) missing-QC/reacquire churn on advancing peers (`missing_qc=266`, payload reacquire `111`), and (3) ingress endpoint instability (`endpoint unhealthy=336`, `Connection refused` bursts). Immediate open work: add deterministic single-peer frontier-stall escape logic without safety-rule changes (frontier-local reanchor/backoff mode after sustained no-progress windows), tighten missing-QC storm damping around post-frontier heights, and isolate/fix late-run ingress endpoint refusal path so throughput remains stable through the full 3600s envelope.

Latest sync (2026-03-01 tranche-3 lock-lag frontier-coalesced recovery): landed balanced-damping anti-thrash changes in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` and `tests.rs`: lock-lag canonical dedup bucket coalescing for range pulls, lock-lag in-flight suppression of hash-miss/attempt re-escalation until a no-progress window elapses, distance-aware lock-lag hash-miss cap widening (near/far frontier), and far-future lock-lag `2x TTL` cooldown floor. Added focused regressions (`range_pull_lock_lag_coalesces_far_future_height_to_frontier_bucket`, `missing_block_hash_miss_under_lock_lag_inflight_waits_for_no_progress_window`, `missing_block_attempt_streak_under_lock_lag_inflight_waits_for_no_progress_window`, `effective_hash_miss_cap_under_lock_lag_scales_by_frontier_distance`, `range_pull_lock_lag_far_future_uses_extended_cooldown`) plus the listed safety regressions; targeted `iroha_core --lib` checks are green. Immediate open work: run the unchanged-envelope soak comparison against tranche-2 artifacts and report strict-min advancement, `missing_block_hash_miss_streak` range-pull reduction, 581/785 split-height collapse behavior, and secondary `missing_qc` deltas.

Latest sync (2026-03-01 lock-lag bottleneck hardening): landed lock-lag-aware highest-QC defer/recovery behavior in `iroha_core` so proposals/hints no longer eagerly advance `highest_qc` when ancestry to `locked_qc` is unresolved; added lock-lag future-state pruning + reanchor range-pull hooks in stalled-pending and idle missing-QC paths; and retargeted idle missing-QC range-pulls to lock catch-up height with a wider lock-lag cooldown floor. New focused regressions are in place (`proposal_*_defers_highest_qc_update_when_lock_lag_catchup_unresolved`, `lock_lag_prune_prunes_far_future_state_and_reanchors_range_pull`, `idle_missing_qc_reacquire_*`). Immediate open work is unchanged at gate level: run unchanged-envelope acceptance soak comparisons on top of this baseline and quantify whether the dominant single-height storm concentration and progress-timeout behavior improve versus v22r3 artifacts.

Latest sync (2026-03-01 missing-QC split-vote root-cause fix): landed the vote-domain correction in `iroha_core` so commit vote conflicts/evidence stay scoped to `(phase,height,view,epoch,signer)` rather than freezing a signer per height across views. New regression coverage for local precommit emission, inbound vote validation, and split-view convergence behavior is in place (`emit_precommit_vote_allows_higher_view_different_block_same_height_when_lock_extends`, `validate_and_record_vote_accepts_cross_view_commit_vote_for_same_signer`, `validate_and_record_vote_rejects_same_view_conflicting_commit_vote_and_records_evidence`, `split_view_commit_votes_accept_higher_view_and_form_qc`). Focused tests and `izanami` stable soak profile are green; broad `iroha_core main_loop` remains order-sensitive/flaky in this tree. Open work is unchanged at the gate level: run unchanged-envelope soak comparisons on top of this baseline and, if stalls persist, proceed to proposal/reassembly heuristics as the next tranche while keeping this vote-domain correction.

Latest sync (2026-03-01 missing-QC storm follow-up v2.2 full-soak rerun): executed a fresh unchanged-envelope acceptance soak with explicit capture (`/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_v22r3_20260301T091543Z.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_ITQMYW`) and hit progress-timeout at strict `605` (`successes=2850`, `failures=34`, ingress unhealthy `96`). Compared to the latest 2026-02-26 baseline artifacts (`630/1535`, payload-missing `8/56`, forced-VC `1/1`, fallback `19/48`), churn/stall indicators improved and no progress-timeout/view-change-storm signal appeared; remaining open is throughput gap to gate target heights under the fixed `--tps 5 --max-inflight 8` envelope.

Latest sync (2026-02-27 policy-only consensus liveness refinements): applied additional churn controls in `iroha_core` without FSM/public-config changes (unknown-prev repeat-aware incremental fallback sharing in `block_sync.rs`, in-flight fetch-aware vote-rebroadcast suppression in `main_loop/qc.rs`, and bounded pre-timeout near-quorum recovery escalation in `main_loop/reschedule.rs` + `main_loop.rs`). Focused `iroha_core` + `izanami` tests are green and shared-host profile constants remain pinned (`tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`, DA multipliers `4/3`, floor `2000ms`). Remaining open: execute fresh unchanged-shape 1200s/3600s A/B gate reruns for this exact patch set and confirm reduced 6s-class timeout stalls/fallback churn while preserving no-progress-timeout and no view-change-storm behavior.

Latest sync (offline certificate draft operator derivation, 2026-03-03): Torii certificate issue/renew signing now derives the emitted certificate `operator` account from the configured offline-issuer keypair plus controller domain, and no longer depends on a client-supplied draft `operator`. Draft `operator` remains optional/ignored for backward compatibility in Torii. SDK request encoders were aligned so Swift/Android/JS `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft` payloads omit `operator` by default, with signed-certificate payloads unchanged. Validation: `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && ... ./gradlew :android:test` (ok), JS syntax checks (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (pk-deploy App Attest capture-quality gating + archive scanner, 2026-03-04): tightened fixture automation by extending `scripts/offline_topup/make_pkdeploy_app_attest_settlement_fixture.sh` with capture diagnostics and `--require-real-device` fail-fast mode, and added `scripts/offline_topup/scan_pkdeploy_app_attest_payloads.sh` to inventory/classify archived payloads (`real_device_candidate|placeholder|...`) across pk-deploy roots. The generator now emits fixture provenance + quality metadata and hard-fails in strict mode when simulator placeholders are selected. Validation: generator pass (`attestation_capture_quality=placeholder`) and strict expected fail on `~/.pk-deploy/runs/offline-offline-e2e/20260220_114954-offline-offline`; scanner pass (`total_files=163`, `apple_app_attest_payloads=134`, `real_device_candidates=0`, `placeholder_candidates=134`) and strict expected fail (`--require-real-device`); ignored Torii replay tests still pass with generated fixture. Remaining open for this tranche: no commit-safe real-device App Attest settlement capture exists in current pk-deploy archives, so strict signature-parity regression (`platform_signature_invalid` from true App Attest chain/assertion evidence) is still blocked on a fresh real-device capture.

Latest sync (pk-deploy App Attest settlement fixture automation, 2026-03-04): closed the local fixture-bootstrapping gap by adding `scripts/offline_topup/make_pkdeploy_app_attest_settlement_fixture.sh` and wiring docs in `fixtures/offline_allowance/README.md`. The helper auto-discovers `ios_send_to_android` payload + Android receiver account/seed from a pk-deploy run directory, decodes the archived payload via `offline_bundle_decode --kind receipt`, derives the authority private key from archived seed material (`802620<seed_hex>`), and writes a Torii replay fixture suitable for `IROHA_APPLE_ATTEST_REAL_DEVICE_SETTLEMENT_FIXTURE` (default `/tmp/iroha_app_attest_real_device_settlement_fixture.from_pkdeploy.json`). Validation: fixture generation command passed on `~/.pk-deploy/runs/offline-offline-e2e/20260220_114954-offline-offline`; ignored replay tests `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_real_device_fixture_replay_respects_strict_signature_policy` and `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_real_device_fixture_replay_persists_final_status_and_reason` both passed with the generated fixture; cross-SDK tx-status/error contract spot checks also passed (Java harness, Swift fixture test, JS fixture test pattern). Remaining open for this tranche: archived pk-deploy iOS payloads still contain `legacy-offline-attestation` placeholders, so strict real-device signature-parity (`platform_signature_invalid` from true App Attest chain/assertion material) still requires a commit-safe redacted capture beyond archived simulator fixtures.

Latest sync (cross-SDK case-insensitive tx-status error-key extraction, 2026-03-04): closed the remaining mixed-case payload/header drift by extending the shared fixture contract (`fixtures/sdk/tx_status_error_message_contract.json`) with a mixed-case key/header case (`Error.Detail` + `X-IrOhA-ReJeCt-CoDe`) and updating SDK extractors/tests accordingly. JavaScript now performs case-insensitive candidate-key lookup in `_extractErrorMessageValue(...)` and fixture tests honor optional `reject_code_header_name`; Swift `ToriiClient.extractErrorMessage(from:)` now resolves candidate keys case-insensitively and fixture tests honor `reject_code_header_name`; Android `HttpErrorMessageExtractor` now resolves structured message keys case-insensitively and `HttpClientTransportStatusTests` uses fixture-provided reject header names. Latest sync (cross-SDK tx-status error contract + Android offline parity, 2026-03-04): completed the follow-up hardening requested for status/error setup ergonomics and drift prevention. Added shared Android utility `java/iroha_android/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/HttpErrorMessageExtractor.java` and wired both `HttpClientTransport` and `OfflineToriiClient` to use the same case-insensitive reject-header extraction plus structured error-message extraction (nested `message`/`error`/`errors`/`detail`/`details`/`reason`/`rejection_reason`/`description`), compact sorted JSON fallback, and ASCII truncation. Added cross-SDK contract fixture `fixtures/sdk/tx_status_error_message_contract.json` and fixture-driven validation in Swift (`IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiClientTests.swift`), Android (`java/iroha_android/src/test/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/HttpClientTransportStatusTests.java`), and JavaScript (`javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js`) to lock precedence behavior and prevent future drift. Also expanded Android offline non-2xx tests for nested/compact message surfacing and mixed-case `X-Iroha-Reject-Code` propagation (`OfflineToriiClientTests`). Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiClientTests/testGetTransactionStatus` (ok; `10 passed`), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/openjdk@21/21.0.10/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.HttpClientTransportStatusTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test --test-name-pattern "getTransactionStatus|waitForTransactionStatus|submitOfflineSettlementAndWait" javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js` (ok; `34 passed`), and `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Latest sync (Android/JS tx-status error-message extraction parity, 2026-03-04): completed the remaining non-Swift tx-status diagnostics gap by aligning Android and JavaScript HTTP error message extraction with the Swift behavior. Android `HttpClientTransport` now extracts status error text from structured JSON payloads (including nested objects and `errors[]` arrays) before falling back to truncated plain text, and `HttpClientTransportStatusTests` now covers nested JSON (`error.detail`), `errors[]` extraction, and oversized-body truncation (`...`). JavaScript `ToriiClient` now recursively extracts HTTP error messages from nested/array payloads (`message`/`error`/`errors`/`detail`/`details`/`reason`/`rejection_reason`/`description`), falls back to compact sorted JSON when no explicit message exists, and truncates oversized fallback text. Added tx-status regressions in `javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js` for nested JSON, `errors[]`, compact JSON fallback, and truncation; rebuilt `javascript/iroha_js/dist/toriiClient.js`. Validation: `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test --test-name-pattern "getTransactionStatus|waitForTransactionStatus|submitOfflineSettlementAndWait" javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js` (ok; `33 passed`, `0 failed`), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/openjdk@21/21.0.10/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.HttpClientTransportStatusTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/openjdk@21/21.0.10/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests,org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.HttpClientTransportStatusTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks` (ok), and `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiClientTests/testGetTransactionStatusHttpError` (ok; `6 passed`). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Swift non-2xx body-message sweep for tx/status surfaces, 2026-03-03): completed the follow-up consistency pass on Swift Torii error reporting so status/transaction failures surface server body context across all touched async paths, not just the initial tx-status subset. In `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift`, response-body-aware `ensureStatus(..., responseBody:)` is now used for account onboarding, subscription/connect reads/mutations, attachment/prover delete flows, DA ingest submit, contract code registration, health/metrics, runtime upgrade mutation endpoints, and governance JSON helpers. This ensures `ToriiClientError.httpStatus` carries actionable message text (JSON `message`/`error`/`detail`/`reason`/`rejection_reason`, UTF-8 fallback) together with `x-iroha-reject-code` when present. Latest sync (Swift tx-status error-message body surfacing, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining operator-debugging gap in Swift transaction status flows by threading HTTP response bodies into status validation. `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift` now derives `ToriiClientError.httpStatus(..., message, ...)` from JSON/body content (`message`/`error`/`detail`/`reason`/`rejection_reason`, UTF-8 fallback) instead of only `HTTPURLResponse.localizedString(...)`, and applies this in `data(for:)` plus direct tx-status paths (`submitTransaction`, `getTransactionStatus`, `getPipelineRecovery`, `getTimeNow`, `getTimeStatus`). Added regression `testGetTransactionStatusHttpErrorSurfacesBodyMessageAndRejectCode` and updated `testSubmitTransactionRejectCodeHeaderSurfaced` expectations to assert body-derived text. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiClientTests/testSubmitTransactionRejectCodeHeaderSurfaced` (ok; `1 passed`), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiClientTests/testGetTransactionStatusHttpErrorSurfacesBodyMessageAndRejectCode` (ok; `1 passed`), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `27 passed`), `cd java/iroha_android && ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.HttpClientTransportStatusTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test --test-name-pattern "getTransactionStatus|waitForTransactionStatus|submitOfflineSettlementAndWait" javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js` (ok; `29 passed`). Latest sync (Swift cancellation + tx-status/error-message reruns, 2026-03-03): normalized Swift transport cancellation semantics in `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift` so `URLError.cancelled` / `NSURLErrorCancelled` from `URLSession.data(for:)` are rethrown as `CancellationError` (instead of `ToriiClientError.transport`), restoring end-to-end cancellation behavior for `waitForTransactionStatus(...)` and `submitOfflineSettlementAndWait(...)`. Added explicit regression coverage `testGetTransactionStatusMapsCancelledTransportToCancellationError` in `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiClientTests.swift` to lock this mapping. Revalidated status/reject diagnostics across surfaces with focused suites: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiClientTests/testGetTransactionStatusMapsCancelledTransportToCancellationError` (ok; `1 passed`), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `27 passed`), `cd java/iroha_android && ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.HttpClientTransportStatusTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `25 passed`, `2 ignored`), and `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `7 passed`). Latest sync (Torii real-device replay final settlement-status assertions, 2026-03-03): extended `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs` replay harness helpers so real-device fixture replays can keep the harness alive after submit, execute the queued `SubmitOfflineToOnlineTransfer`, and assert persisted `/v1/offline/settlements/query` outcomes (`status`, `rejection_reason`, nested `transfer.rejection_reason`). Added ignored test `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_real_device_fixture_replay_persists_final_status_and_reason`, which closes the previous tx-status visibility gap where replay checks stopped at submit-time `Queued` and never validated final strict/non-strict settlement outcomes. Latest sync (JS tx-status cancellation parity, 2026-03-03): completed the remaining JavaScript polling-control gap by extending `TransactionStatusPollOptions` with `signal`, forwarding cancellation through `ToriiClient.waitForTransactionStatus(...)` -> `getTransactionStatus(...)` request loops (including sleep wait abort), and ensuring `submitOfflineSettlementAndWait(...)` passes its caller signal into pipeline polling. Also tightened `getTransactionStatus(...)` option handling to reject unsupported fields and non-boolean `allowShortHash` values, so tx-status setup errors fail fast instead of silently ignoring typos. Added runtime validation tests for invalid signal/option shapes, signal-forwarding tests for both `getTransactionStatus(...)` and `waitForTransactionStatus(...)`, and updated submit+wait delegation expectations so callers can cancel end-to-end with a single controller. Validation: `cd javascript/iroha_js && IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test --test-name-pattern "getTransactionStatus|waitForTransactionStatus|submitOfflineSettlementAndWait" test/toriiClient.test.js` (ok; `29 passed`, `0 failed`) and `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (offline settlement submit+poll helper parity, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining mobile setup friction for settlement status checks by adding one-call submit+poll helpers across SDKs. Android `OfflineToriiClient` now exposes `submitSettlementAndWait(...)` overloads that compose settlement submit with `IrohaClient.waitForTransactionStatus(...)` and include default-options overloads when callers do not need custom polling config; JavaScript `ToriiClient` now exposes `submitOfflineSettlementAndWait(...)` with combined `{ signal + TransactionStatusPollOptions }` validation and explicit failure when settlement responses omit `transaction_hash_hex`; Swift `ToriiClient` now decodes settlement `transaction_hash_hex`, adds `waitForTransactionStatus(...)`, and provides `submitOfflineSettlementAndWait(...)` (plus completion variant), surfacing existing `PipelineStatusError` rejection reasons and caching rejected bundle state after pipeline rejection so `getSettlementStatus(...)` returns `.rejected(reason:)` without waiting for server row visibility. Validation: `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `25 passed`), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "submitOfflineSettlementAndWait|submitOfflineSettlement posts transfer and parses response|submitOfflineSettlement accepts build claim overrides and repair flag|submitOfflineSettlement validates build claim override shape|waitForTransactionStatus surfaces rejection reason on failure status"` (ok; `607 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Also unblocked iOS simulator integration by fixing misplaced `ConnectViewModel` members and iOS-availability issues in `examples/ios/NoritoDemoXcode/NoritoDemoXcode/ContentView.swift`, then fixing XCTest host/testability wiring in `examples/ios/NoritoDemoXcode/NoritoDemoXcode.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` (`TEST_HOST` + `ENABLE_TESTABILITY=YES`) so simulator tests run successfully.
