Latest sync (Torii settlement tx-status + validation-error visibility checks, 2026-03-03): hardened offline settlement diagnostics in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs` by verifying that settlement `transaction_hash_hex` resolves through `/v1/pipeline/transactions/status` (`kind=Transaction`, immediate `Queued` state), adding unknown-hash pipeline status coverage that decodes Norito `ValidationFail` payloads and asserts query-style failure variants (`QueryFailed(NotFound|Conversion(_))`) with non-empty validation text, and tightening duplicate-bundle rejection coverage to require both `x-iroha-reject-code=duplicate_bundle` and decoded conversion-message content (`duplicate_bundle`) instead of header-only checks. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_returns_bundle_id_and_transaction_hash -- --nocapture` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api pipeline_transaction_status_unknown_hash_surfaces_not_found_error_payload -- --nocapture` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_rejects_duplicate_bundle_with_reject_code -- --nocapture` (ok), and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `24 passed`, `1 ignored`). Remaining open for this tranche: provide one commit-safe redacted real-device settlement fixture JSON and run the ignored strict/non-strict replay with these strengthened checks.

Latest sync (Swift/JS tx-status rejection diagnostics hardening, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining cross-SDK transaction failure-surface gap after Android improvements. Swift now decodes optional pipeline `rejection_reason` (`ToriiPipelineTransactionStatus.Status`) and `PipelineStatusError` now exposes `rejectionReason` plus reason-aware localized failure messages; regression coverage in `TxBuilderTests` verifies that rejected status polling preserves and surfaces the reason. JavaScript `TransactionStatusError` now extracts/stores `rejectionReason` from pipeline payloads (top-level/content/status aliases) and appends it to failure messages; typings/docs/dist were updated to include the new field and helper export (`extractPipelineRejectionReason`). Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter TxBuilderTests/testSubmitAndWaitAsyncFailureIncludesRejectionReason` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter TxBuilderTests/testSubmitAndWaitAsyncFailureThrows` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "waitForTransactionStatus rejects on failure status|waitForTransactionStatus surfaces rejection reason on failure status"` (ok; `605 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Android tx-status/error diagnostics hardening, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining Android operational-debugging gap for offline settlement failures by surfacing structured reject diagnostics end-to-end. `OfflineToriiException` now exposes optional `statusCode`, `rejectCode`, and `responseBody`; `OfflineToriiClient` now preserves `x-iroha-reject-code`, includes HTTP path/body details in failure messages, and attaches those structured fields on parse/non-2xx failures; `HttpClientTransport.waitForTransactionStatus(...)` now throws typed `TransactionStatusHttpException` for unexpected polling HTTP responses (hash/status/reject/body fields) and propagates parsed rejection reasons (including alias keys and rejected-status content fallback) through `TransactionStatusException.rejectionReason()` with enriched messages. Added regression coverage in `OfflineToriiClientTests` and `HttpClientTransportStatusTests`, plus README guidance for submit-and-wait error inspection. Validation: `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*HttpClientTransportStatusTests*"` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Torii replay tx-status/error-message assertion hardening, 2026-03-03): strengthened App Attest replay diagnostics in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs` so replay assertions now consume response bodies and verify transaction identity fields (`bundle_id_hex`, `transaction_hash_hex`) on successful settlement submissions, while rejection paths now require visible error payload content (`message`/`error`) in addition to HTTP status and `x-iroha-reject-code`. This closes the operational debugging gap where fixture replays could fail with only status/header mismatches and no surfaced error details. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_synthetic_fixture_replay_exercises_parser_and_submission_path -- --nocapture` (ok) and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `23 passed`, `1 ignored`). Remaining open for this tranche: obtain and commit one redacted real-device settlement fixture JSON so the ignored strict/non-strict replay can run with these stronger diagnostics.

Latest sync (offline build-claim typed response ergonomics, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining SDK usability gap after adding `issue` endpoints by introducing typed build-claim response models while preserving raw payload access. Swift now exposes `ToriiOfflineBuildClaim`, `ToriiOfflineBuildClaimPlatform`, and `ToriiOfflineBuildClaimIssueResponse.buildClaimObject()/decodeBuildClaim(...)`; Android now parses `build_claim` into new `OfflineBuildClaim` and exposes it via `OfflineBuildClaimIssueResponse.typedBuildClaim()` (raw `buildClaim()` map retained for compatibility); JavaScript now normalizes/validates `build_claim` into a structured shape (`claim_id`, `nonce`, canonical platform token, app/build/window/signature fields) and updates `index.d.ts` to type `ToriiOfflineBuildClaimIssueResponse.build_claim` accordingly. Updated SDK docs/examples and corrected test fixture hash-literal CRCs used by the new response validation paths. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `21 passed`), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "issueOfflineBuildClaim|submitOfflineSettlement|issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok; `604 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Torii synthetic App Attest replay coverage + Java HOME fallback docs, 2026-03-03): added a non-ignored regression `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_synthetic_fixture_replay_exercises_parser_and_submission_path` in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs` so the new fixture parser + replay submission path is continuously exercised without external real-device captures. The test writes a synthetic settlement fixture JSON, replays it through `/v1/offline/settlements` in strict/non-strict modes, and asserts fixture-driven outcomes, while the existing real-device replay test remains ignored for external capture data. Also updated `java/iroha_android/README.md` with an explicit Homebrew JDK21 `JAVA_HOME` fallback command for environments without `/usr/libexec/java_home`, matching the command validated on this host. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_synthetic_fixture_replay_exercises_parser_and_submission_path -- --nocapture` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `23 passed`, `1 ignored`), and `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME="$(brew --prefix openjdk@21)/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home" ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: capture and commit one redacted real-device settlement fixture JSON to exercise the ignored Torii strict/non-strict replay in manual/CI-adjacent runs.

Latest sync (offline build-claim client validation hardening, 2026-03-03): tightened SDK-side preflight validation for build-claim setup paths after adding direct issue endpoint wrappers. Swift now rejects unsupported build-claim platforms and invalid settlement override `tx_id_hex` values before network submission; Android `OfflineBuildClaimIssueRequest` and `OfflineSettlementBuildClaimOverride` now normalize/validate 32-byte hash IDs via `OfflineHashLiteral.parseHex` and enforce `platform ∈ {apple, android}`; JavaScript now enforces the same platform allowlist and normalizes build-claim IDs / settlement override tx IDs with `normalizeHashLike32` (accepting canonical hash literals or 32-byte hex, then emitting normalized hex). Updated SDK examples to use canonical 32-byte hex tx IDs for overrides, plus added regression tests covering invalid platform/invalid tx-id preflight rejection. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "issueOfflineBuildClaim|submitOfflineSettlement|issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (offline build-claim issue endpoint SDK parity, 2026-03-03): added first-class client helpers for `POST /v1/offline/build-claims/issue` across Swift (`ToriiClient.issueOfflineBuildClaim` + request/response models + endpoint/encoding tests), Android (`OfflineToriiClient.issueBuildClaim` + new `OfflineBuildClaimIssueRequest`/`OfflineBuildClaimIssueResponse` + parser/tests), and JavaScript (`ToriiClient.issueOfflineBuildClaim` + request/response normalization + typings/tests/docs). This closes the remaining mobile setup gap where clients needed operator-signed `OfflineBuildClaim` material before settlement but had no SDK wrapper for the existing Torii endpoint. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter OfflineToriiEncodingTests` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "issueOfflineBuildClaim|submitOfflineSettlement|issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Torii App Attest real-device replay harness + Android Java-path fix, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining execution gaps by adding an ignored Torii end-to-end replay test `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_real_device_fixture_replay_respects_strict_signature_policy` in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs`, plus a fixture parser/harness path that runs the same settlement capture with `apple_app_attest_strict_signature=false/true` and asserts expected acceptance/rejection code per mode. Also fixed local Android validation by using an explicit OpenJDK21 `JAVA_HOME` path instead of `/usr/libexec/java_home` on this host, and verified `OfflineToriiClientTests` via the Gradle harness filter (`ANDROID_HARNESS_MAINS=org.hyperledger.iroha.android.client.OfflineToriiClientTests ... ./gradlew :core:test --tests org.hyperledger.iroha.android.GradleHarnessTests --rerun-tasks`). Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `22 passed`, `1 ignored`) and the Android harness command above (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: capture and commit one redacted real-device settlement fixture JSON to exercise the new ignored Torii replay in CI-adjacent manual runs.

Latest sync (Swift offline App Attest nonce-forwarding + typed-draft metadata closure, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining Swift-side ergonomics gaps by making `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft.metadata` internal (raw string-key map no longer public) while exposing typed access through `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft.parsedMetadata()`, and by adding explicit `attestationNonce` forwarding on `ToriiClient.topUpOfflineAllowance(...)`, `ToriiClient.topUpOfflineAllowanceRenewal(...)`, `OfflineWallet.topUpAllowance(...)`, and `OfflineWallet.topUpAllowanceRenewal(...)` so iOS App Attest callers can pass top-up/renew challenge hashes directly into certificate `attestation_nonce`. Added regression coverage in `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests.swift` (nonce encoding on top-up and renewal, invalid nonce length rejection) plus wallet-level forwarding coverage in `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/OfflineWalletTopUpTests.swift`. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `18 passed`), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter OfflineWalletTopUpTests` (ok; `5 passed`), and `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter OfflineToriiEncodingTests` (ok; `5 passed`). Remaining open for this tranche: collect commit-safe redacted real-device App Attest fixtures for Torii strict/non-strict replay coverage.

Latest sync (Offline App Attest raw-challenge signature compatibility, 2026-03-03): closed the `platform_signature_invalid` follow-up for legacy iOS assertion flows by extending App Attest verification compatibility in `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs` to accept raw receipt-challenge-hash clientData/signature bindings when strict mode is disabled, while preserving strict-mode canonical enforcement behind `settlement.offline.apple_app_attest_strict_signature`. Added targeted regression coverage for both compatibility forms (`CompactRawReceiptHash` and `CborDerSignatureRawReceiptHash`) plus strict-mode rejection tests, and updated offline docs section 15 with both compatibility-path semantics. Also closed the missing regression for `settlement.offline.skip_build_claim_verification` by adding `offline_settlements_submit_accepts_missing_build_claim_when_verification_skipped` in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs` and documenting skip behavior in the offline allowance guide. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_core --lib apple_attestation -- --nocapture` (ok; `13 passed`, `1 ignored`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_core --lib app_attest_signature_compat_fixture_replays_and_respects_strict_mode -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-offline-compat cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_accepts_missing_build_claim_when_verification_skipped -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`). Remaining open for this tranche: keep collecting commit-safe redacted real-device fixtures for Torii end-to-end strict/non-strict replay coverage.

Latest sync (offline settlement build-claim override SDK parity, 2026-03-03): filled the SDK gap for `/v1/offline/settlements` override/repair flow by adding Swift request-model support (`build_claim_overrides[]`, `repair_existing_build_claims`) plus transfer convenience initializer wiring and request-encoding tests, adding Android `OfflineToriiClient.submitSettlement(...)` overloads with typed `OfflineSettlementBuildClaimOverride` plus harness coverage and README usage docs, and adding JavaScript `ToriiClient.submitOfflineSettlement(...)` with override/repair normalization and regression tests/docs. Validation: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter OfflineToriiEncodingTests` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "submitOfflineSettlement|issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Offline App Attest strict-signature + compat telemetry + fixture replay, 2026-03-03): closed the remaining App Attest follow-up gaps by adding strict signature-policy config (`settlement.offline.apple_app_attest_strict_signature`), wiring it through offline platform verification, and rejecting SHA256(clientDataHash) fallback signatures when strict mode is enabled. Added telemetry counter `iroha_offline_app_attest_signature_compat_total` for compatibility-path usage and a fixture-backed App Attest compatibility replay test (`fixtures/offline_allowance/ios-demo/app_attest_signature_compat.json`) plus strict-mode rejection coverage in `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs`. Updated offline allowance docs with nonce/challenge semantics and strict-mode operator guidance, plus explicit synthetic-fixture provenance/TODO notes in `fixtures/offline_allowance/README.md`. Added an ignored real-device replay harness test (`apple_attestation_real_device_fixture_replay_respects_strict_signature_policy`) that reads `IROHA_APPLE_ATTEST_REAL_DEVICE_FIXTURE`, with a companion template fixture (`fixtures/offline_allowance/ios-demo/app_attest_real_device_replay.template.json`) so operators can run strict/non-strict replays on redacted captures once available. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib app_attest_signature_compat_fixture_replays_and_respects_strict_mode -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib apple_attestation -- --nocapture` (ok; `9 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_config -- --nocapture` (ok; full crate pass), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `21 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `7 passed`), `swift test --filter OfflineWalletTopUpTests` (ok), `swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), and `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern \"issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance|offline\"` (ok; `597 passed`, `7 skipped`). Remaining open for this tranche: investigate active `platform_signature_invalid` reports from live iOS captures beyond the compatibility-signature path; full Torii strict-signature end-to-end parity still requires a commit-safe redacted real-device App Attest chain/assertion fixture (Torii integration tests cannot use the core unit-test root-override path).

Latest sync (offline certificate draft operator derivation + issuer rotation follow-up, 2026-03-03): Torii certificate issue/renew signing derives the emitted certificate `operator` from the configured offline-issuer keypair plus controller domain (client draft `operator` is optional/ignored), and build-claim issuance now supports issuer-key rotation by selecting a signer from `torii.offline_issuer.operator_private_key` plus `torii.offline_issuer.legacy_operator_private_keys` that matches the certificate operator key. SDK request encoders remain aligned so Swift/Android/JS `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft` payloads omit `operator` by default; JS docs/tests were updated to reflect omission on draft requests. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api` (ok; `7 passed`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_build_claims_issue_uses_legacy_operator_key_when_primary_rotated -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_config -- --nocapture` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Kura block-index alignment race fix, 2026-03-03): hardened `crates/iroha_core/src/kura.rs` block-index entry writes to use fixed-size 16-byte entry encoding in single write calls across live paths (`append_block_batch`, `write_block_index`, and eviction rewrite temp index generation), eliminating transient `blocks.index` length misalignment windows observed by concurrent readers in `sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store`. Added layout regression test `block_index_encoding_is_fixed_little_endian_layout` and revalidated the failing integration scenario (`cargo test -p integration_tests sumeragi_da::sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store -- --nocapture`, ok). Remaining open for this tranche: monitor CI for recurrence; no additional code changes queued.

Latest sync (unstable-network 5-peer supply stall hardening, 2026-03-02): updated `integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/unstable_network.rs` to always issue a bounded post-recovery transaction rebroadcast probe (`recovered_submit_window`) before waiting for per-round supply convergence, even when partitioned submission already returned success. The recovered probe now logs and falls through to periodic supply-wait retries instead of failing the round immediately, preserving progress under transient submit-path instability. Added helper coverage for the new window computation (`recovered_submit_window_has_minimum_probe_delay`, `recovered_submit_window_caps_at_sync_timeout`) and validated the previously failing scenario: `cargo test -p integration_tests extra_functional::unstable_network::unstable_network_5_peers_1_fault -- --nocapture` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: rerun the wider unstable-network matrix (`8/9/12` peer variants) under CI contention to confirm no new regressions.

Latest sync (unstable-network soft-fork supply retry hardening, 2026-03-01): updated `integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/unstable_network.rs` so per-round supply waits can resubmit recovered transactions during forced soft-fork runs even when no peers are explicitly marked faulty (`allow_supply_resubmit` now returns true for `force_soft_fork`). Added regression coverage `supply_resubmit_allowed_for_faults_or_soft_fork` and validated `extra_functional::unstable_network::soft_fork` in repeated runs. Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (local compile acceleration enablement, 2026-03-02): installed host accelerators via Homebrew (`sccache`, `llvm`, `lld`) and validated wrapper auto-detection (`sccache` + `ld64.lld`). Extended `scripts/cargo_fast.sh` with persistent cache placement (`--sccache-dir`) and cache-heavy mode (`--no-incremental`, sets `CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0`) in addition to `--zero-debug`. Validation: `brew install sccache llvm` (ok), `brew install lld` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh --print-env -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh --no-incremental --print-env -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok). Measured two-pass accelerated `irohad` cold-target run with shared sccache: run1 `162.35s` -> run2 `151.29s`. Remaining open: run one uncontended median-of-N timing sweep (with and without `--no-incremental`) to lock a reproducible recommended local profile.

Latest sync (integration-tests build-script dependency pruning, 2026-03-02): replaced `integration_tests/build.rs` heavyweight sample generation (which pulled `ivm`/`iroha_data_model`/`norito` and related crates as build-dependencies) with deterministic fixture staging from `integration_tests/fixtures/ivm/*.to`, removed the now-unused `[build-dependencies]` from `integration_tests/Cargo.toml`, and added missing fixture samples to keep the full prebuilt sample set available. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), cold baseline `/usr/bin/time -p scripts/cargo_fast.sh --no-sccache --linker off --target-dir /tmp/iroha_int_before -- check -p integration_tests --tests` -> `real 168.62s`, cold post-change same command with `/tmp/iroha_int_after` -> `real 156.72s` (~7% faster), plus `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p irohad` (ok). Remaining open: evaluate whether `crates/ivm/src/bin/ivm_prebuild.rs` can reuse the same fixture source for consistency and avoid drift between fixture/prebuild generation paths.

Latest sync (unused build-dependency edge cleanup, 2026-03-02): removed two additional unused build-dependencies where `build.rs` does not reference them (`crates/ivm`: removed `cargo_metadata`; `crates/iroha_js_host`: removed `napi-build`). Validation: `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p ivm` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p iroha_js_host` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p irohad` (ok), `cargo tree -p ivm -i cargo_metadata` (expected no match), `cargo tree -p iroha_js_host -e build -i napi-build` (expected no build-edge path). Remaining open: run one clean, uncontended A/B timing sweep on `ivm`/`iroha_js_host` to quantify the direct wall-clock gain from these two edge removals.

Latest sync (integration-tests direct dev-dependency pruning, 2026-03-02): removed unused direct dev-dependencies in `integration_tests/Cargo.toml` (`soranet-relay`, `expect-test`, and direct `sorafs_chunker`). Validation: `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p integration_tests --tests` (ok), plus dependency-graph confirmation (`cargo tree -p integration_tests -i soranet-relay` and `-i expect-test` now report no match). Remaining open: run a clean idle-host A/B to determine whether this direct-edge pruning yields a stable measurable wall-clock reduction (initial cold run remained within observed session variance).

Latest sync (`irohad` direct dependency pruning, 2026-03-02): removed unused direct dependencies (`tokio-util`, `owo-colors`) and unused dev-dependencies (`json5`, `futures`) from `crates/irohad/Cargo.toml`. Validation: `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p irohad` (ok), and direct-edge confirmation (`cargo tree -p irohad --depth 1 | rg 'tokio-util|owo-colors|json5|futures v0.3'` -> no matches). Remaining open: run an uncontended before/after timing pass dedicated to `irohad` direct-edge pruning to quantify standalone wall-clock impact.

Latest sync (dev/test dependency codegen-units throughput tuning, 2026-03-03): updated workspace profiles in `Cargo.toml` to set `[profile.dev.package."*"].codegen-units = 256` and `[profile.test.package."*"].codegen-units = 256` to increase local compile throughput for dependency crates while keeping runtime features unchanged. Validation: cold A/B on `irohad` with accelerators disabled showed `160.87s -> 151.24s` (~6.0%) using `/usr/bin/time -p scripts/cargo_fast.sh --no-sccache --linker off --target-dir /tmp/iroha_cgu_before|after -- check -p irohad`; cross-target sanity `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p integration_tests --tests` (ok). Remaining open: run an uncontended median-of-N sweep for `irohad`, `iroha_cli`, and `iroha_torii` under this profile tuning to lock a stable recommended dev profile.

Latest sync (compile-feature audit follow-up, 2026-03-02): reran Cargo manifest audit for redundant build-dependency edges (`[build-dependencies]` crates without `build.rs`/`build = ...`) across the workspace after `iroha_crypto` cleanup; no additional removable edges remain. Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (compile-time speed-up for unchanged feature set, 2026-02-28): extended the previous two-pass tranche with a third pass focused on build-script dependency cost plus a small fourth-pass dependency graph cleanup. `build-support` no longer depends on `vergen`; `VERGEN_CARGO_TARGET_TRIPLE`/`VERGEN_CARGO_FEATURES` are now emitted directly from Cargo env (`TARGET`, `CARGO_CFG_FEATURE`) while preserving `VERGEN_GIT_SHA` and deterministic git rerun hints. Also split tool-only dependencies by making `norito` optional behind `norito-json` in `build-support`, set build-script consumers (`irohad`, `iroha_cli`, `iroha_telemetry`, `iroha_torii`) to `build-support = { default-features = false }`, and removed a redundant `build-support` build-dependency edge from `iroha_crypto` (crate has no build script). Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass8b_build_support cargo test -p build-support` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass8c_check_irohad cargo check -p irohad` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass9_crypto cargo check -p iroha_crypto` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass9_irohad cargo check -p irohad` (ok), `cargo tree -p iroha_crypto -i build-support` (expected failure: removed edge). Timing runs during this session were high-variance (`check --timings` around `2m36s..2m37s`, `test --no-run --timings` around `3m34s..3m45s`), so remaining open for this tranche: run one uncontended A/B timing sweep on an otherwise idle host to quantify the net wall-clock impact of Pass 3/4.

Latest sync (developer build acceleration helper, 2026-02-28): added `scripts/cargo_fast.sh`, a safe Cargo wrapper that auto-enables `sccache` when present and probes fast-linker compatibility before applying `-fuse-ld`; otherwise it falls back to system defaults. Auto-linker detection now checks discovered platform candidates (`mold`/`lld`/`ld.lld` on Linux, `zld`/`ld64.lld`/`lld` on macOS) and skips unnecessary failed probes when no candidate binary is installed. Added an opt-in `--zero-debug` mode (`CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0`, `CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0`) for faster local iteration without changing feature sets. Added usage discoverability in `scripts/AGENTS.md`. Validation: `bash -n scripts/cargo_fast.sh` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh --print-env -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh --linker lld --print-env -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok, graceful fallback), `scripts/cargo_fast.sh --zero-debug --print-env -- check -p build-support --no-default-features` (ok), cold A/B on `irohad` (`cargo check`: baseline `154.91s`, helper `154.60s` with no detected accelerators). Remaining open for this tranche: install/enable at least one accelerator (`sccache` and/or supported fast linker) and rerun the same A/B sweep to capture non-fallback gains.

Latest sync (sandbox serialized-network drop flake hardening, 2026-02-28): stabilized `integration_tests/src/sandbox.rs` test `serialized_network_drop_completes_on_current_thread_runtime` by adding an explicit peer startup timeout override (`with_peer_startup_timeout(Duration::from_secs(480))`). This test targets `SerializedNetwork` drop behavior under a current-thread runtime; on loaded hosts startup can exceed the generic 240s floor and fail before reaching the drop assertion. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests serialized_network_drop_completes_on_current_thread_runtime -- --nocapture` (ok; target test passed, observed runtime `862.22s`). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Sumeragi adversarial RBC flake hardening, 2026-02-27): stabilized `integration_tests/tests/sumeragi_adversarial.rs` checks for `sumeragi_adversarial_chunk_drop` and `sumeragi_adversarial_locked_qc_gate_rejects_conflicting_proposal` by switching from single-snapshot `delivered` gating to post-wait, height-wide session delivery evaluation plus committed-height confirmation. Added helper regression coverage (`delivered_height_check_scans_all_sessions_for_the_height`). Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_adversarial -- --nocapture` (ok; `12 passed`, `0 failed`, `623.31s`). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale repeated-cycle strict retry hardening, 2026-02-27): stabilized `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` repeated-cycle localnet coverage by adding bounded per-cycle retries (`AUTOSCALE_MULTI_CYCLE_RETRY_LIMIT=2`) with attempt-scaled strict load (`96 -> 192`) in `nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet`. Strict deterministic transition assertions remain enabled on each attempt; retries only absorb first-attempt strict expansion misses observed under localnet startup contention. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok; `28 passed`), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `224.57s`). Remaining open for this tranche: monitor nightly soak/repeated-cycle stability; no additional code changes queued.

Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace startup retry stability, 2026-02-27): fixed stale startup-run cleanup in `crates/iroha_test_network/src/lib.rs` by changing `Network::shutdown()` to invoke `shutdown_if_started()` for all peers (not only peers with `is_running=true`). This closes a retry panic where a peer could exit during startup, flip `is_running=false`, keep a stale `run` handle, and then panic on next startup attempt with `already running`. Added regression test `network_shutdown_clears_stale_peer_runs_even_when_not_marked_running` and revalidated the original failing scenario: `cargo test -p iroha_test_network network_shutdown_clears_stale_peer_runs_even_when_not_marked_running -- --nocapture` (ok) and `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::cross_dataspace_localnet::cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok, `296.26s`). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (SORA parliament JIT/no-council completion + VRF epoch-seed syscall, 2026-02-26): completed the planned parliament hardening in `iroha_core` by binding approvals to proposal-time multibody sortition snapshots (selection epoch, beacon, roster commitment) in parliament mode and removing council-state dependency for those approvals; stage quorum checks now use proposal-bound epochs when snapshots exist. Added bounded bond-tier ticket weighting in governance citizen draws and maintained hostile/Sybil coverage. Implemented ABI syscall `VRF_EPOCH_SEED (0x7E)` with shared Norito envelopes (`ivm::vrf::{VrfEpochSeedRequest,VrfEpochSeedResponse}`), world-snapshot hydration in CoreHost setup paths, host tests for found/missing/latest-fallback behavior, and synced ABI/docs artifacts (`ivm` spec/docs/generated table + golden syscall list). Validation: `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo run -p ivm --bin gen_syscalls_doc -- --write`, `cargo test -p ivm --test abi_syscall_list_golden --test syscalls_doc_sync`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib vrf_epoch_seed_syscall_`, and targeted governance suites (`gov_council_gate`, `gov_pipeline_sla`, `gov_propose_validation`, `gov_enact_deploy`, `gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil`) all passed. Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Sumeragi DA Kura-eviction test flake fix, 2026-02-26): stabilized `integration_tests/tests/sumeragi_da.rs` scenario `sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store` by replacing per-iteration network-wide height synchronization with a timeout-bounded wait on the inspected peer (`peer.once_block(expected_height)`). This aligns synchronization with the scenario's verification target (single-peer Kura eviction + DA-store rehydration) and prevents unrelated peer lag from triggering false `Network overall height did not pass given predicate` failures. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_da sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_da sumeragi_da_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Offline allowance/App Attest gap closure, 2026-02-27): closed the remaining follow-up gaps in `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs` by adding dual App Attest nonce-extension decoding compatibility (Apple production DER wrapper + legacy plain OCTET STRING), adding fixed DER fixture decoding and legacy-encoding verification tests, introducing registration-time reallocation logic so certificate reprovision/reissue can proceed without mandatory balance change when sufficient allowance is already reserved on-ledger (shortfall-only reserve remains enforced), and adding deterministic newest-first reallocation regression coverage to lock consensus behavior. Validation now includes integration-level offline admission checks. Updated `docs/source/offline_allowance.md` to document nonce-format compatibility. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core attestation::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok; `18 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p iroha_core register_allowance_reissue_ -- --nocapture` (ok; `2 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p iroha_core register_allowance_ -- --nocapture` (ok; `11 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test offline_allowance_security -- --nocapture` (ok; `3 passed`, `0 failed`). Latest sync (Offline lineage/build-claim fixture gap closure, 2026-02-27): completed follow-up fixture/test alignment for enforced certificate lineage and build claims across core and Torii by updating register/reissue/renew test certificates to carry valid lineage metadata chains, isolating future-issued rejection coverage from lineage collisions, and updating Torii offline app/certificate API fixtures to include valid lineage drafts plus signed Apple build claims with required iOS metadata bindings. Latest sync (2026-02-26 post-policy reruns complete): reran unchanged-shape 1200s/3600s gates after the near-quorum backlog-threshold and aggregate-mismatch retry refinements. Artifacts: pilot `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_gate_20260226T191033Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_8QiS6c` (`successes=2971 failures=1`, strict `630`, forced-VC `1`, fallback-anchor `19`); acceptance `/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_20260226T191033Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_PYVD6w` (`successes=7473 failures=4`, strict `1535`, forced-VC `1` with one 6s-class event, fallback-anchor `48`). No progress-timeout failure occurred. Latest sync (2026-02-26 bottleneck-policy follow-up implemented): completed the internal liveness refinements for the top stall/churn bottlenecks in `iroha_core` without FSM/public-config changes: near-quorum reduced-timeout gates now use thresholded queue backlog semantics (`main_loop.rs`, `reschedule.rs`, `propose.rs`), forced-VC pending-stall path now uses soft-limit RBC backlog gating for near-quorum reduction, QC missing-payload fast recovery prioritizes fetch/range-pull before vote churn (`main_loop/qc.rs`), and block-sync `AggregateMismatch` QC failures now enter retryable quarantine/replay instead of immediate final-drop (`main_loop/block_sync.rs`). Focused validation is green (`cargo fmt --all` plus targeted `iroha_core` tests for forced-VC near-quorum gating, reschedule suppression semantics, QC fast-recovery cooldown, cached-slot behavior, and aggregate-mismatch retry classification). Remaining open in this roadmap area: rerun unchanged-shape 1200s/3600s gates and verify that these policy shifts reduce payload-missing/fallback churn while preserving the previously improved 6s-class timeout behavior.

Latest sync (gate A/B reruns executed, 2026-02-26): completed the planned unchanged-shape reruns with canonical envelope (`--allow-net --nexus --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 1200s/3600s --target-blocks 1200/3600 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`). Pilot artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_gate_20260226T145420Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_L8nEAl` (`successes=3073 failures=1 ingress_failover_total=0 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=0`, strict persisted height `651`). Acceptance artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_20260226T162742Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_KJGDpZ` (`successes=7453 failures=3 ingress_failover_total=8 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=1`, strict persisted height `1537`). Compared to the latest 2026-02-26 recovery baseline (`3101/7554` successes, strict `652/1555`), throughput dipped modestly while safety/liveness guardrails held: no progress-timeout failures, no `no proposal observed before cutoff` increase, forced-VC warnings decreased (`pilot 2->1`, `acceptance 2->0`), and 6s-class forced-VC events reduced (`acceptance 2->0`). Remaining open: reduce payload-missing/fallback churn (`stake quorum observed but block payload missing` and fallback-anchor share warnings both rose slightly) while recovering the lost `-1` pilot / `-18` acceptance strict-height gap versus the latest baseline.

Latest sync (2026-02-26 policy-only liveness refinement complete): implemented the DA/commit-path stall-reduction changes in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{qc.rs,propose.rs,reschedule.rs,tests.rs}`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/mode.rs`, and `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs` (plus shared-host profile alignment already maintained in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs`) without public CLI/config/FSM changes. Focused validation is green (`cargo test -p iroha_core main_loop -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib should_share_unknown_prev_hash_tracks_peer_and_height -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo fmt --all`). Remaining open for this roadmap area: run unchanged-shape 1200s/3600s gate A/B reruns against the 2026-02-26 baselines and confirm reduced 6s timeout-bound stalls/fallback-anchor churn with no progress-timeout or view-change-storm regression.

Latest sync (hotfix reruns executed, 2026-02-26): reverted the regression caused by the previous consensus timeout patch by (1) restoring shared-host DA multipliers in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` (`quorum_timeout_multiplier=4`, `availability_timeout_multiplier=3`) and (2) narrowing cached-slot timeout forcing in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs` so slots with observed precommit votes no longer trigger forced view-change. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), and focused `iroha_core` pacemaker/reschedule/QC regressions (all ok). Gate reruns with unchanged shape (`--tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`) show recovery: pilot `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_gate_20260226T105101Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_tLZ5Yo` reached persisted `651/651/652/652` with summary `successes=3101 failures=1 ingress_failover_total=0 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=0`; acceptance `/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_20260226T111739Z.log` + `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_4pyRKc` recovered from the prior 600s progress-timeout failure and finished at persisted `1554/1554/1554/1554` with summary `successes=7554 failures=12 ingress_failover_total=8 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=2`. Compared to the bad run (`295` pilot quorum candidate, `508` acceptance), liveness/throughput is restored; compared to baseline (`633` pilot, `1554` acceptance), acceptance pace is effectively back to baseline and pilot is slightly improved.

Latest sync (post-patch pilot + acceptance gate reruns executed, 2026-02-26): reran both gates unchanged (`--tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`) after the consensus stall-timeout patch and observed severe regression. Pilot artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_gate_20260226T082333Z.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_riNQf3`; outcome: `target blocks not reached`, summary `successes=954 failures=66 ingress_failover_total=238 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=100`, with persisted per-peer state showing split (`295/296/77/296`, strict candidate `77`, quorum candidate `295`). Acceptance artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_20260226T085029Z.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_ajnB2Y`; outcome: `no block height progress for 600s (quorum min 508, strict min 508, target 3600)`, summary `successes=2387 failures=30 ingress_failover_total=160 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=121`. Compared to the 2026-02-26 baseline gates (`633` pilot, `1554` acceptance), throughput regressed materially (`pilot quorum candidate 295`, `acceptance 508`), and churn shifted toward proposal starvation (`no proposal observed before cutoff` and cached-slot stalls increased sharply). The only positive signal was fewer 6s timeout-boundary age events; this did not translate to liveness or pace uplift. Remaining open: roll back or narrow the near-quorum timeout behavior and re-run A/B with identical gate shape before further tuning.

Latest sync (consensus stall-budget removal patch finalized, 2026-02-26): implemented the internal-only throughput/convergence tuning in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{qc.rs,reschedule.rs,propose.rs,votes.rs}` and `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` with no public CLI/config schema changes. The patch adds fast cooldown-gated payload-missing QC recovery, near-quorum reduced timeout gating (`clamp(2 * rebroadcast_cooldown, 400ms..2000ms)`) for stale reschedule and cached-slot stall paths (suppressed under backlog or legitimate RBC in-flight progress), and tunes balanced shared-host DA multipliers to `2/2` (quorum/availability) while keeping `tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`, and pipeline floor unchanged. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core main_loop -- --nocapture` (ok; `1289 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib should_share_unknown_prev_hash_tracks_peer_and_height -- --nocapture` (ok). Remaining open for this roadmap item: rerun the unchanged 1200s/3600s gates and compare against the 2026-02-26 baseline artifacts to confirm reduced timeout-bound churn and improved sustained block pace.

Latest sync (pilot + acceptance gate reruns executed, 2026-02-26): reran both gates unchanged (`--tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`). Pilot artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_gate_20260226T040832Z.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_XKwAsf`; outcome: `target blocks not reached`, final height `633/1200`, strict/quorum max divergence `2`, summary `successes=3023 failures=4 ingress_failover_total=0 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=0` (includes `repeat_trigger_0`-not-found submission failures). Acceptance artifacts: `/tmp/izanami_acceptance_3600_gate_20260226T043457Z.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_a4a4B0`; outcome: `target blocks not reached`, final height `1554/3600`, strict/quorum max divergence `1`, summary `successes=7592 failures=1 ingress_failover_total=0 ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=0`. Versus the prior 2026-02-25 scopefix baseline, pilot regressed by `-17` blocks while acceptance improved by `+8` blocks; acceptance progress windows averaged `4.33` blocks/10s (required `10`), so the open blocker remains throughput (not consensus split), with sustained underproduction at `43.17%` of target block pace.

Latest sync (plan implementation finalized, 2026-02-25): completed the scoped `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` patch with no public CLI/config changes: pilot+soak shared-host recovery selection now keys off `is_shared_host_stable_recovery_run(...)` (`nexus`, stable profile, `faulty_peers=0`, `peer_count>=4`, `duration>=1200s`), load-shape pinning remains soak-only (`duration>=3600s`), submit-permit scope is restricted to submit RPC/retry only, and deterministic submit backlog is bounded by `max_inflight * IZANAMI_SUBMISSION_BACKLOG_MULTIPLIER`. Shared-host baseline constants were kept unchanged (`tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`, `pipeline_time_ms=900`), and config-layer regression coverage was added (`shared_host_recovery_profile_config_layer_writes_tuned_da_timeouts`). Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p izanami submission_ -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib should_share_unknown_prev_hash_tracks_peer_and_height -- --nocapture` (ok); formal check remains environment-blocked (`bash ci/check_sumeragi_formal.sh`: `apalache-mc` missing, Docker daemon unavailable). Remaining open for this area is unchanged: 3600s acceptance throughput is still under target and needs a one-variable-at-a-time consensus-throughput tuning follow-up with fresh artifacts.

Latest sync (SORA hostile-governance adversarial coverage + 4-peer localnet closure, 2026-02-26): completed the hostile-governance tranche across core + integration and closed all remaining items. Added six adversarial Sybil/sortition tests in `crates/iroha_core/tests/gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil.rs` and hostile blocked/captured governance scenarios in `integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs`; captured-hostile path now models full committee capture under multibody sortition quorum, with explicit quorum-derived guard checks (`HOSTILE_PARLIAMENT_QUORUM_BPS`, `hostile_required_approvals()`) and dead-code warning suppression for the shared runtime helper import. Validation: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_sybil_core cargo test -p iroha_core --test gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil --no-run` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_sybil_core cargo test -p iroha_core --test gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil` (ok; `6 passed`), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_sybil_integration_live cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke --no-run` (ok), targeted captured-hostile run (ok; `1 passed`, `270.02s`), and full integration file run `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_sybil_integration_live cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `3 passed`, `609.02s`). Remaining open for this tranche: none.

Latest sync (Time-trigger scenario stabilization, 2026-02-26): hardened `integration_tests/tests/triggers/time_trigger.rs` to reduce `triggers::time_trigger::time_trigger_scenarios` flakes under transient view-change/commit lag. The `mint_nft_for_every_user_every_1_sec` path now drives sample transactions without strict per-submit height waits, uses unique metadata payloads for each driving transaction, and waits for expected NFT counts via bounded polling across all accounts. Added helper predicate coverage (`counts_meet_expected_requires_each_account_to_reach_target`). Latest sync (SORA runtime-governance helper extraction + overlap-policy regression, 2026-02-25): extracted shared runtime-governance fixture/round logic into `integration_tests/tests/common/sora_runtime_governance.rs` and wired both `integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs` and `integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs` to consume it (shared governance builder in smoke + shared runtime round execution in both files). Runtime chained-round scheduling now derives from on-chain runtime-upgrade state (`max end_height + 1`) instead of local fixture-only state. Added negative regression `sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_rejects_overlapping_runtime_window_proposal` that enacts a baseline runtime upgrade and asserts a second overlapping proposal is rejected at submission. Resilience lint hygiene was tightened by removing broad file-level allow usage and pruning duplicated local helper scaffolding. Latest sync (Cross-lane hardening gap closure + CI filter wiring, 2026-02-25): completed the remaining optional cross-lane hardening gaps in `crates/iroha/src/nexus.rs` and `crates/iroha/src/client.rs`, and wired a dedicated PR CI filter. Latest sync (SORA runtime-upgrade resilience e2e gap fill, 2026-02-25): completed `integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs` implementation (the file had been left truncated at `#[tokio::test]`) by adding full runtime-governance resilience coverage. New scenarios include peer-restart fault injection during multi-body approvals (`sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_peer_restart_during_multibody_sortition`) and an ignored chained-upgrade soak (`sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_chained_rounds_soak`). Runtime round orchestration now gates referendum polling on successful `ProposeRuntimeUpgradeProposal` application and reuses the proven governance permission prerequisites (`CanProposeContractDeployment`, `CanEnactGovernance`) to avoid admission flakes. Follow-up hardening for chained rounds added non-overlap scheduling (`next_runtime_window_start`) and reduced round window duration (`RUNTIME_WINDOW_DURATION_BLOCKS=40`) so second-round activation is observable within deterministic tick budgets. Latest sync (Nexus autoscale gap-closure validation follow-up, 2026-02-25): completed targeted cargo validation for the new strict-contraction baseline and soak reporter paths. Also smoke-validated the nightly workflow command shape with forced-fail env override (`cargo test ... nexus_autoscale_soak_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1`) and confirmed artifact flush under `/tmp/nexus_autoscale_soak_ci_smoke`. Latest sync (Taira quorum-first membership lag hardening + staged soak gate, 2026-02-24): implemented quorum-first membership lag classification in `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` by replacing join/leave `Result<bool>` lag flags with `MembershipCycleOutcome { hard_lagged, warning_lagged }`, keeping only submit/propagation/quorum failures as hard lag, and making joiner catch-up stall threshold crossings warning-only. `run_taira_simulation(...)` now tracks `membership_churn_warning_cycles`, applies membership churn backoff only on hard lag, and writes the new summary JSON field without removing existing fields. Added focused tests for warning-vs-hard classification, hard-only backoff behavior, and summary JSON field presence; updated `integrated_test_framework.md` semantics accordingly. Validation: compile gate `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_taira_soak cargo test -p integration_tests --test taira_public_localnet --no-run` passed; focused tests passed via direct test binary execution (`stalled_joiner_catchup_marks_warning_without_hard_lag`, `propagation_and_quorum_failures_mark_hard_lag`, `membership_backoff_triggers_only_on_hard_lag`, `simulation_summary_json_includes_membership_warning_cycles`, `joiner_stall_warning_threshold_matches_policy`, plus existing membership deadline tests). Stage A 3-seed gate run with preserved artifacts (`IROHA_TAIRA_KEEP_LOCALNET=1`, seeds `taira-matrix-a|b|c`) produced: `a` pass (`3602.28s`, summary `target/taira-localnet/taira-simulationyIq7Ec/localnet/taira_simulation_summary.json`, hard/warn `0/3`), `b` fail (`3605.63s`, `target/taira-localnet/taira-simulationSzZOXi`, failure `membership churn lagged cycles exceeded threshold: lagged=5, total=10, allowed=4`), `c` pass (`3604.89s`, summary `target/taira-localnet/taira-simulationZK5Vl4/localnet/taira_simulation_summary.json`, hard/warn `0/3`). Gate result at this checkpoint was `2/3` passing and Stage B blocked. Remaining open for this historical checkpoint: closed by the 2026-02-25 staged matrix completion.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet gap closure plan execution, 2026-02-24): completed the requested hardening scope across strict contraction semantics, soak observability artifacts, deterministic matrix tooling, and nightly CI gating. `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` now requires strict scale-in quorum against post-expansion transition baselines (cycle-start deltas retained for diagnostics only), emits structured soak artifacts (`autoscale_soak_summary.json`, `autoscale_soak_events.jsonl`) through internal reporter structs, supports deterministic seed control via `IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_SEED`, supports artifact output override via `IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_ARTIFACT_DIR`, and includes a test-only forced-failure hook (`IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_FORCE_FAIL_CYCLE`) so failure runs still flush artifacts. Added `scripts/nexus/run_autoscale_soak_matrix.sh` for multi-seed ignored-soak matrix execution + artifact-native aggregation (CSV/JSON, pass/fail counts, retry/max-attempt/cycle distributions, expansion/contraction p50/p95), and added `.github/workflows/nightly_nexus_autoscale_soak.yml` for scheduled/manual ignored-soak gating with always-on artifact upload. Validation completed so far: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` and `bash -n scripts/nexus/run_autoscale_soak_matrix.sh` (both ok). The earlier “queued cargo validations still pending” note from this entry is superseded by the completed follow-up validations listed above.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet soak resilience hardening, 2026-02-24): closed the remaining autoscale soak reliability gaps in `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` by adding attempt-based strict-cycle load escalation (`96 -> 192 -> 288 -> 384`), running cooldown-clearance prechecks only on the first attempt of each cycle (so retries are not blocked on no-load non-empty-height movement), and making expansion top-up pressure load-aware during strict waits (periodic/reinforce/post-storage top-ups now scale with per-attempt load). Added helper/unit coverage for retry load profile, cooldown-clearance gating policy, and load-scaled top-up behavior. Latest sync (Nexus matrix runner five-gap hardening closure, 2026-02-24): completed the remaining reliability gaps in `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh` by adding strict option-value guards, timeout-marker-based `timed_out` attribution, fail-fast summary finalization via `trap finalize_outputs EXIT`, process-group timeout teardown (`SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL`) to prevent orphan children, and exact/anchored test-id matching for `--prefer-test-binary` resolution. Latest sync (deterministic missing-QC + shared-host soak hardening, 2026-02-25): completed the requested deterministic recovery tranche in Sumeragi (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/{block_sync.rs,proposal_handlers.rs,qc.rs,votes.rs}`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`), validated targeted regressions, and revalidated formal checks (`bash ci/check_sumeragi_formal.sh` now passes locally with Apalache). Latest sync (pilot recovery selector + submit-path throughput uncap, 2026-02-25): implemented the planned `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` changes while keeping the external gate interface fixed at `--tps 5 --max-inflight 8`: (1) `recovery_profile_for(...)` now uses new `is_shared_host_stable_recovery_run(...)` (`nexus`, stable, `faulty_peers=0`, `peer_count>=4`, `duration>=1200s`) so pilot-scale runs get shared-host recovery tuning; (2) shared-host load-shape pinning remains soak-only (`duration>=3600s`); (3) submit permits were moved from supervisor pre-plan scope into `submit_plan(...)` immediately before submit RPC/retry and released right after submit completion; (4) deterministic submit-backlog bound added with `IZANAMI_SUBMISSION_BACKLOG_MULTIPLIER=8` and enforced queue gating at `max_inflight * multiplier`; (5) regression coverage added for pilot recovery selection and backlog/permit invariants.
Validation for this patch: `cargo fmt --all` (ok); `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok); `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok); `cargo test -p izanami submission_ -- --nocapture` (ok); `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib should_share_unknown_prev_hash_tracks_peer_and_height -- --nocapture` (ok); `bash ci/check_sumeragi_formal.sh` (environment failure: `apalache-mc` missing, Docker daemon unavailable).
Remaining open for this item: maintain the fixed interface and carry the next single-variable throughput experiment with fresh pilot + acceptance reruns and artifact-backed comparison against this `650/1200` and `1546/3600` baseline.

Latest sync (lagging-peer recovery storm suppression + inflight A/B lock, 2026-02-25): completed deterministic recovery-loop suppression in `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`, and `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs` (per-peer unknown-prev fallback suppression; one active highest-QC-missing defer marker/reacquire per `(height, view, highest_qc_hash)` key with stale-marker cleanup), and pinned the shared-host baseline in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` (`tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`) with profile regression coverage. A/B pilot evidence from 1200s runs (`/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_inflight8_20260225T162303.log`, `/tmp/izanami_pilot_1200_inflight6_20260225T165243.log`, and paired sample JSONL artifacts) showed `max_inflight=8` materially reduces divergence and missing-fetch churn versus `max_inflight=6`, so the inflight decision is complete and fixed at `8` for acceptance soaks.
Remaining open: only the 3600s shared-host acceptance gate remains (`--duration 3600s --target-blocks 3600 --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`), with pass criteria requiring all four peers at height `>= 3600` without progress-timeout and without prolonged stale missing-QC/proposal-missing loops.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale repeated-cycle scale-in gating follow-up, 2026-02-24): stabilized `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` by making cycle-level strict scale-in enforcement conditional on expansion status quorum visibility. Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet strict expansion gating + load-submit resilience, 2026-02-23): tightened `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` to reduce false-positive expansion detection and improve localnet reliability under partial peer instability. `wait_for_expanded_lanes_with_heartbeat(...)` now supports strict mode that requires quorum deterministic scale-out transitions and suppresses storage-only fallback in that mode; repeated-cycle flow now keeps strict scale-out checks in both cycles while enforcing deterministic scale-in transition quorum in cycle 1. Also hardened `submit_load_round_robin(...)` to continue on per-transaction submission failures and only fail when all submissions fail, so one unhealthy peer does not starve expansion load. Latest sync (Confidential localnet ZkNative behavior re-alignment + duplicate-nullifier stabilization, 2026-02-22): reverted the temporary rejection-only ZkNative assumptions in `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` after direct runtime verification, restoring observed behavior coverage for transparent mint/transfer (`confidential_zknative_transparent_mint_creates_public_balance_localnet`, `confidential_zknative_transparent_transfer_after_mint_localnet`). Latest sync (Confidential localnet per-transaction real Halo2 proof generation + quorum poll hardening, 2026-02-22): updated `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` to eliminate shared cached proof attachments and generate fresh real Halo2 backend attachments per `ZkTransfer`/`Unshield` operation via `iroha_core::zk::test_utils::halo2_ivm_execution_envelope(...)` (`live_halo2_attachment`). Also hardened non-empty quorum polling with temporary mute-on-error-streak behavior (`STATUS_ERROR_STREAK_FOR_MUTE=3`, `MUTED_PEER_COOLDOWN_ATTEMPTS=5`) guarded by quorum-safe muting (`should_temporarily_mute_peer`) so unhealthy peer polls are de-prioritized without lowering required quorum checks. Increased fallback polling horizon (`ATTEMPTS=200`, `DELAY=300ms`) after observing one non-empty progression timeout under load. Added helper tests for muting/quorum logic. Validation: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_zk_conf_localnet_fast NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/iroha3d IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=16 cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet -- --test-threads=1` (pre-horizon-adjustment run: `22 passed`, `1 failed` at `confidential_zknative_transparent_transfer_after_mint_localnet` with `expected non-empty block 3 on quorum 3`; runtime `368.25s`). Post-adjustment targeted reruns are currently blocked by pre-existing long-running/stalled cargo processes (no CPU progress) and were left untouched to comply with the workspace rule against killing `cargo`/`rustc` without explicit approval. Remaining open: rerun the full confidential file and workspace-level validations after those external stuck cargo processes are cleared.

Latest sync (Nexus matrix runner auto-aggregate summaries, 2026-02-22): extended `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh` to publish built-in aggregate results for every invocation, eliminating manual post-processing. The runner now writes `<output-dir>/matrix_summary.txt` and prints `cross_summary`/`runtime_summary` lines derived from CSV data (run count, fail/timeout count, duration min/avg/max, plus soak/pass-rate and delta/fallback metrics). Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet repeated-cycle closure, 2026-02-22): closed the strict localnet autoscale test gap by updating cycle assertions to require `scale-in` transitions on a full cycle baseline window (instead of post-expansion-only deltas), wiring strict contraction waits to baseline transition counters, and avoiding contraction heartbeat writes in strict mode. Also tuned localnet autoscale utilization normalization (`per_lane_target_tps=40`) so synthetic burst traffic still triggers deterministic `scale-out` while permitting deterministic `scale-in` emission under the same run profile. Core autoscale scale-in evaluation was split into `autoscale_scale_in_triggered(...)` and no longer depends on latency ratio. Validation: `cargo test -p iroha_core autoscale_scale_in_triggered -- --nocapture` (`2 passed`), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_gapfill cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (`13 passed`), strict repeated-cycle localnet (`... nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --nocapture`) now passes with quorum `scale-in` in both cycles (`since cycle start 4/4`), and single-cycle localnet (`... nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_expands_and_contracts_lanes_in_localnet -- --nocapture`) remains passing with matching transition evidence. Remaining open for this area: track pathological early `out_latency_ratio_permille` values observed in peer logs (very large startup-time ratios) and decide whether to clamp/guard latency sampling in core autoscale metrics.

Latest sync (Nexus matrix runner resilience follow-up, 2026-02-22): hardened `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh` to support partial and bounded matrix execution so a stalled test leg does not block all data collection. Added `--skip-cross`/`--skip-runtime`, optional per-run timeout knobs (`--cross-timeout-s`, `--runtime-timeout-s`, default disabled), `--cargo-jobs` (default `1`) for deterministic compile parallelism in busy environments, `--prefer-test-binary` for direct execution of prebuilt `mod-*` test binaries from `<target-dir>/debug/deps` (avoids cargo rebuild overhead on warm targets), and default `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1` injection (opt-out: `--no-skip-bindings-sync`) to avoid fresh-target JDK parity-check failures. CSV outputs include `timed_out` fields for both cross/runtime rows. Validation: `bash -n scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh` (ok), `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh --help` (ok), guard-path check `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh --skip-cross --skip-runtime` (expected fast failure with no-run selection), and missing-binary guard `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh --skip-cross --runtime-runs 1 --prefer-test-binary --target-dir /tmp/iroha_no_such_target_dir` (expected fast failure: `Unable to locate executable mod-* test binary ...`). Additional smoke evidence: `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_cross_smoke3/cross_dataspace_matrix.csv` contains completed new-schema cargo-path cross row (`run=1`, `exit=0`, `timed_out=0`, `soak_passed=1/1`, `duration_s=832`), `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_cross_smoke4/cross_dataspace_matrix.csv` contains completed direct-binary cross row (`run=1`, `exit=0`, `timed_out=0`, `soak_passed=1/1`, `duration_s=78`), `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_runtime_smoke/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` captured non-timeout runtime failure row (`exit=101`) on missing-JDK Norito parity path, `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_runtime_smoke2/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` captured bounded-timeout row (`exit=124`, `timed_out=1`, `duration_s=600`), `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_runtime_smoke4/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` captured successful direct-binary runtime row (`run=1`, `exit=0`, `timed_out=0`, `duration_s=33`, `pass_rate=100.0`, delta max `0/0`), and combined smoke `/tmp/iroha_nexus_matrix_smoke5/{cross_dataspace_matrix.csv,runtime_registration_matrix.csv}` captured successful cross+runtime rows in one invocation (`cross: exit=0 timed_out=0 duration_s=79`; `runtime: exit=0 timed_out=0 duration_s=21 pass_rate=100.0`).

Latest sync (Confidential localnet bounded all-peer wait hardening, 2026-02-22): updated `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` to cap all-peer non-empty block waits at `15s` (`ALL_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT`) before falling back to quorum progression checks, preventing prolonged stalls when a minority peer times out during real Halo2-proof verification. Validation: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_zk_conf_localnet_fast NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/iroha3d IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=16 cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet -- --test-threads=1 confidential_public_and_shielded_three_hop_localnet` (ok, `34.01s`), and full suite rerun `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet -- --test-threads=1` (ok, `20 passed`, `258.45s`). For comparison, a pre-hardening in-flight run completed at `20 passed` in `891.39s`. Remaining open for this area: keep observing long-run peer timeout frequency, but no blocking correctness failures remain in the confidential localnet suite.

Latest sync (formal model liveness closure + local Apalache verification, 2026-02-23): fixed the remaining Apalache liveness counterexample in `docs/formal/sumeragi/Sumeragi.tla` by encoding post-GST scheduler fairness operationally in `Next` (explicit enabled predicates + timeout/fault preemption guards), then revalidated `fast` and `deep` with a local Apalache 0.52.2 binary (`NoError` to bounded length 10 in both modes). Updated `docs/formal/sumeragi/README.md` to document the operational fairness approach because Apalache 0.52.x does not support `WF_` fairness operators inside checked temporal properties.
Remaining open from this formal tranche: first live PR confirmation of the `sumeragi_formal` GitHub Actions job (and optional image digest pinning policy decision) is still pending.

Latest sync (deep fair-liveness closure + Apalache tag alignment, 2026-02-23): re-enabled fair-liveness checks in ignored deep topologies (`fair_model_permissioned_n10_equivalence_deep_safety_liveness_fairness`, `fair_model_npos_n13_equivalence_deep_safety_liveness_fairness`) by tightening fair-scheduler behavior in `state_machine_fairness_model_tests.rs` so post-GST `TimeoutTick` cannot preempt enabled non-timeout fair-progress events. Local validation: syntax checks pass, fallback messages behave as expected, and `scripts/formal/sumeragi_apalache.sh fast` currently exits `127` in this environment due missing local binary and unavailable Docker daemon (`/var/run/docker.sock` absent).
