Latest sync (Sumeragi DA restart-recovery rerun closure, 2026-02-23): executed the previously-open clean rerun `cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_da sumeragi_rbc_recovers_after_peer_restart -- --nocapture` and the scenario now passes end-to-end (`1 passed`, `0 failed`, `finished in 259.60s`). Observed shutdown/log-flush warnings from `iroha_test_network` during teardown (`forcing peer shutdown after fatal signal`, `timed out waiting for log flush`) remained non-fatal; peers exited `status(0)` and the test assertion passed.

Latest sync (Sumeragi model runtime closure + deep-tier runner fix, 2026-02-23): reduced bounded model runtime in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests/state_machine_model_tests.rs` and `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests/state_machine_fairness_model_tests.rs` by treating post-commit states as terminal in bounded BFS, collapsing byzantine counters under equivalence reduction, and caching fairness-enabled masks in adversarial fairness recursion. Fixed `scripts/tests/sumeragi_model_tiers.sh` deep ignored invocation (`-- --ignored --nocapture`). Deep ignored topology tests now run and pass with safety+liveness+fair-liveness under fair-scheduler timeout-preemption guards. Validation: `scripts/tests/sumeragi_model_tiers.sh fast` (model `12 passed` in `85.18s`, fairness `5 passed`, `2 ignored` in `82.62s`) and `scripts/tests/sumeragi_model_tiers.sh deep` (model `12 passed` in `92.46s`, non-ignored fairness `5 passed`, `2 ignored` in `87.74s`, ignored deep fairness topologies `2 passed` in `174.98s`).

Latest sync (Sumeragi fairness model + formal scaffolding, 2026-02-23): added `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests/state_machine_fairness_model_tests.rs` and wired it from `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs` to model stake-weighted permissioned/NPoS safety+liveness under Byzantine faults with RBC causality constraints, weak-fairness starvation checks, and runtime stake-quorum parity assertions. Added tiered runners (`scripts/tests/sumeragi_model_tiers.sh` with `fast`/`deep`, `scripts/formal/sumeragi_apalache.sh` with `fast`/`deep`) and TLA+/Apalache assets (`docs/formal/sumeragi/Sumeragi.tla`, `Sumeragi_fast.cfg`, `Sumeragi_deep.cfg`, `README.md`). Validation: `scripts/tests/sumeragi_model_tiers.sh fast` passed (`state_machine_model_tests`: `12 passed` in `426.73s`; fairness suite: `5 passed`, `2 ignored` in `605.91s`). Remaining open in this tranche: Apalache execution is blocked in this environment until `apalache-mc` is installed (`scripts/formal/sumeragi_apalache.sh fast` currently reports tool missing).

Latest sync (missing-block in-flight progress accounting + canonical soak rerun, 2026-02-23): stabilized the remaining parallel `iroha_core` failures by isolating shared status globals in `commit_pipeline_uses_epoch_for_height_when_emitting_votes` and `da_gate_context_does_not_block_quorum_reschedule` (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`), then reran `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib -q` (ok: `3480 passed; 0 failed; 2 ignored`). Reran the unchanged canonical Izanami command (`RUST_LOG=izanami::summary=info,izanami::workload=warn ... --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`) and captured artifacts at `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260223T043425_codex_3600s.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_lIA9iV`. The run failed with `no block height progress for 300s (quorum min height 138, strict min 65, target 2000, tolerated_failures 1)` at `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs:1540`; terminal summary: `successes=398 failures=15 expected_failures=0 unexpected_successes=0 izanami_ingress_failover_total=70 izanami_ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=59`. Peer spread at failure was `139/139/138/65`; ingress warnings showed repeated `/transaction` and `/v1/node/capabilities` timeouts on lagging endpoints. Remaining open: eliminate the single-peer stall/divergence path (stuck around height 65) so strict-min tracks quorum-min through the full 3600s window.

Latest sync (canonical 3600s soak rerun after stack-overflow recursion guard, 2026-02-22): reran the unchanged canonical Izanami command and captured artifacts at `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260222T131236_codex_3600s.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_yJqKuh`. The run failed with `no block height progress for 300s (quorum min height 127, strict min 127, target 2000, tolerated_failures 1)` at `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs:1502`. Terminal summary: `successes=551 failures=17 expected_failures=0 unexpected_successes=0 izanami_ingress_failover_total=80 izanami_ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=52`. Signal shift versus the earlier failing baseline: no peer stack-overflow aborts (all peer stderr logs empty), but throughput still converged-then-stalled at height `127/127/127/127` with repeated workload `transaction queued for too long` / `status_timeout_ms=20s` errors and ongoing `missing_qc`/`no proposal observed` warnings in peer stdout. Remaining open: clear the converged stall at fixed height under queue-timeout pressure and rerun the canonical 3600s soak.

Latest sync (canonical 3600s soak rerun after no-roster hardening, 2026-02-22): reran the unchanged canonical Izanami command and captured artifacts at `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260222T043549_codex_3600s.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_EDoxPo`. The run failed early with `no block height progress for 300s (quorum min height 30, strict min 30, target 2000, tolerated_failures 1)` at `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs:1502` (exit code 1). Terminal summary: `successes=126 failures=18 expected_failures=0 unexpected_successes=0 izanami_ingress_failover_total=55 izanami_ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=48`. New dominant failure signal: two peers aborted with `sumeragi-tick` stack overflow (`aware_elver`, `prevalent_mammoth`), then ingress degraded with repeated endpoint `Connection refused`/timeout + `transaction queued for too long` warnings. Remaining open: fix the `sumeragi-tick` stack-overflow path under this profile, then rerun the canonical 3600s soak and compare divergence/progress/no-roster counters against the 2026-02-22 baseline.

Latest sync (no-roster starvation root-cause hardening pass, 2026-02-22): landed deterministic no-roster bootstrap liveness hardening in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop{.rs,/votes.rs,/commit.rs,/reschedule.rs,/tests.rs}` by introducing explicit bootstrap state (`RosterReady`/`RosterBootstrapPending`/`RosterBootstrapExhausted`), ordered bootstrap reacquire before fail-closed (highest-QC fetch, parent-gap fetch, anchor range-pull, commit-topology refresh), and one deterministic refresh-then-single-fallback allowance per view before strict fail-closed. No-roster fallback budgeting now derives from active timing (`effective_no_roster_fallback_views = clamp(1..cap, ceil(quorum_timeout/propose_timeout))`, capped by config) while preserving deterministic once-per-view/height limits. Torii status now exposes no-roster diagnosis counters (`consensus_no_roster_fallback_total`, `consensus_no_roster_fallback_allowed_total`, `consensus_no_roster_fail_closed_total`) in `crates/iroha_torii/src/routing/consensus.rs`. Izanami fast profile now explicitly sets deterministic no-roster recovery knobs in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` (`missing_qc_reacquire_window_ms=450`, `no_roster_fallback_views=2`, `no_roster_refresh_retry_per_view=2`). Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`; `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_liveness_state_machine_tests:: -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib no_roster_bootstrap_prevents_premature_fallback_suppression -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib force_view_change_if_idle_reacquires_missing_qc_once_before_rotating -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib unknown_prev_fallback_anchor_selects_deterministic_start_height -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p iroha_torii status_snapshot_json_includes_recovery_reacquire_fields -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p izanami derive_npos_timing_ -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_applies_pipeline_time -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_injects_npos_parameters -- --nocapture`. Remaining open: rerun the unchanged canonical 3600s Izanami soak and compare divergence/proposal/no-roster warning profiles against the 2026-02-22 baseline logs.

Latest sync (canonical 3600s soak rerun after sub-1s tuning tranche, 2026-02-22): reran the unchanged canonical Izanami command (`RUST_LOG=izanami::summary=info,izanami::workload=warn`, 4 peers, `--duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --tps 5 --max-inflight 8`) and captured artifacts at `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260222T001931_impl_3600s.log` and `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_I5J2ZU`. The run did not reach the 3600s horizon: strict divergence guard tripped early at `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs:1459` with `divergence=9` (`quorum min=13`, `strict min=4`, threshold `2` over `30s`). Terminal summary: `successes=26 failures=0 expected_failures=0 unexpected_successes=0 izanami_ingress_failover_total=7 izanami_ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=4`; peer stderr remained empty. Warning sample counts remained proposal/recovery-skew oriented (`no proposal observed for view before changing view=3`, `no-roster fallback budget exhausted=6`, signature mismatch indicators `0`). Remaining open: reduce early view/roster skew under low height so strict-min tracks quorum-min before throughput tuning can be meaningfully measured against the 3600s target.

Latest sync (sub-1s localnet tuning tranche implemented, 2026-02-21): updated Izanami fast-path consensus defaults in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` for the under-1s objective by setting NPoS timing to `180ms/180ms` (`block_time_ms`/`commit_time_ms`), lowering DA quorum multiplier to `1`, explicitly writing DA availability knobs (`availability_timeout_multiplier=1`, `availability_timeout_floor_ms=250`), tightening recovery windows (`height_window_ms=600`, `missing_qc_reacquire_window_ms=250`, `deferred_qc_ttl_ms=600`, `missing_block_height_ttl_ms=600`), and adding deterministic timeout floors (`propose>=40ms`, `prevote>=60ms`, `precommit>=80ms`) in `derive_npos_timing`. Validation passed: `cargo test -p izanami derive_npos_timing_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_applies_pipeline_time -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_injects_npos_parameters -- --nocapture`. Remaining open: rerun canonical 3600s soak and confirm p95 commit latency <1s with reduced missing-qc/proposal-starvation warnings.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet repeated-cycle scale-in assertion follow-up, 2026-02-22): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` so cycle execution can explicitly require deterministic `scale-in` transition deltas after contraction. `run_expand_contract_cycle` now takes a strictness flag; the single-cycle localnet test keeps strictness disabled for baseline regression coverage, while the repeated-cycle localnet test enables strictness and fails immediately when contraction appears in status but `scale-in` transitions remain absent on quorum peers. Validation: `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_gapfill cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok, `13 passed`), `TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_gapfill cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_expands_and_contracts_lanes_in_localnet -- --nocapture` (ok; `scale-in transitions 0/4`), and `... nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --nocapture` (fails in cycle 1 with explicit `scale-in transitions 0/4` quorum assertion). Remaining open: deterministic scale-in transition emission is still missing in localnet, blocking true multi-cycle expansion/contraction validation.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet transition-counter diagnostics follow-up, 2026-02-22): extended `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` with per-peer autoscale transition snapshots derived from peer `run-*-stdout.log` markers (`applied deterministic lane autoscale scale-out/scale-in transition`), surfaced those counters in expansion fallback/timeout diagnostics, and logged cycle-phase transition deltas after expansion/contraction. Also stabilized heartbeat waits by removing premature abort on transient status-snapshot streaks (timeout-scoped retries) and skipping cooldown-clearance gating for cycle 1. Added local unit coverage for transition-marker parsing and baseline delta semantics. Remaining open: functional autoscale lifecycle gap where deterministic scale-in transitions are not observed, preventing true contraction/re-expansion cycling.

Latest sync (Confidential localnet real backend proof coverage, 2026-02-22): updated `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` to replace debug proof placeholders with real `halo2/ipa` fixture attachments from `iroha_core::zk::test_utils::halo2_fixture_envelope("halo2/ipa:tiny-add-v1", ...)` for `ZkTransfer`/`Unshield` flows. Localnet bootstrap now explicitly enables Halo2 verification (`zk.halo2.enabled=true`) and sets `confidential.verify_timeout_ms=600000`. Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace/runtime-registration matrix follow-up, 2026-02-22): expanded stability validation with a 5x5 rerun matrix after soak timeout/retry hardening (`SOAK_PHASE_WAIT_TIMEOUT=32s`, `SOAK_ITERATION_ATTEMPTS=3`). Cross-dataspace matrix passed all `5/5` runs with strict soak `10/10` each run (`soak_fallbacks` range `3..8`, no soak retries, no swap non-convergence fallbacks). Runtime registration matrix passed all `5/5` runs with `pass_rate=100.0%`; submitter/cluster height-delta max remained `0` in every run and is now explicitly treated as expected behavior for control-plane `/v1/nexus/lifecycle` state mutation (`crates/iroha_torii/src/routing.rs`), not a missing-commit regression. Remaining non-functional gap: repeated `cargo test` loops can include intermittent in-run rebuild/startup overhead that inflates wall-clock timing outliers.

Latest sync (Taira remaining-gap closure for public localnet churn/load harness, 2026-02-21): completed the pending hardening in `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` by adding enforced lag-cycle thresholds (`IROHA_TAIRA_MAX_LAGGED_CYCLE_RATIO`, default `0.35`), adaptive settle/churn timing so short runs still execute churn (`effective_final_settle_window`, `initial_churn_delay`), committed/finalized TPS assertions from validator-min `txs_approved` deltas (`IROHA_TAIRA_MIN_COMMITTED_TPS_RATIO`, default `0.6`), strict all-validator final convergence after quorum convergence, and an absolute transient skew cap (`IROHA_TAIRA_MAX_TRANSIENT_HEIGHT_SKEW`, default `10`). Summary output now includes `committed_tps` and `committed_txs_min_delta`, and helper coverage was added for settle-window/churn-delay/lag-threshold/approved-tx helpers. Latest sync (Nexus autoscale repeated-cycle status-transition follow-up, 2026-02-22): extended `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` with baseline-aware expansion signals so status checks can detect lane transitions even when absolute counters are sparse: lane declaration transitions (`lane_governance`/lane presence) plus lane-progress transitions (lane status deltas, commitment `block_height`/counter deltas, and relay-envelope `block_height` deltas). Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet strict transition validation, 2026-02-21): reworked `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` to validate multilane expansion/contraction against dynamic commit quorum and observed runtime activity, not fixed thresholds. Added resilient status snapshot retries, lane-active checks using `capacity || committed`, storage-provisioning expansion signal, unique load payload sequencing (`LOAD_TX_SEQUENCE`), and per-cycle chain-progress gating to prevent false-positive repeated cycles. Added unit test coverage for storage expansion quorum semantics. Validation: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok, 4 passed), direct single-cycle localnet run `... target/debug/deps/mod-8c7afc1a90b9898d nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_expands_and_contracts_lanes_in_localnet --nocapture` with `IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=$PWD/target/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d KAGAMI_BIN=$PWD/target/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d)` (ok, ~59.33s), and direct repeated-cycle run `... nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet --nocapture` (ok, ~60.43s).

Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace strict-soak gap closure, 2026-02-21): finalized hardening in `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` after repeated-sweep failures by increasing setup retry tolerance (`SETUP_REGISTER_MINT_ATTEMPTS=3`) with barrier-timeout query fallback, adding bounded per-iteration soak retries (`SOAK_ITERATION_ATTEMPTS=2`) and higher soak wait budgets, classifying rollback `"transaction queued for too long"` as inconclusive for committed-history fallback, and deferring successful/reverse swap fallback barrier-timeout failures to strict balance assertions instead of immediate abort. Validation: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/irohad KAGAMI_BIN=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok, `287.01s`; soak `10/10`; health `setup_retries=0 swap_fallbacks=1 soak_fallbacks=10 soak_retries=0`) and `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok, `15.80s`; pass_rate `100.0%`). Pre-fix 3-run sweep captured and closed two concrete gaps: rollback misclassification on `"transaction queued for too long"` and premature failure on successful-swap fallback height-barrier timeout.

Latest sync (Confidential localnet transparent-only transfer policy coverage, 2026-02-21): extended `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` with `confidential_transfer_rejected_in_transparent_only_mode`, validating that `ZkTransfer` is rejected when `asset_mode=Hybrid` but transparent-only policy toggles disable shield/unshield operations (`allow_shield=false`, `allow_unshield=false`), with post-attempt public-balance invariants and chain-progress barriers. Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace tx-outcome confirmation + hybrid soak fallback hardening, 2026-02-21): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to use tx-entrypoint committed-outcome confirmation for setup grants and successful/reverse swaps (submit prebuilt tx -> wait committed outcome -> assert state), reducing reliance on pure height-proxy barriers. Soak confirmation is now hybrid: committed-outcome first with automatic fallback to height-tick barrier when outcome lookup is inconclusive, improving report-only soak stability under load. Rollback recovery keeps bounded-first behavior and committed-history reason lookup, with a final uncapped blocking submit only as a last resort when history lookup is also inconclusive, preserving strict underfunded reason assertions (`requires 10000`) and rollback balance invariants. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/irohad KAGAMI_BIN=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass, `394.26s`; soak pass_rate `100.0%`; rollback phase `19.881s`; total phase runtime `394.180s`) and `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass, `14.62s`; submit `142.547/167.024/198.926ms`; commit/apply `3.593/11.941/21.762ms`; visibility `3.955/12.103/26.960ms`; total `173.100/191.068/211.954ms`).

Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace barrierized swaps + bounded rollback-history fallback, 2026-02-21): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` so setup grants and successful/reverse swaps use enqueue + explicit barrier + strict state assertions (replacing capped blocking confirm waits on those paths), and changed rollback rejection recovery from a final uncapped blocking submit to bounded committed-history lookup (`ROLLBACK_HISTORY_RETRY_TIMEOUT=4s`, `ROLLBACK_HISTORY_FALLBACK_TIMEOUT=25s`) after bounded blocking retries. Added committed-rejection detail rendering (`Display + Debug`) so the strict `requires 10000` assertion remains deterministic. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/irohad KAGAMI_BIN=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass, `353.29s`; rollback phase `60.591s`, soak pass_rate `60.0%`) and `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass, `21.90s`; submit `219.201/284.929/333.192ms`, commit/apply `3.185/23.903/70.189ms`, visibility `5.447/15.731/33.587ms`, total `263.635/324.563/409.735ms`).

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet deterministic scale-out tuning, 2026-02-21): finalized `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` reliability hardening for multilane expansion/contraction by replacing aggressive adaptive top-up with deterministic burst cadence (`16 tx` every 10 heartbeats + `32 tx` reinforcement every 30 heartbeats) and tuning test-only autoscale thresholds to avoid latency-gate misses under slow startup (`scale_out_latency_ratio=0.2`, `scale_in_latency_ratio=0.15`, `scale_out_window_blocks=2`). Kept minimum-peer expansion convergence detection (3/4 peers) and contraction semantics checks unchanged. Validation: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/codex-test KAGAMI_BIN=$PWD/target/debug/kagami TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=$PWD/target/debug/irohad IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (2 passed), and clean skip-build 10-run stress sweep `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_expands_and_contracts_lanes_in_localnet -- --nocapture` looped 10x (log `/tmp/autoscale_finalcfg_10run_20260221T145529.log`) with `10/10` pass, including slow-start cases up to `39.666s` startup.

Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace blocking-confirmation cap + rollback reason fallback, 2026-02-21): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to cap confirmation waits (`BLOCKING_CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT=20s`) for setup grants and correctness-path swaps, treating timeout-class blocking outcomes as inconclusive where strict permission/balance assertions remain authoritative. For failing underfunded rollback, capped retries are explicitly limited to `ROLLBACK_CAPPED_ATTEMPTS=2` on fresh leader-targeted submitters before escalating to one final uncapped blocking fallback when both capped attempts are inconclusive, preserving strict rejection-reason verification (`requires 10000`) and rollback balance invariants. Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/irohad KAGAMI_BIN=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass, latest `377.68s`; setup-grants confirm capped to `20.839s` on inconclusive confirmation; rollback reason recovered via final uncapped fallback after two capped attempts; soak report-only pass_rate `50.0%`) and `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate `100.0%`; submit `199.929ms/1.027s/3.574s`; commit/apply `3.696/14.165/33.995ms`; visibility `2.861/24.075/68.907ms`; total `272.532ms/1.066s/3.630s`).

Latest sync (canonical 3600s soak rerun after low-load liveness fixes, 2026-02-21): reran canonical command with unchanged Izanami parameters and long-run target dir (`/tmp/izanami_soak_20260221T225206_impl_rerun.log`, network `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_Gmn4g9`) and observed a materially improved failure mode: no strict-divergence trip, peers stayed converged through the run (last live sample `1426/1426/1426/1426`), and all peer stderr logs stayed empty. Run still ended with `izanami run stopped before target blocks reached` (`crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs:1386`, exit code 1) with summary counters `successes=6839 failures=1 expected_failures=0 unexpected_successes=0 izanami_ingress_failover_total=27 izanami_ingress_endpoint_unhealthy_total=8`. One workload warning surfaced for duplicate `register_domain` (`chaos_child_342`) in retry path. Remaining open: close the throughput gap to reach `target-blocks=2000` within the fixed 3600s window while preserving the new no-divergence behavior.

Latest sync (low-load consensus liveness fix implementation, 2026-02-21): implemented the missing-QC liveness plan across config/core/status/torii/block-sync: new recovery knobs in `iroha_config` (`missing_qc_reacquire_window_ms`, `max_forced_proposal_attempts_per_view`, `no_roster_refresh_retry_per_view`, `rotate_after_reacquire_exhausted`), proposal-liveness state machine and bounded leader forced-proposal/watchdog paths in `sumeragi/main_loop`, bounded missing-QC reacquire defer/rotate flow, no-roster refresh-retry budgeting, deterministic unknown-prev block-sync fallback anchors, and status/torii exposure of recovery counters (including `consensus_forced_proposal_*`, `consensus_missing_qc_reacquire_exhausted_total`, `consensus_missing_qc_rotation_deferred_total`, `consensus_no_roster_refresh_retry_total`). Latest sync (Izanami throughput/recovery plan implementation, 2026-02-21): landed the next consensus recovery and soak-safety tuning across `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` and `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop{.rs,/qc.rs,/tests.rs}`. Izanami now writes stricter recovery knobs for canonical soaks (attempt caps/windows, hash-miss and range-pull escalation, retry backoff multiplier/cap, signer-fallback attempt bound), lowers NPoS timing to `400ms/600ms`, reduces RBC per-tick traffic while widening pacemaker backlog soft limits, and enforces a strict divergence guard (`quorum min - strict min > 2` for 30s => fail run) to surface unsafe lag before prolonged stalls. Core missing-block handling now applies exponential retry backoff per-attempt (capped) and escalates attempt streaks to range-pull before hitting hard-cap view-change thresholds. Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_applies_pipeline_time -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami divergence_state_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami make_network_builder_forwards_rust_log_and_sets_peer_base_level -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p izanami run_with_queue_timeout_retry -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core retry_missing_block_requests_backoff_scales_with_attempts_and_caps -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core missing_block_attempt_streak_escalates_to_range_pull_before_hard_cap -- --nocapture`. Latest sync (Izanami canonical soak post-recovery rerun + peer log-level mitigation, 2026-02-21): reran canonical soak `RUST_LOG=izanami::summary=info,izanami::workload=warn IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_KEEP_DIRS=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_izanami_longrun cargo run -p izanami --release --locked -- --allow-net --nexus --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable` (`/tmp/izanami_soak_20260221T173428_postplan.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_exjbWe`). Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace blocking-confirmation cap + rollback reason fallback, 2026-02-21): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to cap confirmation waits on correctness-path swap submissions (`BLOCKING_CONFIRMATION_TIMEOUT=20s`) and treat timeout-class blocking outcomes as inconclusive for successful/reverse swap submission, with strict balance convergence checks remaining authoritative. Membership join/leave checks now use validator-wide quorum visibility (`wait_for_peer_presence_across_clients` + `min_presence_matches`), final settle uses quorum convergence (`wait_for_cluster_convergence_quorum`), peer queries retry timeout-class errors (`PEER_QUERY_REQUEST_RETRIES=3`, `PEER_QUERY_REQUEST_RETRY_BACKOFF=200ms`), non-fatal interim convergence waits are bounded (`INTERIM_CONVERGENCE_TIMEOUT_SECS=45`), and leave-propagation lag is warning-only rather than aborting the run. Latest sync (Confidential localnet shielding/unshielding coverage, 2026-02-21): added `integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs` with `confidential_public_and_shielded_three_hop_localnet`, covering a 4-peer confidential-enabled localnet flow for (1) public asset shield -> three shielded hops -> unshield -> public transfer to `BOB_ID`, and (2) a second asset registered as confidential with three shielded hops. Added `confidential_unshield_rejected_when_disabled`, `confidential_shield_rejected_when_disabled`, `confidential_shield_rejected_without_zk_registration`, and `confidential_unshield_rejected_with_stale_root_hint` to cover policy denials (`allow_unshield=false`, `allow_shield=false`), missing zk registration, and stale root-hint rejection, all with post-attempt balance invariants after chain progress. The scenarios use all-peer transaction submission plus non-empty block-height progression waits to avoid single-peer pipeline-status confirmation stalls before balance assertions. Added helper detector tests for transient-network and duplicate-transaction classification and revalidated the full file run. Latest sync (Taira churn catch-up tolerance follow-up, 2026-02-21): extended `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` hardening by (1) keeping joiner progress-aware catch-up gating (`wait_for_height_or_progress`, `JoinerCatchupAssessment`, `JOINER_CATCHUP_TIMEOUT_SECS=60`, target=`baseline - INTERIM_CONVERGENCE_MAX_SKEW`) and (2) relaxing process-restart catch-up to a skew-tolerant target via `validator_restart_catchup_target(baseline)` with non-fatal lag logging, avoiding false failures when a restarted validator trails by 1-2 blocks under aggressive churn. Latest sync (Taira status-timeout retry follow-up, 2026-02-21): updated `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` so validator status sampling retries timeout-class `/status` errors per peer (`collect_statuses` now uses `get_status_with_retry`, with `STATUS_REQUEST_RETRIES=3` and `STATUS_REQUEST_RETRY_BACKOFF=200ms`) instead of failing immediately on a single transient timeout. Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace barrier follow-up + benchmark revalidation, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to keep concurrent `ds1`/`ds2` route probes while removing redundant Bob-height waiting from setup grants (permission visibility query remains the propagation gate), tightened balance/permission tick cadence (`10 -> 5` polls), and moved successful/reverse swap assertions to tick-assisted balance convergence. Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace route-probe stabilization, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to replace flaky `sumeragi_status.dataspace_commitments` probe checks with pipeline-event routing assertions at `TransactionStatus::Queued` (lane/dataspace), while still waiting for `Approved` to enforce commit progression. This keeps both explicit `ds1`/`ds2` probes and removes observer-dependent commitment races; a short SSE handshake delay was added before probe submit for queued-event reliability. Revalidated full swap semantics and soak path with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/irohad KAGAMI_BIN=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling/debug/kagami IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate `100.0%`, total `285.317s`, soak min/avg/max `9.421/13.040/20.123s`) and reran registration isolation with `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate `100.0%`, total min/avg/max `149.723/203.065/320.872ms`).

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet load-tuning + startup attribution, 2026-02-21): kept `LOAD_TX_COUNT=48` in `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs`, added per-phase timing instrumentation, and revalidated repeatedly. Long-tail reruns still pass but show high variance in build-enabled mode (`225.25s`, `166.35s`, `208.13s`, `376.59s`, `605.31s`, `665.16s`, `532.62s`), with phase logs attributing most variance to startup (`network startup: 609.021s`, expansion/contraction single-digit seconds). After rebuilding local binaries (`cargo build -p irohad -p iroha_kagami`) and running with `IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1` plus explicit binary paths (`KAGAMI_BIN`/`TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD`), autoscale localnet stayed green with much lower startup/runtime (`network startup 16.415s`, total `22.726s`; steady-state rerun `network startup 14.491s`, total `25.268s`, wall `56s`; additional consecutive reruns `network startup 12.663s`, total `25.644s`, and `network startup 12.816s`, total `26.434s`; latest rerun `network startup 12.201s`, expansion `6.234s`, total `18.577s`). Additional skip-build stress evidence: one 5-run batch showed 4 passes + 1 expansion-timeout flake (`/tmp/autoscale_repeat_20260221T114131.log`, failed run at `135.19s` waiting for lane-1 provisioning), immediate rerun passed (`startup 12.572s`, `expansion 8.331s`, total `21.191s`), and a 3-run follow-up batch passed 3/3 (`/tmp/autoscale_followup_20260221T115600.log`, total min/avg/max `19.359/21.373/22.926s`). Post-flake hardening: `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` now injects deterministic top-up load while awaiting expansion (every 10 heartbeat ticks, 16 tx burst), includes unit coverage (`nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests::expansion_top_up_cadence_is_deterministic`), and revalidated cleanly over 3/3 post-change reruns (`/tmp/autoscale_post_topup_20260221T120319.log`, startup min/avg/max `12.164/12.822/13.215s`, expansion `6.079/6.100/6.112s`, total `18.390/19.030/19.425s`) plus a post-change 5-run sweep with 5/5 pass (`/tmp/autoscale_post_topup_5run_20260221T120932.log`, startup min/avg/max `12.276/12.400/12.636s`, expansion `7.154/8.836/10.532s`, total `19.542/21.429/23.195s`). A larger post-topup 10-run sweep showed `9/10` pass (`/tmp/autoscale_post_topup_10run_20260221T122538.log`) with one expansion-timeout where 3/4 peers had already converged on elastic-lane storage/status; follow-up hardening now treats that deterministic minimum-peer convergence as expansion success (3/4) and adds helper coverage (`nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests::expansion_observed_when_minimum_peers_converge`). Post-threshold rerun is `5/5` pass (`/tmp/autoscale_post_threshold_5run_20260221T125344.log`, total min/avg/max `18.567/62.845/112.852s`), and a final post-threshold stress sweep is `10/10` pass (`/tmp/autoscale_post_threshold_10run_20260221T130806.log`, startup min/avg/max `12.252/12.530/13.616s`, expansion `5.084/6.014/7.120s`, total `17.457/18.646/20.824s`). Also fixed a compile unblock in `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` by using the existing runtime binding (`let (network, rt) = ...`) for route-probe waits.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet hardening + pacemaker clamp, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` so expansion is asserted by provisioned Kura lanes (`1 -> 2`) while contraction is asserted by runtime lane deactivation (`lane 0` active, `lane 1` removed or `capacity=0`), and added short Torii request deadlines for autoscale probes to bound stalled `/status`/heartbeat calls. Latest sync (consensus recovery continuation, 2026-02-20 late): closed remaining `iroha_core` compile/test blockers in the deterministic recovery sweep (`main_loop.rs`, `votes.rs`, `tests.rs`) by fixing borrow-ordering around missing-block fetch planning, stabilizing sidecar mismatch prune retention, and hardening idle view-change recovery regression setup while preserving missing-block pending behavior. Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_consensusfix cargo check -p iroha_core -p iroha_config -p iroha_p2p -p iroha_telemetry`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_consensusfix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib missing_block_height_ -- --nocapture`, `... --lib missing_block_hash_miss_streak_escalates_to_range_pull ...`, `... --lib sidecar_mismatch_final_drop_trips_after_retry_cap ...`, `... --lib force_view_change_if_idle_triggers_even_when_missing_blocks_pending ...`, `... --lib quarantined_block_sync_qc_expiry_is_bounded ...`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_consensusfix cargo test -p iroha_p2p --lib deferred_queue_ -- --nocapture`, and `... --lib missing_session_ -- --nocapture` (all green). Follow-up: canonical 3600s Izanami soak was relaunched with the fixed command and reached peer-directory bootstrap output; final pass/fail artifact is still pending completion.

Latest sync (Sumeragi stack-overflow hardening, 2026-02-20): removed default-stack risk on consensus worker threads by introducing a shared internal `sumeragi_thread_builder` in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/mod.rs` and routing all Sumeragi thread spawns through it (`sumeragi`, `sumeragi-tick`, queue workers, commit/validation/QC verify/vote verify, RBC persist/seed). Added regression unit coverage for the new helper. Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_stackfix cargo check -p iroha_core --lib`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_stackfix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib sumeragi_thread_builder_applies_requested_thread_name -- --nocapture`.

Latest sync (Izanami canonical 3600s rerun, 2026-02-20T20:23 local): reran the fixed soak command with unchanged parameters and captured logs at `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260220T202356_rerun.log` (network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_cljHEm`). The run bootstrapped and peers progressed to `~485`, then entered a persistent lagging split `485/485/485/473` where `advanced_onager` stopped committing (`last commit height=473 @ 2026-02-20T16:42:45Z`) while other peers held at `485` (`~16:43:29Z`). `advanced_onager` then spammed `telemetry sync failed; returning last metrics snapshot` (34 sampled) with no new commits. Recovery activity remained present (`missing_block_retry`, range-pull requests, occasional height hard-cap logs), but Izanami emitted neither progress summaries nor the expected `progress-timeout` failure line; the attempt had to be manually terminated after stall duration exceeded the configured 300s window. Remaining open: fix this harness-visible stuck mode (timeout not surfacing to Izanami output) and the lagging-peer stall itself, then rerun the canonical soak for a definitive pass/fail artifact.

Latest sync (deterministic round topology follow-up, 2026-02-20): removed remaining hash-coupled roster selection for active/future rounds by adding `canonical_round_roster_with_mode` in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` and routing `roster_for_vote_with_mode` / `roster_for_new_view_with_mode` through canonical height-based derivation (commit topology, activation roster, commit-QC roll-forward without target hash). Updated proposal leader-index derivation in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/proposal_handlers.rs` to use the same canonical round roster and restricted hash-coupled sidecar roster replay in `BlockCreated` processing to historical committed heights only. Added deterministic assertions in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs::conflicting_vote_does_not_override_first` so competing hashes at the same `(height, view)` resolve identical commit/new-view rosters. Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib conflicting_vote_does_not_override_first -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_updates_leader_index_status -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib proposal_missing_highest_qc_triggers_missing_block_fetch -- --nocapture`. Remaining open: rerun the canonical 3600s soak and >=4-peer induced-failure integration matrix to confirm convergence bounds under lagging-peer churn.

Latest sync (consensus recovery stabilization broad sweep, 2026-02-20): implemented the aggressive deterministic recovery pass across `iroha_core`/`iroha_config`/`iroha_telemetry`/`iroha_p2p`: height-scoped missing-block budgets with hard-cap/range-pull escalation, fail-closed sidecar mismatch quarantine/final-drop state, dependency-gated empty-topology recovery, and proposal gating while missing dependencies are unresolved. Added/validated focused regressions for hash churn, view churn, recovery-budget clear-on-success, hash-miss range-pull escalation, sidecar quarantine gating/final-drop, and deferred-send reconnect burst stability (`missing_session_deferred_burst_schedules_single_reconnect`). Validation passed: `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --no-run`, targeted `iroha_core` recovery tests (7 new/updated cases), `cargo test -p iroha_p2p --no-run`, `cargo test -p iroha_p2p deferred_queue_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_p2p missing_session_defers_frame_and_schedules_reconnect -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_p2p missing_session_deferred_burst_schedules_single_reconnect -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_config`. Remaining open: rerun canonical 3600s soak and targeted >=4-peer integration scenarios to confirm lagging-peer convergence bounds (`no progress for 300s` and `>2 blocks behind for >60s` gates).

Latest sync (Nexus soak submission-loop overhead trim, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` soak path to (1) reuse a leader-targeted submitter with refresh every 3 iterations (`SOAK_SUBMITTER_REFRESH_EVERY`) and per-iteration `target-refresh` telemetry, and (2) replace per-iteration status polling with a rolling `next_soak_target_height` barrier target derived from the previous iteration’s synced height. This removes one status RPC per soak iteration while preserving barrier correctness and balance assertions. Validation passed: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing --no-run` and post-change full run `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_opt_rolling NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate 100.0%, soak min/avg/max `5.301/8.305/14.249s`, barrier `3.487/7.689/14.237s`, target-refresh `0.000/0.531/1.987s`; startup `848.900s` due one-time in-run `irohad --release` build); prior passing benchmark run for refresh telemetry remains the low-contention reference (`IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`: pass_rate 100.0%, soak min/avg/max `3.254/5.895/10.921s`, barrier `3.156/5.690/10.596s`, target-refresh `0.000/0.087/0.395s`) and runtime benchmark stayed green (`IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`: pass_rate 100.0%, total min/avg/max `57.898/60.369/62.530ms`).

Latest sync (Taira stability hardening follow-up III, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` to (1) keep unrecovering-skew detection + queue-timeout retry submits, (2) reduce membership churn cadence to one event per `churn_interval` (offset start), and (3) raise default stall timeout via `IROHA_TAIRA_STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS=300` while retaining configurable overrides. Updated `integrated_test_framework.md` optional knobs for the new stall-timeout default. Validation passed: `cargo check -p integration_tests --test taira_public_localnet`, `cargo test -p integration_tests --test taira_public_localnet simulation_config_defaults_are_valid -- --nocapture`, `KAGAMI_BIN=$PWD/target/debug/kagami TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=$PWD/target/debug/irohad IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 IROHA_TAIRA_SIM_DURATION_SECS=120 IROHA_TAIRA_CHURN_INTERVAL_SECS=30 cargo test -p integration_tests --test taira_public_localnet taira_public_localnet_5tps_churn_stability -- --ignored --nocapture`, and `KAGAMI_BIN=$PWD/target/debug/kagami TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=$PWD/target/debug/irohad IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 IROHA_TAIRA_SIM_DURATION_SECS=900 cargo test -p integration_tests --test taira_public_localnet taira_public_localnet_5tps_churn_stability -- --ignored --nocapture` (simulation validations passed with non-fatal joiner catch-up lag warnings).

Latest sync (Nexus leader-target freshness fallback, 2026-02-20): updated `leader_or_highest_height_peer_index(...)` in both `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` and `integration_tests/tests/nexus/runtime_dataspace_registration_perf.rs` so the reported leader is only selected when its observed height is within one block of the status probe height; otherwise submissions fall back to highest-height peer targeting. Validation passed: `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture` and `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture`.

Latest sync (Nexus soak barrier consolidation, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` to remove per-iteration Bob propagation waits inside the 10-iteration soak and keep one final `soak final bob sync barrier` after the soak loop. This preserves cross-peer propagation coverage while removing repeated barrier round-trips from the throughput sample path. Validation passed: `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate 100%, soak min/avg/max `3.443/5.853/9.383s`).

Latest sync (Nexus route-probe + soak attribution telemetry, 2026-02-20): expanded `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` diagnostics so shared route-probe waits now emit per-dataspace first-seen commitment timing/height (`[route-probe] ds1/ds2 first_seen ...`) and soak output now includes per-iteration `submit`/`barrier`/`query` min/avg/max buckets for direct bottleneck attribution. Validation passed: `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate 100%, route-probe `ds1 first_seen=25.271s`, `ds2 first_seen=7.065s`; soak total min/avg/max `5.563/9.969/14.117s`, submit `0.014/0.061/0.215s`, barrier `2.626/6.762/10.560s`, query `0.006/0.062/0.201s`).

Latest sync (Nexus soak bucket telemetry, 2026-02-20): extended `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` soak reporting to include per-iteration `submit`/`barrier`/`query` min/avg/max buckets in addition to end-to-end soak latency, improving bottleneck attribution during throughput regressions. Validation passed: `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (pass_rate 100%, total min/avg/max `4.756/7.711/10.969s`, submit `0.003/0.033/0.091s`, barrier `2.604/6.267/9.535s`, query `0.005/0.031/0.071s`).

Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace query-overhead trim, 2026-02-20): removed redundant post-wait balance re-queries in `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` (successful/reverse/failing swap phases) so correctness checks rely on the existing `wait_for_expected_balances(...)` barriers without duplicate asset fetches. Revalidated both nexus tracks. Validation passed: `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture` and `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture`. Follow-up experiment: tighter tick cadence (5 polls) increased soak latency and was reverted to 10 polls.

Latest sync (Nexus route-probe shared barrier completion, 2026-02-20): finalized `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` probe refactor so both explicit `ds1`/`ds2` route probes submit in one enqueue phase and synchronize via one `wait_for_dataspace_commitments(...)` barrier, while keeping separate probe assertions/timing and unchanged swap rollback semantics. Revalidated both nexus tracks with current metrics. Validation passed: `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture` and `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture`.

Latest sync (Nexus autoscale localnet regression coverage, 2026-02-20): added `nexus.autoscale` config defaults/parsing and deterministic block-apply autoscale transitions in core, plus a new localnet regression `integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` that asserts lane expansion (burst load) and contraction (heartbeat under low utilization) by polling active Kura lane segments on all peers. Scoped validation passed: `cargo check -p iroha_core -p iroha_config -p integration_tests`, `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_expands_and_contracts_lanes_in_localnet -- --nocapture` (sandbox loopback bind restriction auto-skipped startup and test passed); full `cargo test -p integration_tests ...` remains blocked by unrelated `offline_allowance_security` compile errors.

Latest sync (Taira localnet stability hardening follow-up III, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/taira_public_localnet.rs` to combine max-height stall liveness checks, unrecovering-skew detection (skew breach + lagging-min-height no-progress age), queue-timeout retryable membership submits, and reduced membership churn cadence (one event per `churn_interval`, offset-started). Defaults now use `IROHA_TAIRA_MAX_HEIGHT_SKEW_GRACE_SECS=30` and `IROHA_TAIRA_STALL_TIMEOUT_SECS=300`; runbook defaults in `integrated_test_framework.md` were updated accordingly. Added focused helper unit coverage (`skew_breach_is_not_unrecovering_when_min_height_progresses_recently`, `skew_breach_is_unrecovering_when_duration_and_min_age_exceed_grace`, `queue_timeout_error_classifier_matches_expected_message`). Latest sync (Nexus runtime peer-visibility polling refinement, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/runtime_dataspace_registration_perf.rs` to measure all-peer visibility via round-robin polling across pending peers (excluding submitter), removing sequential per-peer wait bias from propagation metrics. Added explicit output note when submitter commit height does not advance during lifecycle visibility. Latest sync (Nexus cross-dataspace follow-up tuning, 2026-02-20): updated `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_localnet.rs` and `integration_tests/tests/nexus/runtime_dataspace_registration_perf.rs` to improve performance attribution and reduce avoidable runtime overhead. Cross-dataspace soak now pipelines forward/reverse swaps with `submit_all` + one barrier per iteration, balance assertions use targeted `FindAssetById`, per-poll validator activation tick txs were removed, and dataspace probe tick txs are throttled (every 10 polls) to keep probe reliability with lower noise. Runtime benchmark now submits one lifecycle plan to a leader-targeted peer per iteration and reports distinct `submit`, `commit/apply`, `all-peer visibility`, and `total` metrics with ms/us formatting plus submitter height-delta stats. Validation passed: `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::runtime_dataspace_registration_perf::runtime_nexus_registration_reports_lane_lifecycle_costs -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` and `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::cross_dataspace_localnet::cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`.

Latest sync (IrohaSwift settlement execution status API, 2026-02-20): added `ToriiSettlementExecutionStatus` plus `ToriiClient.getSettlementStatus(bundleIdHex:)` / `getOfflineSettlementStatus(bundleIdHex:)` (async + completion) in `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift` to classify offline settlement bundles as settled/pending/rejected/unknown. The SDK now caches submit outcomes by bundle id so accepted submits surface as `pending` until a settlement row appears, and reject-code submit failures surface as `rejected(reason:)`; rejected settlement rows also resolve reason from payload or revocation-query fallback. Added endpoint tests in `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests.swift` for settled/pending/rejected/unknown outcomes and reject/revocation reason resolution. Validation passed: `swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests`. Full-suite `swift test` still reports pre-existing `TransactionParityFixturesTests` mismatches outside this change.

Latest sync (Izanami 3600s soak rerun, 2026-02-20, second run): reran the canonical long soak (`RUST_LOG=izanami::summary=info,izanami::workload=warn IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_KEEP_DIRS=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_izanami_longrun cargo run -p izanami --release --locked -- --allow-net --nexus --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`) and still failed with `no block height progress for 300s (min height 670, target 2000)` (log: `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260220T162157.log`, network dir: `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_7CWNyy`). Failure remained lagging-peer concentrated: `sisterly_owlet` retained tiered-state manifests at heights `668/669` while peers held `678/679`, with bounded but persistent recovery churn (`empty commit topology`=135, `roster sidecar mismatch`=133, `missing block fetch retry`=428) and no deterministic hard-cap escalation log (`missing-block retry attempts exceeded deterministic cap`=0). Remaining open: make missing-block hard-cap escalation and sidecar-mismatch final-drop paths trigger reliably under this soak profile so lagging peers converge instead of sustained bounded retries.

Latest sync (Izanami 3600s soak rerun, 2026-02-20): reran the canonical long soak (`RUST_LOG=izanami::summary=info,izanami::workload=warn IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_KEEP_DIRS=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_izanami_longrun cargo run -p izanami --release --locked -- --allow-net --nexus --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable`) and still failed with `no block height progress for 300s (min height 360, target 2000)` (log: `/tmp/izanami_soak_20260220T143914.log`, network dir: `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_RW1dQX`). Three peers committed through height `364` by `2026-02-20T10:58:59Z`, while lagging peer `learning_chinook` stopped committing at height `360` (`10:58:30Z`) and entered sustained bounded-retry churn: `deferring consensus path: empty commit topology` (3686), `ignoring roster sidecar with mismatched hash` (7993), and `retrying missing block fetch` (9438), alongside workload backpressure warnings (`transaction queued for too long`, tx confirmation timeout). Remaining open: tighten roster-sidecar mismatch quarantine/final-drop path and missing-block retry escalation so the lagging peer deterministically converges instead of cycling in high-volume bounded retries under this soak profile.

Latest sync (consensus empty-topology + deferred-send hardening, 2026-02-20): completed bounded empty-commit-topology recovery in Sumeragi (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` + companions) with deterministic staged escalation (topology refresh, targeted block-sync fetch, bounded wait, forced view change), per-round retry/age bounds, and defer-log cooldown suppression; wired new status/telemetry counters (`consensus_empty_commit_topology_defer_total`, `consensus_empty_commit_topology_escalation_total`, `consensus_recovery_state_transitions_total{state=*}`); and bounded deferred roster-QC replay with TTL/attempt-driven fetch + expiry escalation. Completed P2P deferred-send bundle in `crates/iroha_p2p/src/network.rs` with unit coverage for queue ordering/generation markers, TTL/cap drops, and reconnect scheduling; updated p2p integration config fixtures for new `deferred_send_ttl`/`deferred_send_max_per_peer` fields. Validation passed: `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib deferred_roster_qc_expiry_is_bounded -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib empty_commit_topology_recovery_escalates_after_bounded_retries -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib missing_block_fetch_counters_surface_in_snapshot -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_p2p deferred_queue_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_p2p missing_session_defers_frame_and_schedules_reconnect -- --nocapture`. Remaining open: rerun the canonical 3600s soak to verify no 300s progress stalls and confirm lagging-peer recovery bounds end-to-end.

Latest sync (consensus latency recovery hardening, 2026-02-20): implemented bounded deferred-QC missing-payload recovery and bounded block-sync QC quarantine/revalidation without changing quorum/lock semantics. Added `DeferredQcEntry` and `QuarantinedQcCandidate` paths in Sumeragi (`qc.rs`, `block_sync.rs`) with TTL/cap/attempt bounds, targeted missing-block fetch escalation, and deterministic view-change fallback. Added new status/Prometheus counters (`blocksync_qc_quarantine_total`, `blocksync_qc_revalidated_total`, `blocksync_qc_final_drop_total{reason=*}`, `qc_deferred_missing_payload_total`, `qc_deferred_resolved_total`, `qc_deferred_expired_total`) with focused regression tests for bounded expiry paths and snapshot surfacing. Validation passed: `cargo check -p iroha_core -p iroha_telemetry`; targeted tests `deferred_missing_payload_qc_expiry_is_bounded`, `quarantined_block_sync_qc_expiry_is_bounded`, `block_sync_counters_surface_in_snapshot`, `missing_block_fetch_counters_surface_in_snapshot`.

Latest sync (offline petal katakana distance-safe + realtime simulation, 2026-02-20): updated `crates/iroha_cli/src/offline/petal.rs` and `docs/source/petal_stream.md` so katakana-base94 keeps explicit `--channel` routing with binary fallback while adding `--katakana-preset {balanced|distance-safe}` auto-defaults when grid/chunk are not forced (`balanced`: `chunk_size=176`, min grid `41`; `distance-safe`: `chunk_size=96`, min grid `33`). Added `offline petal simulate-realtime` to emulate frame-by-frame live camera decoding (capture perturbation, channel-aware decode, assembler progression, timeline/report metrics, optional payload write). Added regression coverage for distance-safe preset resolution and deterministic realtime katakana reconstruction.

Latest sync (ZK audit remediation gap closure, 2026-02-19): closed the remaining Torii/Halo2 hardening items by persisting and querying a report-summary index in `crates/iroha_torii/src/zk_prover.rs` (list/count/delete/GC no longer require full report scans), adding bounded report-file reads (`REPORT_FILE_MAX_BYTES`), enforcing attachment metadata scan caps with explicit `429` on over-budget scans and tri-state legacy tag fallback in `crates/iroha_torii/src/zk_attachments.rs`, and keeping limited Rayon pool cache eviction bounded in `crates/iroha_zkp_halo2/src/lib.rs` (clippy-clean). Targeted checks passed before unrelated workspace blockers: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_gapfix cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib report_index_tracks_save_and_delete -- --nocapture`, `... load_report_rejects_oversized_report_file ...`, `... count_reports_filters_using_report_summaries ...`, `... attachment_meta_tag_match_backfills_legacy_tags ...`, `... attachment_meta_tag_match_marks_inconclusive_after_legacy_cap ...`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_gapfix cargo clippy -p iroha_zkp_halo2 --features parallel --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_gapfix cargo test -p iroha_zkp_halo2 --features parallel limited_pool_cache_is_bounded -- --nocapture`. Follow-up status: unrelated compile blockers from that snapshot were closed in the 2026-02-19 follow-up entry above; remaining work is environment-only (free disk for broader clippy sweep).

Latest sync (offline escrow reclaim + authority hardening, 2026-02-19): implemented controller-driven post-expiry recovery and escrow-source transfer hardening across data model/core/executor, added `OfflineAllowanceReclaimed` event/filter handling, rejected direct transfers from offline escrow source accounts, added reclaim and escrow-source rejection regressions, and updated `docs/source/offline_allowance.md`.

Latest sync (Torii MCP transaction/connect alias hardening, 2026-02-19): tightened agent ergonomics for transaction polling and Connect session flows in `crates/iroha_torii/src/mcp.rs`. `iroha.transactions.status` now accepts `transaction_hash` aliases consistently (including `query.transaction_hash`) and canonicalizes query keys before dispatch so agents can poll `/v1/pipeline/transactions/status` with either hash field. Explorer detail aliases now also accept nested `path.transaction_hash` for `iroha.transactions.get` and `iroha.instructions.get` (in addition to `path.hash` and top-level shortcuts). Connect flows now accept `session_id` aliases alongside `sid` for `connect.ws.ticket`, `connect.session.create`, `connect.session.create_and_ticket`, and `connect.session.delete` (including `path.sid` and `path.session_id` for delete), and Connect schemas now enforce required alias groups (`connect.ws.ticket` requires `role` plus `sid`/`session_id`; `connect.session.delete` requires `sid`/`session_id`/`path`, with nested `path.sid`/`path.session_id` validation). Added focused unit coverage (`ws_ticket_accepts_session_id_alias`, `build_connect_session_create_body_accepts_session_id_shortcut`, `extract_optional_transaction_hash_argument_accepts_query_alias_shortcut`, `extract_connect_sid_argument_accepts_path_session_id_alias`, `extract_transaction_hash_argument_accepts_path_alias_shortcut`), endpoint coverage in `crates/iroha_torii/tests/mcp_endpoints.rs` (`mcp_jsonrpc_tools_call_agent_alias_transaction_status_accepts_transaction_hash_alias`, `mcp_jsonrpc_tools_call_agent_alias_transaction_wait_accepts_query_transaction_hash_alias`, `mcp_jsonrpc_tools_call_agent_alias_transactions_get_accepts_path_transaction_hash_alias`, `mcp_jsonrpc_tools_call_agent_alias_instructions_get_accepts_path_transaction_hash_alias`, lifecycle/create-ticket tests updated to use `session_id` aliases including nested delete-path alias), and docs updates in `crates/iroha_torii/docs/mcp_api.md`. Latest sync (Kura/WSV hardening implementation, 2026-02-19): implemented the first remediation tranche from `docs/source/kura_wsv_security_performance_audit_20260219.md`. `crates/iroha_core/src/kura.rs` now reports writer IO failures without panic (writer fault state + `Error::BlockWriterFaulted`) and added incremental pending-budget accounting on enqueue/persist paths; `crates/iroha_core/src/state.rs` now handles DA cursor advancement failures in `apply_without_execution` without panicking; `crates/ivm/src/mock_wsv.rs` now enforces envelope-size limits and caller permission checks for JSON admin aliases (`ManageRoles`, `ManagePermissions`, `ManageTriggers`, `ManagePeers`) with new unit/integration regressions. Validation passed via targeted `iroha_core` and `ivm` tests under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_kura_wsv_harden` (`store_block_reports_writer_fault`, `replace_top_block_reports_writer_fault`, `apply_without_execution_persists_da_shard_cursor_journal_in_background`, `wsv_host_roles_triggers_envelope`, `wsv_host_execute_query_envelope`, `input_publish_tlv_rejects_oversized_envelope`, `execute_instruction_rejects_oversized_json_envelope`, `parse_manage_permissions_variants_ok`). Remaining Kura/WSV open work from the audit: reduce eviction lock hold time/full-file rewrite latency and shorten coarse `view_lock` commit critical sections.

Latest sync (ZK audit remediation implementation, 2026-02-19): implemented the planned ZK security/performance hardening across Torii/IVM/data-model/Halo2 surfaces. `crates/iroha_torii/src/zk_prover.rs` now sanitizes report IDs end-to-end, rejects invalid report IDs on app API routes, bounds report scans (`REPORT_SCAN_MAX_FILES`), and tightens background scan scheduling (bounded in-flight joins + pre-spawn permit acquisition). `crates/iroha_torii/src/zk_attachments.rs` now stores ingest-time `zk1_tags` metadata and uses bounded legacy body scanning (`TAG_FILTER_LEGACY_SCAN_CAP`) for tag filters. `crates/ivm/src/zk.rs` now rejects cross-chunk memory writes during `verify_trace` replay; `crates/iroha_data_model/src/proof.rs` now applies explicit caps to len-prefixed decode fields; `crates/iroha_zkp_halo2/src/lib.rs` now caches limited Rayon pools for batch verification. Validation passed via targeted tests: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo test -p ivm verify_trace_rejects_cross_chunk_memory_writes -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_torii get_report_rejects_invalid_id -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib delete_report_rejects_invalid_id -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib zk1_extract_tags_collects_tlv_tags -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_data_model take_len_prefixed_slice_rejects_fields_beyond_cap -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test -p iroha_data_model proofbox_decode_rejects_oversized_len_prefixed_payloads -- --nocapture` (all ok). Remaining open follow-up: run full `cargo test --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` for end-to-end regression sweep when CI budget allows.

Latest sync (offline settlement atomicity hardening, 2026-02-19): tightened `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs` so `SubmitOfflineToOnlineTransfer` applies allowance/commitment/counter mutation and writes transfer audit records only after escrow-backed deposit credit succeeds, eliminating the partial-failure path where escrow rejection could still decrement `remaining_amount`. Hardened `credit_deposit_account` with destination-account preflight and refund-on-deposit-failure behavior so failed credits cannot silently debit escrow. Added regressions `submit_transfer_rejects_insufficient_escrow_without_mutating_allowance_or_credit` and `credit_deposit_account_rejects_missing_deposit_without_escrow_debit`; revalidated related replay/escrow regressions and integration coverage. Validation passed: `rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_insufficient_escrow_without_mutating_allowance_or_credit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_duplicate_bundle_without_mutating_allowance -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_replayed_counter_without_credit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib credit_deposit_account_rejects_insufficient_escrow_without_credit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib credit_deposit_account_rejects_missing_deposit_without_escrow_debit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p integration_tests --test offline_allowance_security -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`.

Latest sync (Kura/WSV security-performance audit, 2026-02-19): completed exhaustive audit coverage for `crates/iroha_core/src/kura.rs`, production WSV paths in `crates/iroha_core/src/state.rs`, and IVM test/dev WSV host paths in `crates/ivm/src/mock_wsv.rs`; report captured in `docs/source/kura_wsv_security_performance_audit_20260219.md`. Priority follow-ups to schedule: replace Kura writer panic-on-IO with recoverable handling, reduce full-file eviction lock hold/latency, add incremental Kura budget accounting on enqueue, bound mock-host TLV/JSON envelope sizes, and add explicit permission checks (or strict feature-gating) for mock WSV JSON admin aliases.

Latest sync (offline settlement spend-safety hardening, 2026-02-18): added an early duplicate-bundle rejection in `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs` so replayed `bundle_id`s are rejected before proof/sig processing and before any settlement-path allowance mutations. Added `credit_deposit_account_rejects_insufficient_escrow_without_credit`, `submit_transfer_rejects_duplicate_bundle_without_mutating_allowance`, and `submit_transfer_rejects_replayed_counter_without_credit` to lock in no-credit/no-debit behavior for rejected settlement paths (escrow underfunding, bundle replay, and counter replay). Validation passed: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib credit_deposit_account_rejects_insufficient_escrow_without_credit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_duplicate_bundle_without_mutating_allowance -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_replayed_counter_without_credit -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_revoked_verdict -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_delta_mismatch -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p integration_tests --test offline_allowance_security -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`.

Latest sync (offline allowance revocation + test harness hardening, 2026-02-18): tightened `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs` so settlement rejects allowances whose `verdict_id` is present in `offline_verdict_revocations`, preserving no-side-effect rejection semantics; added/updated regression coverage in `integration_tests/tests/offline_allowance_security.rs` (non-controller + missing-escrow rejection) and fixed offline allowance genesis setup in `integration_tests/tests/address_canonicalisation.rs` by pre-minting the controller asset and enabling offline metadata so stricter register-time checks pass. Validation passed: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib refresh_after_expiry_is_rejected -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib register_allowance_rejects_future_certificate -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib register_allowance_rejects_expired_certificate -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p iroha_core --lib submit_transfer_rejects_revoked_verdict -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p integration_tests --test offline_allowance_security -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_tmp_security_fix cargo test -p integration_tests --test address_canonicalisation offline_allowances_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`.

Latest sync (Izanami 3600s soak + block-sync fallback anchor, 2026-02-18): full long-run validation `cargo run -p izanami --release --locked -- --allow-net --nexus --peers 4 --faulty 0 --duration 3600s --target-blocks 2000 --progress-interval 10s --progress-timeout 300s --tps 5 --max-inflight 8 --workload-profile stable` (with `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_KEEP_DIRS=1`, `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=2`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_izanami_longrun`) failed at `min height 1217` due `no block height progress for 300s`. Peer heights diverged (`1239/1239/1239/1217`) and late-stage logs showed renewed `no proposal observed before cutoff` plus `commit quorum missing past timeout` on the straggler, alongside sustained `qc_missing_payload` and block-sync lock conflicts. Implemented a follow-up in `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs`: unknown-`prev_hash` responses now anchor from requester `latest_hash` when known (`unknown_prev_fallback_start_height`) instead of always sharing from genesis, reducing repeated `Block hash not found; sharing from genesis` churn on lagging peers. Added unit coverage (`unknown_prev_fallback_uses_known_latest_hash_height`, `unknown_prev_fallback_none_when_latest_hash_unknown`) and validated with `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib unknown_prev_fallback_ -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib should_share_unknown_prev_hash_tracks_peer_and_height -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p iroha_core --lib`. Remaining open: re-run the 3600s soak to confirm whether late-stage straggler proposal starvation around heights `~1220-1230` is reduced and whether min-height skew closes.

Latest sync (Izanami 3600s soak + DA consensus backlog tuning, 2026-02-19): implemented three consensus-path mitigations in `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` + `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs`: (1) proposal-gap idle view-change now adds availability-timeout grace when DA backlog is active (`consensus_queue_backlog`), (2) near-quorum reschedule deferral now considers block/payload/consensus queue backlog (not only `rbc_chunk_rx`), and (3) near-quorum rebroadcast widens to all non-local topology peers when inferred missing-vote targets collapse to <=1 peer. Added regressions `force_view_change_if_idle_defers_proposal_gap_with_backlog_until_availability_timeout`, `reschedule_defers_near_commit_quorum_while_block_queue_backlogged`, and `quorum_reschedule_near_quorum_fans_out_payload_rebroadcast_targets` (plus rechecked `reschedule_defers_near_commit_quorum_while_rbc_chunks_arrive`), all passing under `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib ...`. Full 3600s rerun (`/tmp/izanami_soak_20260219T060658.log`, network dir `/var/folders/n2/xxntlr312qbfdnp0j1xp52hw0000gn/T/irohad_test_network_5Z9C0f`) finished at `min_height=1480` (`pace=24.711 blocks/min`, `shortfall=25.867%` vs target 33.333). Bottlenecks shifted but remain: `qc_payload_missing=47` (down), `commit_quorum_missing=1` (down), `no_proposal_cutoff=5` (up), `block_sync_locked_conflict=293` (up), with largest synchronized stall windows around heights `737->739` and `1173->1174` (~16–18s gaps). Remaining open: reduce lock-conflict churn and proposal-gap view-change bursts during late-window backlog convergence to close the remaining ~26% throughput gap.

Latest sync (tx validation perf, 2026-02-18): improved both stateless and stateful transaction validation hot paths in `crates/iroha_core/src/block.rs`. Stateless cache reuse now covers all static-validation entry paths (`validate`, `validate_with_events`, `validate_keep_voting_block_inner`, `validate_keep_voting_block_with_events`): static validation reads/writes `State::stateless_validation_cache`, passes cache-hit snapshots into `validate_static_with_snapshot`, and skips cached transactions in static scheme preverification/per-tx checks. Stateful block validation precomputes `routing_decisions` once and reuses them across fraud-policy stateless checks, telemetry, and apply flow. Added cache warmup coverage for non-voting + voting variants (`validate_populates_stateless_cache`, `validate_with_events_populates_stateless_cache`, `validate_keep_voting_block_populates_stateless_cache`, `validate_keep_voting_block_with_events_populates_stateless_cache`). Validation passed: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_txval_perf cargo test -p iroha_core --lib populates_stateless_cache -- --nocapture` (4 passed), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_txval_perf cargo test -p iroha_core --lib validate_and_record_transactions_skip_stateless_matches_full -- --nocapture`, `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_txval_perf cargo test -p iroha_core --lib validate_static_snapshot_rejects_invalid_signature -- --nocapture`. Benchmark comparison (`cargo bench -p iroha_core --bench validation -- validate --noplot --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 3`): previous run `11.104 µs .. 11.142 µs`, current run `11.291 µs .. 11.393 µs` (Criterion: `+1.53% .. +2.57%`, regression).
